вқҢ (A) hand in glove вҖ” 'Hand in glove' = closely associated, in collaboration. 'Easy' meaning аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'They work hand in glove' = closely together work аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'easy' context-аөҪ 'hand' related idiom confuse аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) apple of their eye вҖ” 'Apple of one's eye' = most precious/beloved person or thing. 'Easy' meaning аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'The daughter is the apple of her father's eye' = аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙөаөҫ.

вқҢ (C) cut corners вҖ” 'Cut corners' = do something hastily/cheaply, sacrifice quality. 'Very easy' meaning аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” shortcut/quality compromise meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Easy' вү  'cutting corners'.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'PIECE OF CAKE = SWEET + EASY!' вҖ” Cake eat аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҪ easy аҙүаҙӮ sweet аҙүаҙӮ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! Easy task = piece of cake! Imagine eating a delicious slice of cake вҖ” effortless! That's 'a piece of cake'!
вҖў Context clue: 'because it was very easy' = direct hint! 'Very easy' вҶ’ 'piece of cake'! Context аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ idiom answer аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ reveal аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Idiom blank question-аөҪ sentence-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ 'because/since/as' clause аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ read аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ вҖ” reason = clue to idiom. 'Because very easy' вҶ’ piece of cake. Guaranteed!

questionText:
Substitute the underlined phrase with the correct word.
Hitler believed himself to be __all powerful__.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Omnipotent

вҖў 'All powerful' = having unlimited/supreme power (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙ¶аҙ•аөҚаҙӨаө»).
вҖў 'Omnipotent' = omni (all) + potent (powerful) = all powerful.
вҖў 'Hitler believed himself to be omnipotent' = Hitler himself-аҙЁаөҶ all-powerful аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ believe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҒ.
вҖў 'Omni-' prefix = all/every (Latin) вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ: omni = all!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Omnipotent = Omni (all) + Potent (powerful) = ALL POWERFUL
вҖў вӯҗ 'Omni-' prefix words (PSC guaranteed):
  - Omnipotent = all powerful (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙ¶аҙ•аөҚаҙӨаө»)
  - Omniscient = all knowing (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙңаөҚаҙһаө»)
  - Omnipresent = present everywhere (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙӘаҙҝ)
  - Omnivore = eats everything/all food types (аҙёаҙёаөҚаҙҜаҙӯаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙЈаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӮаҙёаҙӯаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙЈаҙөаөҒаҙӮ)
вҖў Latin roots:
  - omni = all
  - potent = powerful (potential, potency)
  - scient = knowing (science, conscience)
  - present = present/being there
  - vore = eater (herbivore, carnivore)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Omni- prefix words вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. God is omnipotent. (all powerful вҖ” аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙ¶аҙ•аөҚаҙӨаө»)
  2. God is omniscient. (all knowing вҖ” аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙңаөҚаҙһаө»)
  3. God is omnipresent. (everywhere вҖ” аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙӘаҙҝ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Omniscient вҖ” 'Omni + scient' = ALL KNOWING (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙңаөҚаҙһаө»). 'All powerful' вү  'all knowing' вҖ” different qualities. Hitler 'knew everything' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ claim аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙүаҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ, 'all powerful' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: All 'omni-' words similar look аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ, students confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) Omnipresent вҖ” 'Omni + present' = PRESENT EVERYWHERE (аҙёаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙӘаҙҝ). 'All powerful' = omnipotent аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, 'everywhere present' = omnipresent аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Different divine qualities.

вқҢ (D) Omnivore вҖ” 'Omni + vore' = EATS EVERYTHING. Bears, pigs are omnivores. 'All powerful' meaning аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Omnivore = biological eating habit indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ, power indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'OMNI- = ALL. POTENT = POWER (potential/potency). OMNI + POTENT = ALL POWER = OMNIPOTENT!' вҖ” 'Potential' вҶ’ 'potent' вҶ’ 'omnipotent'. Power words аҙ’аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ 'pot-' root аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙө!
вҖў Omni- family quick memory:
  POTENT (power) вҶ’ omnipOTENT
  SCIENCE (know) вҶ’ omniSCIENT
  PRESENT (here) вҶ’ omniPRESENT
  VORE (eat) вҶ’ omniVORE
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'All powerful' вҶ’ omni + power = omnipOTENT. Root 'potent' = power (remember: impotent = powerless). !

questionText:
Use an appropriate article.
After a long enquiry, they bought ________ ewe from Chennai.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) a

вҖў 'Ewe' = female sheep (аҙӘаөҶаөә аҙҶаҙҹаөҚ). Pronunciation: /juЛҗ/ вҖ” аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ sound 'y' (consonant sound) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, vowel sound аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ!
вҖў 'Ewe' letter 'e' аөҪ start аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ pronunciation /juЛҗ/ = 'you' аҙӘаөӢаҙІаөҶ вҖ” consonant sound 'j/y' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў Consonant sound-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ®аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаөҪ вҶ’ 'a' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў 'A ewe' вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙӘаөҶаөә аҙҶаҙҹаөҚ вҖ” 'a' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ correct article.
вҖў Classic PSC trap: 'ewe' vowel letter-аөҪ start аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ sound consonant аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Letter аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ вҖ” SOUND аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ! Vowel SOUND вҶ’ 'an' | Consonant SOUND вҶ’ 'a'
вҖў Vowel letter but CONSONANT sound вҶ’ 'a' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ:
  - A ewe (/juЛҗ/ = y sound вҶ’ a)
  - A university (/juЛҗ/ = y sound вҶ’ a)
  - A European (/jКҠЙҷr/ = y sound вҶ’ a)
  - A one-eyed man (/w/ = w sound вҶ’ a)
  - A useful tool (/juЛҗ/ = y sound вҶ’ a)
вҖў Consonant letter but VOWEL sound вҶ’ 'an' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ:
  - An hour (h silent вҶ’ an)
  - An honest man (h silent вҶ’ an)
  - An heir (h silent вҶ’ an)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Articles 'a' vs 'an' вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. A ewe was grazing in the field. (/juЛҗ/ вҶ’ a)
  2. A university degree helps in career. (/juЛҗ/ вҶ’ a)
  3. An hour has sixty minutes. (h silent вҶ’ an)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) an вҖ” 'An' vowel sound-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ®аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'Ewe' letter 'e' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ sound /juЛҗ/ (y sound) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Vowel letter вү  vowel sound! 'An ewe' = wrong pronunciation. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: 'e' letter vowel аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ 'an' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) the вҖ” 'The' definite article specific thing indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'The ewe' = previously mentioned specific ewe. аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'a' (indefinite, first time mention) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ.

вқҢ (D) No article вҖ” 'Ewe' countable singular noun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Singular countable noun-аҙЁаөҚ indefinite article (a/an) аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ. Article аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ singular countable noun аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'EWE = YOU!' вҖ” 'Ewe' /juЛҗ/ sounds exactly like 'you'! 'A you' (a ewe) вҖ” 'you' consonant sound 'y' вҶ’ 'a'! Sheep = 'you' sounds like! A ewe!
вҖў Tricky 'a' words (vowel letter, consonant sound): ewe, university, European, union, uniform, unit, unique, eulogy, euphoria вҖ” аҙҮаҙө аҙ’аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ 'a' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Word аҙүаҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ pronounce аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ. 'Ewe' = 'yoo' = y sound = consonant = 'a'. Letter trap-аөҪ аҙөаөҖаҙҙаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable preposition.
The proposal had significant drawbacks so she was angry ________ my decision to accept it.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) at

вҖў 'Angry at' = fixed adjective + preposition collocation.
вҖў 'She was angry at my decision' = аҙҺаө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ decision-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚ аҙ…аҙөаөҫ аҙҰаөҮаҙ·аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Angry at' вҖ” a decision/situation/thing-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі anger indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ 'at' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Angry with' = person-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі anger. 'Angry at' = situation/thing/action-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі anger.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Angry AT (thing/situation/action) | Angry WITH (person)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite adjective + preposition collocations:
  - Angry at (thing) / angry with (person)
  - Pleased with (person/thing)
  - Satisfied with
  - Disappointed with/at
  - Surprised at/by
  - Annoyed at/with
  - Excited about
  - Worried about
  - Jealous of
  - Proud of
  - Capable of
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Adjective + Preposition вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She was angry at the delay. (delay = thing вҶ’ at)
  2. He was angry with his friend. (friend = person вҶ’ with)
  3. They were angry at the decision. (decision = thing вҶ’ at)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) on вҖ” 'Angry on' = not a standard English collocation. 'On' time/surface/topic indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'Angry on my decision' = wrong preposition. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Malayalam-аөҪ 'decision-аөҪ аҙҰаөҮаҙ·аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ' вҶ’ 'on' translate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) in вҖ” 'Angry in' = not a collocation for 'angry'. 'In' place/time/state indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'Angry in my decision' = unnatural.

вқҢ (C) by вҖ” 'Angry by' = also not standard. 'By' agent/means indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ (passive voice). 'Angry by' fixed phrase аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ English-аөҪ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ANGRY AT = AIM AT the target!' вҖ” Anger AT a specific target (thing/decision). 'At' = pointing towards something specific. Decision = specific target вҶ’ angry AT!
вҖў Angry AT/WITH distinction: 'AT' thing, 'WITH' person. 'I am angry AT the rule / angry WITH the rulemaker.'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Adjective + preposition question-аөҪ fixed collocation аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. 'Angry at' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ standard pair аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. options-аөҪ 'at' аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ anger context-аөҪ = first check аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!

questionText: Which of the following is correctly spelt?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Lapidary

вҖў 'Lapidary' = correct spelling: L-A-P-I-D-A-R-Y.
вҖў 'Lapidary' = relating to the cutting, polishing, or engraving of gems and precious stones; also a person who cuts gems.
вҖў аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙҜ spelling: Lap + i + dary = Lapidary.
вҖў Etymology: Latin 'lapidarius' (lapis = stone) вҶ’ English 'lapidary'.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: L-A-P-I-D-A-R-Y (8 letters вҖ” LAP + I + DARY)
вҖў 'Lapidary' meaning:
  - Adjective: relating to precious stones or their engraving
  - Noun: a person who cuts, polishes, or engraves gems
  - Also: precise and elegant (lapidary prose = beautifully concise writing)
вҖў Etymology: Latin 'lapis' = stone вҶ’ lapidary (stone cutter/polisher)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Advanced vocabulary spelling вҖ” Main examination-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The lapidary carefully cut the diamond. (gem cutter)
  2. He wrote in a lapidary style. (precise and elegant)
  3. Lapidary work requires great skill and patience.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Laepidary вҖ” 'ae' combination wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Lapidary' = 'a' аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҮ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаөӮ аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ syllable-аөҪ. 'Lae-' prefix wrong. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Similar looking spelling confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) Lapeadary вҖ” 'ea' аҙүаҙӮ extra syllable аҙүаҙӮ wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Lapidary' = 3 syllables (Lap-i-da-ry), 'Lapeadary' = extra 'ea' inserted, incorrect.

вқҢ (D) None of these вҖ” Option C 'Lapidary' аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙҜ spelling аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'LAPIDARY = LAP + I + DARY' вҖ” Lap-аөҪ (lap) аҙҮаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ precious stones-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ work аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙҶаөҫ! LAP + I + DARY. Or: LAPIS (stone in Latin) вҶ’ LAPIDARY!
вҖў Word root: LAPIS = stone (Latin). Lapidary = stone person. Remember: LAPIS LAZULI (blue precious stone) вҶ’ lapis = stone!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Advanced spelling-аөҪ Latin root аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. Lapis (stone) вҶ’ lapidary. Root аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ spelling automatically correct аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ!

questionText: Find one word for 'A public place for walking'.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Promenade

вҖў 'Promenade' = a public place/path designed for leisurely walking, especially along the seafront or in a park.
вҖў 'A public place for walking' = promenade вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ exact definition.
вҖў 'Promenade' = аҙңаҙЁаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙөаҙҝаҙЁаөӢаҙҰ аҙёаҙһаөҚаҙҡаҙҫаҙ°аҙӮ/morning walk аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ public pathway.
вҖў Famous promenades: Marine Drive (Mumbai), Marina Beach walkway (Chennai) вҖ” аҙҮаҙө promenades аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Public walking place = Promenade
вҖў вӯҗ One Word Substitutes вҖ” Places (PSC favourites):
  - Promenade = public place for walking
  - Alley = narrow passage between buildings
  - Byway = small road/path away from main road
  - Maze = complex network of paths (labyrinth)
  - Arena = place for sports/events
  - Amphitheatre = open-air theatre
  - Sanctuary = place of refuge/safety
  - Mortuary = place where dead bodies are kept
  - Aviary = place for keeping birds
  - Apiary = place where bees are kept
вҖў Etymology: French 'promener' = to take for a walk вҶ’ promenade
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: One word substitutes (places) вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. They strolled along the promenade by the sea. (seafront walking path)
  2. The dark alley was dangerous at night. (narrow passage)
  3. They got lost in the maze. (complex paths)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Byway вҖ” 'Byway' = a minor road or path away from the main roads, often in rural areas. PUBLIC walking-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ specific meaning аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, general small path аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Public place' specifically indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Byway' = walking related, students confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) Alley вҖ” 'Alley' = a narrow passage/lane between buildings. Public walking-аҙЁаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ designed аҙҶаҙҜ place аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” buildings-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі narrow path аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Often dark/private.

вқҢ (D) Maze вҖ” 'Maze' = a complex network of paths where it's easy to get lost (labyrinth). Public walking place аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” puzzle/entertainment purpose аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'PROMENADE = PRO + MENADE (MADE for walking)!' вҖ” PRO = forward, MENADE = made/walking path. 'PROM' аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ вҖ” school prom-аөҪ аҙҶаҙіаөҒаҙ•аөҫ dress up аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ walk аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ! Promenade = formal walk!
вҖў Places one-word substitutes: Promenade (public walk), Alley (narrow lane), Maze (labyrinth), Arena (sports), Aviary (birds), Apiary (bees), Mortuary (dead bodies) вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ list аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Public place for walking' = promenade. Public + walk = promenade. Definition directly map аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!

questionText:
Report the given sentence.
She said to him, "Stay here until your turn comes."
optionA: She requested him to stay there until her turn came.
optionB: She requested him that he should stay there until his turn came.
optionC: She requested him to stay there until his turn came.
optionD: She told him to stay there until his turn comes.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) She requested him to stay there until his turn came.

вҖў Direct speech: 'Stay here until your turn comes' вҖ” imperative sentence (polite request/instruction).
вҖў Reported speech changes:
  - 'Stay' (imperative) вҶ’ 'to stay' (infinitive in reported)
  - 'here' вҶ’ 'there' (place change)
  - 'your turn' вҶ’ 'his turn' (pronoun change: 'you' вҶ’ 'he')
  - 'comes' вҶ’ 'came' (tense backshift: present вҶ’ past)
  - 'said to him' + imperative вҶ’ 'requested/told him to'
вҖў Option C: all changes correct вҖ” 'to stay there until his turn came'. вң“
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Imperative reported speech: said to + [person] вҶ’ told/requested + [person] + to + V1
вҖў Imperative Reported Speech rules:
  - Positive imperative: 'Do X' вҶ’ told/requested/asked + person + to + X
  - Negative imperative: 'Don't do X' вҶ’ told/asked + person + not to + X
вҖў Changes in reported speech:
  - here вҶ’ there
  - this вҶ’ that
  - come вҶ’ go
  - your вҶ’ his/her (context based)
  - Verb tense backshift: comes вҶ’ came
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Reported speech вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She said, 'Come here.' вҶ’ She told him to go there.
  2. He said, 'Wait outside.' вҶ’ He told her to wait outside.
  3. Teacher said, 'Be quiet.' вҶ’ The teacher told students to be quiet.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) until her turn came вҖ” 'Her turn' = wrong pronoun. 'She' said to 'him' вҶ’ 'him' (male) = 'his turn', not 'her turn'. Pronoun error. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'She' subject аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, students 'her' pronoun use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) that he should stay there вҖ” 'That he should stay' = awkward reported imperative. Standard form: 'requested him TO STAY' (infinitive), not 'that he should'. This structure is grammatically awkward for imperative reporting.

вқҢ (D) until his turn comes вҖ” 'Comes' = tense backshift аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ! Reported speech-аөҪ 'said' (past) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ reporting verb вҶ’ 'comes' вҶ’ 'came' аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ. Present tense вҶ’ past tense backshift missed.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: '4 CHANGES in reported speech: (1) Verb form (to+V1), (2) HEREвҶ’THERE, (3) YOURвҶ’HIS/HER, (4) Tense backshift (comesвҶ’came)' вҖ” аҙҲ 4 changes аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ options verify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!
вҖў Option elimination: A = wrong pronoun (her). B = awkward structure. D = tense not backshifted. C = all correct!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Reported speech options-аөҪ аҙ“аҙ°аөӢ change аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҠаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ check аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ: pronoun вң“, place word вң“, tense вң“, structure вң“ вҶ’ correct answer!

questionText: Which of the following words is the antonym of the word 'defiance'?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Submission

вҖў 'Defiance' = bold resistance, open disobedience, refusal to obey (аҙ…аҙЁаөҒаҙёаҙ°аҙЈаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙҹаөҚ, аҙ§аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӮ).
вҖў 'Submission' = the act of accepting/yielding to authority, surrender (аҙ•аөҖаҙҙаҙҹаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҪ, аҙ…аҙЁаөҒаҙёаҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ).
вҖў Defiance (resist/defy) вҶ” Submission (accept/yield) вҖ” exact opposites!
вҖў 'In defiance of orders' vs 'in submission to orders' вҖ” complete contrast.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Defiance (resist) вҶ” Submission (yield/accept)
вҖў Word family:
  - Defy (verb) вҶ’ Defiance (noun) вҶ’ Defiant (adjective) вҶ’ Defiantly (adverb)
  - Submit (verb) вҶ’ Submission (noun) вҶ’ Submissive (adjective)
вҖў Synonyms of 'defiance': contumacy, insolence, resistance, rebellion, disobedience
вҖў Antonyms of 'defiance': submission, compliance, obedience, surrender, yielding
вҖў вӯҗ Options A, C, D are all SYNONYMS of defiance!
  - Contumacy = stubborn resistance to authority (defiance synonym)
  - Insolence = disrespectful boldness (related to defiance)
  - Resistance = opposing/withstanding (defiance synonym)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Antonyms вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. His defiance of the rules surprised everyone. (rules аҙ§аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ)
  2. The soldiers' submission ended the war. (surrender)
  3. She stood in defiance against the injustice.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Contumacy вҖ” 'Contumacy' = stubborn refusal to obey authority вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ 'defiance'-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ SYNONYM аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: Options-аөҪ 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern вҖ” students synonym-аөҪ confuse аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) Insolence вҖ” 'Insolence' = rude/disrespectful behaviour = another SYNONYM of defiance. Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Insolent behaviour' = defiant/disrespectful.

вқҢ (D) Resistance вҖ” 'Resistance' = opposing/withstanding = synonym of defiance. Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Resistance to authority' = 'defiance of authority' вҖ” same meaning.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'DEFIANCE = DEFY + ANCE = REFUSE! SUBMISSION = SUBMIT + ION = ACCEPT!' вҖ” Defy = refuse вҶ’ Defiance. Submit = accept вҶ’ Submission. REFUSE вҶ” ACCEPT = exact opposites!
вҖў Antonym question strategy: 3 options (contumacy, insolence, resistance) вҖ” аҙҮаҙө аҙ’аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ 'resistance/disobedience' meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. аҙ’аҙ°аөҮ meaning аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі 3 options вҶ’ аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ remove, remaining (submission) = antonym!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: When 3 options have similar meaning вҶ’ they're all synonyms вҶ’ answer is the odd one out = antonym!

questionText:
Identify the meaning of the idiomatic expression.
'To take the wind out of one's sails' (~~deleted by psc~~)

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Dishearten

вҖў 'To take the wind out of someone's sails' = to make someone feel less confident or enthusiastic, to deflate their excitement (аҙҶаҙөаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ/аҙҶаҙӨаөҚаҙ®аҙөаҙҝаҙ¶аөҚаҙөаҙҫаҙёаҙӮ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•).
вҖў 'Dishearten' = to make someone lose hope or enthusiasm = exact meaning.
вҖў Origin: Sailing-аөҪ wind (wind) sail-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ take away аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ ship-аҙЁаөҚ speed аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаҙҫаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ вҖ” person-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ momentum/enthusiasm take away аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ image.
вҖў 'His criticism took the wind out of my sails' = his criticism-аөҪ enthusiasm аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Take the wind out of sails = Dishearten / Deflate enthusiasm
вҖў вӯҗ Sailing/nautical idioms:
  - Take the wind out of one's sails = dishearten, deflate
  - Plain sailing = easy progress (smooth sailing)
  - In the same boat = in the same difficult situation
  - Rock the boat = disturb a stable situation
  - Miss the boat = miss an opportunity
  - Sail through = complete easily without problems
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Idioms вҖ” PSC-аөҪ guaranteed topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Her negative comments took the wind out of his sails. (enthusiasm аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒ)
  2. The failure took the wind out of their sails. (аҙҶаҙӨаөҚаҙ®аҙөаҙҝаҙ¶аөҚаҙөаҙҫаҙёаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ)
  3. Don't let criticism take the wind out of your sails. (dishearten аҙҶаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Excite вҖ” 'Excite' = enthusiasm increase аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ• вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ OPPOSITE meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! Wind take away аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ ship slow аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ (excitement decreases). 'Excite' = wind add аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, take away аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Wind/sails = exciting image вҶ’ 'excite' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) Thrive вҖ” 'Thrive' = grow/prosper/flourish (аҙүаҙҜаөјаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҜаҙҹаҙҜаөҒаҙ•). аҙҮаҙӨаөҒаҙӮ positive meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Taking wind out = negative effect вҶ’ 'thrive' wrong.

вқҢ (C) Invigorate вҖ” 'Invigorate' = give energy/vitality to (аҙҠаөјаҙңаөҚаҙңаҙӮ аҙ•аөҠаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•). аҙҮаҙӨаөҒаҙӮ positive/energizing meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Wind take away = energy take away вҶ’ invigorate opposite direction.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'WIND = ENERGY. TAKE AWAY WIND = TAKE AWAY ENERGY = DISHEARTEN!' вҖ” Imagine a sailing ship: no wind = stopped, deflated, sad ship! That's how someone feels when wind is taken from their sails = disheartened!
вҖў Nautical idioms all options-аөҪ: 'take the wind OUT' = remove energy = dishearten. 'Give wind' = inspire. 'Take away' = always negative вҶ’ dishearten/deflate!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Idiom meaning-аөҪ positive/negative identify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ. 'TAKE/REMOVE something' = negative вҶ’ positive options (excite, thrive, invigorate) eliminate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ вҶ’ 'Dishearten' = answer!

рҹҡ« Deleted Question: PSC аҙ”аҙҰаөҚаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ• Final Answer Key-аөҪ аҙҲ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙ’аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝ (deleted/cancelled), аҙёаөҚаҙ•аөӢаҙұаҙҝаҙӮаҙ—аҙҝаөҪ аҙүаөҫаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. аҙ•аөғаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ аҙёаөҚаҙҘаҙҝаҙ°аөҖаҙ•аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙһаөҚаҙһаҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” 'Dishearten' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ аҙҲ idiom-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ standard, widely-accepted meaning аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ (аҙ®аҙұаөҚаҙұаөҚ options аҙӘаөӢаҙІаөҶ ambiguity аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ genuine alternative аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙ•аҙҫаҙЈаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ). аҙ…аҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ аҙҲ deletion, аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning-аҙІаөҶ ambiguity аҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙІаөҚаҙІ, аҙ®аҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ Malayalam-medium аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҮаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙұаҙҝаҙІаөҶ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ printing/translation аҙӘаҙҝаҙ¶аҙ•аөҚ аҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ…аҙЁаөҒаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.

questionText:
Identify the underlined word with correct parts of speech.
A long night of travelling left me with __bloodshot__ eyes.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Adjective

вҖў 'Bloodshot' = having the white part of the eye streaked with red (blood vessels visible due to tiredness).
вҖў 'Bloodshot eyes' вҖ” 'bloodshot' 'eyes' (noun)-аҙЁаөҶ modify/describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ вҶ’ Adjective.
вҖў Adjective = noun-аҙЁаөҶ describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ word. 'Bloodshot' eyes describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'A long night of travelling left me with bloodshot [eyes]' вҖ” bloodshot = tired, red-streaked eyes describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ adjective.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Word that describes/modifies a noun вҶ’ ADJECTIVE
вҖў Adjective identification test: Can you say 'very ___' or '___ noun'? If yes вҶ’ adjective.
  - 'Very bloodshot' = possible вҶ’ adjective
  - 'Bloodshot eyes' (adj + noun) вҶ’ adjective
вҖў Compound adjectives (noun + past participle): bloodshot, heartbroken, sun-dried, hand-made, well-known, short-sighted
вҖў 'Bloodshot' = blood + shot (blood-streaked) = compound adjective
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Parts of speech identification вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She had bloodshot eyes after crying. (bloodshot = adjective modifying eyes)
  2. He is a well-known actor. (well-known = adjective)
  3. She wore a hand-made dress. (hand-made = adjective)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Adverb вҖ” Adverb verb/adjective/other adverb-аҙЁаөҶ modify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'Bloodshot' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ noun (eyes)-аҙЁаөҶ modify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. Noun modify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ = adjective, verb modify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ = adverb. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: '-shot' ending verb-related feel аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ.

вқҢ (C) Noun вҖ” 'Bloodshot' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ person/place/thing/idea indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Eyes' (noun)-аҙЁаөҶ describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ quality word аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ adjective, noun аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Verb вҖ” 'Bloodshot' action indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Left' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҲ sentence-аөҪ main verb. 'Bloodshot' eyes describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ adjective аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'BLOODSHOT DESCRIBES EYES = ADJECTIVE!' вҖ” Bloodshot = describing word for eyes. Any word describing a noun = adjective. Simple test: 'What kind of eyes?' вҶ’ bloodshot eyes вҶ’ bloodshot = adjective!
вҖў Compound adjective pattern: Noun + past participle = adjective. Blood + shot = bloodshot (describing condition of eyes). Similar: heart + broken = heartbroken, sun + dried = sun-dried.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Underlined word + noun after it? вҶ’ check if it describes the noun. Describes noun = adjective. 'Bloodshot EYES' вҶ’ bloodshot describes eyes вҶ’ adjective!

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag.
Few people seem to understand these new regulations, __________?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) do they

вҖў 'Few people seem to understand' вҖ” 'few' = negative meaning (almost nobody).
вҖў Negative meaning statement вҶ’ Positive question tag аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ.
вҖў Subject: 'Few people' вҶ’ pronoun 'they' (third person plural).
вҖў Tense: Simple Present ('seem') вҶ’ auxiliary 'do'.
вҖў Positive tag: 'do they?' (plural present positive)
вҖў 'Few people seem to understand these regulations, do they?' = аҙҲ regulations аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ аҙҶаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ аҙ®аҙЁаҙёаөҚаҙёаҙҝаҙІаҙҫаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ, аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ?
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: 'Few' = negative meaning вҶ’ Positive tag
вҖў 'Few' vs 'A few':
  - Few = negative (almost none, not enough) вҶ’ positive tag
  - A few = positive (some, enough) вҶ’ negative tag
вҖў Negative quantifiers вҶ’ positive tag:
  - Few people вҶ’ do they?
  - Little money вҶ’ does it?
  - Hardly anyone вҶ’ do they?
  - Never/seldom/rarely вҶ’ do/does/did + subject?
вҖў Subject-pronoun matching:
  - Few people вҶ’ they (third person plural)
  - NOT 'we' (first person)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Question tags with 'few' and negative quantifiers вҖ” PSC favourite!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Few students passed, did they? (few вҶ’ positive tag вҶ’ did)
  2. A few students passed, didn't they? (a few вҶ’ negative tag вҶ’ didn't)
  3. Few people know this, do they? (few вҶ’ do they?)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) do we вҖ” 'We' = first person plural. Subject 'few people' = third person plural вҶ’ 'they'. 'We' wrong pronoun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'These new regulations' general context-аөҪ 'we' feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) don't we вҖ” Two errors: (1) 'we' wrong pronoun (should be 'they'). (2) 'don't' negative tag аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, 'few' negative meaning вҶ’ positive tag аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ.

вқҢ (D) don't they вҖ” Pronoun correct (they) вң“, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'don't' negative tag аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Few' = negative meaning вҶ’ positive tag 'do they?' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ. Negative + Negative = double negative = wrong.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'FEW = NOT MANY = NEGATIVE вҶ’ POSITIVE TAG!' вҖ” FEW = negative idea (not many people understand). Negative idea = positive tag (do they?). Remember: FEW already said 'NO', so tag says 'YES (do they)?'
вҖў Few vs A few trick: 'A FEW = positive (some). FEW alone = negative (almost none). FEW вҶ’ do/does. A FEW вҶ’ don't/doesn't.'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Few people' вҶ’ they (pronoun, not we). 'Few' negative вҶ’ positive tag (do). 'do they?' = C = correct!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable phrasal verb.
Everyone expected him to ________ within a week and start succeeding.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) come around

вҖў 'Come around' = (1) recover from illness or a setback, regain consciousness; (2) change one's opinion; (3) occur again (cyclically).
вҖў Context: 'within a week and start succeeding' = setback-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ recover аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ succeed аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Everyone expected him to come around within a week' = аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаҙҙаөҚаҙҡаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙҝаөҪ recover аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ succeed аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҺаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙ°аөҒаҙӮ expect аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҒ.
вҖў 'Come around' = recover/bounce back = this context-аөҪ perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Come around = recover from setback / change mind / regain consciousness
вҖў 'Come' phrasal verbs вҖ” PSC favourites:
  - Come around/round = recover, change opinion (recover аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•)
  - Come by = obtain/acquire (something rare) (аҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•)
  - Come about = happen, occur (аҙёаҙӮаҙӯаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•)
  - Come up = arise, be mentioned (аҙүаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙ•)
  - Come across = find/meet unexpectedly (аҙҶаҙ•аҙёаөҚаҙ®аҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•)
  - Come forward = present oneself voluntarily (аҙ®аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙ•)
  - Come through = survive, succeed despite difficulties (survive аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Phrasal verbs вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She came around after the surgery. (recover from anaesthesia)
  2. Good jobs are hard to come by. (rare to obtain)
  3. How did this situation come about? (happen/occur)
  4. A problem came up at work. (arose)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) come by вҖ” 'Come by' = obtain or acquire something (especially something rare). 'Come by success' = obtain success вҖ” possible meaning, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'within a week and start succeeding' = recovery context аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, acquisition context аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Come around' more naturally fits 'bounce back/recover'. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'come by success' feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) come about вҖ” 'Come about' = happen/occur. 'He came about within a week' = he happened/occurred within a week вҖ” doesn't make sense with a person subject in this recovery context.

вқҢ (D) come up вҖ” 'Come up' = arise, be mentioned, (sun) rise. 'He came up within a week' = slightly different вҖ” 'come up' more for issues/sun rising, not personal recovery/bounce-back context.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'COME AROUND = CIRCLE BACK TO NORMAL!' вҖ” Imagine a path going around in a circle вҖ” 'come around' = complete the circle = return to normal state = RECOVER! Setback вҶ’ come around вҶ’ back to normal!
вҖў Context clue: 'within a week and START SUCCEEDING' = recovery/bounce-back context вҶ’ 'come around' (recover) is the natural fit!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-аөҪ context (what happens before/after blank) read аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ. 'Start succeeding after setback' = recovery = come around. Context аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ key!

questionText: John, as well as his brother ________ in honesty and hard work.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Believes

вҖў 'John, as well as his brother' вҖ” аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ main subject 'John' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ (singular).
вҖў 'As well as', 'along with', 'together with', 'in addition to' вҖ” аҙҮаҙө two subjects-аҙЁаөҶ join аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ verb аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ subject-аҙЁаөҶ follow аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'John' = singular вҶ’ verb-аөҪ 's' аҙҡаөҮаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ вҶ’ 'Believes'.
вҖў 'John believes in honesty' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ core sentence. 'as well as his brother' extra information аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў 'аҙңаөӢаөә, аҙ…аҙөаө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙёаҙ№аөӢаҙҰаҙ°аҙЁаөҒаҙӮ, аҙҶаҙӨаөҚаҙ®аҙҫаөјаҙҘаҙӨаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аҙ аҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаҙ§аөҚаҙөаҙҫаҙЁаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаҙҝаҙ¶аөҚаҙөаҙёаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ' вҖ” Believes аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ correct.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Subject1 + [as well as / along with / together with] + Subject2 вҶ’ verb agrees with Subject1 ONLY!
вҖў аҙҲ phrases verb agreement change аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ:
  - As well as
  - Along with
  - Together with
  - In addition to
  - Besides
  - Accompanied by
  - Rather than
вҖў аҙҮаҙө bracketed addition аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” remove аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒаҙӮ sentence correct аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў 'And' = different! John AND his brother = plural вҶ’ believe (no 's').
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Subject-verb agreement вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The teacher, as well as students, was present. (teacher = singular вҶ’ was)
  2. Raju, along with his friends, plays cricket. (Raju = singular вҶ’ plays)
  3. The manager, together with staff, attends meetings. (manager = singular вҶ’ attends)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Believe вҖ” 'Believe' = plural verb form (no 's'). 'As well as his brother' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ students 'brother' аҙүаҙӮ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ plural choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: 'as well as' = 'and' like feel вҶ’ plural verb. But 'as well as' вү  'and'!

вқҢ (C) Believed вҖ” 'Believed' = past tense. Question аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ general truth/habit state аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ (present tense аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ). Past tense context аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ sentence-аөҪ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Believing вҖ” 'Believing' = present participle. Main verb аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ wrong. Auxiliary verb ('is believing') аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ participle alone verb аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'AS WELL AS = BRACKET аҙҮаҙҹаөӮ!' вҖ” 'as well as his brother' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ bracket-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаөӮ: 'John [as well as his brother] believes.' Bracket remove аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ 'John believes' = correct! Bracket inside аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаөҚ verb-аҙЁаөҶ affect аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!
вҖў AND vs AS WELL AS: 'John AND his brother believe' (plural). 'John AS WELL AS his brother believes' (singular). This difference is a guaranteed PSC question!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Subject аҙҮаҙҹаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒ аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. 'As well as / along with' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ вҶ’ first subject-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ verb вҶ’ bracket trick use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!

questionText: Which of the following sentences contains a gerund?
optionB: Kiran is standing outside the waiting room.
optionD: Screaming aloud, he ran out of the room.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Swimming is a good exercise.

вҖў 'Swimming' вҖ” аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ sentence-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ SUBJECT аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Swimming is good' = 'аҙЁаөҖаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөҪ аҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ'.
вҖў Verb-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ -ing form аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ NOUN аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ act аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ = GERUND.
вҖў 'Swimming' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'what is good?' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ answer = noun function = gerund.
вҖў Test: Subject/Object аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? вҶ’ Gerund. Verb-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ part аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? вҶ’ Participle.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: -ing form = NOUN function вҶ’ GERUND | -ing form = VERB part вҶ’ PARTICIPLE
вҖў Gerund аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ situations:
  - Subject аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ: Swimming is fun. (gerund)
  - Object аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ: I enjoy swimming. (gerund)
  - Preposition-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ: He is good at swimming. (gerund)
вҖў Participle аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ situations:
  - Continuous tense-аөҪ: He is swimming. (participle вҖ” auxiliary 'is' аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ)
  - Adjective аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ: a swimming pool (adjective)
  - Participial phrase: Swimming fast, he won. (participle phrase)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Gerund vs Participle вҖ” PSC Main exam-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Reading is my hobby. (Reading = gerund, subject)
  2. She is reading a book. (reading = participle, part of continuous tense)
  3. He is tired of waiting. (waiting = gerund after preposition 'of')
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) He is smoking a cigarette вҖ” 'Smoking' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ Present Continuous tense-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ part аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ (is + smoking). Auxiliary verb 'is' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ PARTICIPLE аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, gerund аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'smoking' -ing form аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, students gerund choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) Kiran is standing outside the waiting room вҖ” 'Standing' = present continuous participle. 'Waiting' = 'waiting room' = adjective (participle acting as adjective). аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ gerund аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Screaming aloud, he ran out of the room вҖ” 'Screaming' = participial phrase (Screaming aloud = while screaming). аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ 'he' (subject)-аҙЁаөҶ modify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ participle аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, gerund аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'GERUND = GERMS (noun) вҖ” аҙ—аөӮаҙўаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ noun аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ verb!' вҖ” Gerund = verb disguised as noun. 'Swimming is fun' = 'Noun is fun' = gerund! Is аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ? (Continuous) вҶ’ Participle. Is аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ subject/object аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? вҶ’ Gerund!
вҖў Quick test: '-ing word' substitute 'activity' аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝ аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ вҖ” 'Activity is a good exercise' вң“ = gerund! 'He is activity a cigarette' вң— = participle!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: -ing word-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ function аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. Sentence-аөҪ subject/object аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? = gerund. Verb-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ part аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ (is/was/were + -ing)? = participle.

questionText: Choose the correct sentence from the choices given below.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) He entered the room.

вҖў 'Enter' = transitive verb вҖ” directly object аҙҺаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ verb. Preposition аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'He entered the room' = 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙ®аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаөҪ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙөаөҮаҙ¶аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ correct.
вҖў 'Enter into' = wrong for physical space entry. 'Enter into' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ fixed phrase аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ abstract concepts-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҶ: 'enter into an agreement/discussion' = аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝ. Physical room-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҶ 'enter into' = wrong.
вҖў Rule: Physical space = 'enter + place' (no preposition). Abstract = 'enter into + noun'.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Enter + physical place (NO preposition) | Enter into + abstract concept (agreement/discussion/negotiation)
вҖў 'Enter' without preposition:
  - Enter the room вң“
  - Enter the building вң“
  - Enter the country вң“
вҖў 'Enter into' (abstract only):
  - Enter into an agreement вң“
  - Enter into a discussion вң“
  - Enter into a contract вң“
вҖў Other verbs that DON'T take prepositions (common PSC errors):
  - Discuss something (NOT discuss about)
  - Reach somewhere (NOT reach to/at)
  - Request something (NOT request for)
  - Order something (NOT order for)
  - Accompany someone (NOT accompany with)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Redundant prepositions вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. They entered the hall silently. (no preposition)
  2. The two countries entered into a peace treaty. (abstract вҶ’ into)
  3. Please discuss the matter calmly. (NOT discuss about)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) He entered into the room вҖ” 'Into' physical room-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҶ use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Enter into' = abstract nouns (agreement, contract). 'Into the room' = wrong. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: 'into' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ correct feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ вҖ” 'into the room' Malayalam-аөҪ natural, English-аөҪ redundant.

вқҢ (C) He entered by the room вҖ” 'By' = means/agent/proximity indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ preposition. 'By the room' = room-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ…аҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ/аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӮ. 'Entered by the room' = unnatural, no standard meaning.

вқҢ (D) He entered in the room вҖ” 'In' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ wrong. 'He is in the room' (location, correct) vs 'He entered in the room' (entry action, wrong). Enter = action вҶ’ no preposition.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ENTER = ENTER! No extra baggage!' вҖ” 'Enter' is complete on its own for physical places. Adding 'into/in/by' = extra unnecessary baggage! 'He entered the room' = clean and correct!
вҖў Golden list вҖ” verbs that need NO preposition: Enter (a place), Discuss (a topic), Reach (a place), Request (something), Marry (someone), Resemble (someone). Preposition аҙҮаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ = error!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Enter' + physical place question-аөҪ preposition аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ option = answer. Simply 'entered the room' = always correct!

questionText: If he wore a lifebuoy, he ____________

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Would not drown

вҖў 'If he wore a lifebuoy' вҖ” if-clause-аөҪ past simple ('wore') аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў Past simple in if-clause = Second Conditional (present/future imaginary situation).
вҖў Second Conditional formula: If + past simple вҶ’ would + V1.
вҖў 'Wore' = past simple вҶ’ main clause = 'would not drown'.
вҖў 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ lifebuoy аҙ§аҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ, аҙ®аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҮаҙЁаөҮ' вҖ” present imaginary situation (he is probably not wearing one now).
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula:
  - Type 1: If + Present вҶ’ will + V1 (real future)
  - Type 2: If + Past Simple вҶ’ would + V1 (imaginary present/future)
  - Type 3: If + Past Perfect (had+V3) вҶ’ would have + V3 (past unreal)
вҖў Type 2 Signal: If-clause = past tense (wore, had, came, went)
вҖў 'Wore' = past simple of 'wear' вҶ’ Type 2 вҶ’ would + V1
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Conditionals вҖ” PSC-аөҪ guaranteed topic! Type 2 and Type 3 most frequently tested.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. If I had money, I would buy a car. (Type 2 вҖ” imaginary)
  2. If she studied harder, she would pass. (Type 2 вҖ” imaginary)
  3. If it rains, I will stay home. (Type 1 вҖ” real future)
  4. If he had studied, he would have passed. (Type 3 вҖ” past unreal)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Will not drown вҖ” 'Will' = Type 1 Conditional. Type 1 if-clause-аөҪ present tense аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ: 'If he wears a lifebuoy, he will not drown.' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'wore' (past) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ Type 2 вҶ’ 'will' wrong. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Students 'future' meaning вҶ’ 'will' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) may not drown вҖ” 'May' = possibility (maybe). Conditional sentences-аөҪ 'would' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ standard. 'May' uncertainty indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ, conditional result indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Would not have drowned вҖ” 'Would not have drowned' = Type 3 Conditional result. Type 3-аөҪ if-clause = past perfect (had + V3): 'If he had worn a lifebuoy, he would not have drowned.' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'wore' (past simple) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” past perfect аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҶ’ Type 3 result wrong.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: '1-1-1, 2-2-2, 3-3-3 pattern!' вҖ” Type 1: Present+Will. Type 2: Past+Would. Type 3: Had+Would Have. If-clause tense = type number Г— 1 past step!
вҖў Wore = past simple = Type 2 = Would. Had worn = past perfect = Type 3 = Would have. Wears = present = Type 1 = Will!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: If-clause-аҙІаөҶ verb tense identify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ вҶ’ wore (past) = Type 2 вҶ’ would + V1 = answer!

questionText:
Convert the following sentence into reported speech.
Sheela asked me: "do you believe in the essential goodness of humanity?"
optionA: Sheela asked me whether I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionB: Sheela asked me whether he believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionC: Sheela asked me that I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionD: Sheela asked me whether I believe in the essential goodness of humanity.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Sheela asked me whether I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.

вҖў Direct speech: 'do you believe...?' вҖ” Yes/No question (do/does/did/is/are/was/were аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі question).
вҖў Yes/No question reported speech вҶ’ 'whether' аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ 'if' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў 'you' (Sheela was asking 'me') вҶ’ 'I' (reporter = me, first person).
вҖў Tense backshift: 'believe' (present) вҶ’ 'believed' (past) вҖ” reporting verb 'asked' (past) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў Option A: whether вң“ + I (correct pronoun) вң“ + believed (tense backshift) вң“ = Correct!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula:
  - Yes/No question вҶ’ whether / if
  - Wh- question (what/where/when/who/why/how) вҶ’ same wh- word
вҖў Yes/No question reported changes:
  - Question mark removed
  - 'whether/if' added
  - Auxiliary verb (do/does/did) removed
  - Tense backshift
  - Pronouns adjusted
вҖў Tense backshift table:
  - Present simple вҶ’ Past simple (believe вҶ’ believed)
  - Present continuous вҶ’ Past continuous
  - Past simple вҶ’ Past perfect
  - Will вҶ’ Would
  - Can вҶ’ Could
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Reported speech (questions) вҖ” PSC Main exam-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He asked: 'Are you happy?' вҶ’ He asked whether I was happy.
  2. She asked: 'What do you want?' вҶ’ She asked what I wanted.
  3. He asked: 'Can you help?' вҶ’ He asked whether I could help.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) whether he believed вҖ” 'He' wrong pronoun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Sheela 'me'-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” 'you' = 'me' (reporter) = 'I'. 'He' = third person, аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙҶ context аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Sheela' = she, 'me' = he? Confused pronoun.

вқҢ (C) asked me that I believed вҖ” 'That' = statements-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ reported speech-аөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'She said that...' Yes/No question-аөҪ 'whether/if' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ. 'That' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ grammatically incorrect.

вқҢ (D) whether I believe вҖ” 'Believe' = tense backshift аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ! Reporting verb 'asked' = past tense. Past reporting verb вҶ’ present tense 'believe' вҶ’ past 'believed' аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ. Present tense retain аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ = error.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'YES/NO = WHETHER! WH = WH!' вҖ” Yes/No question reported = Whether/If. Wh- question reported = same wh- word. Simple!
вҖў 3 check points in reported speech: (1) Whether/if (Yes/No) вң“, (2) Pronoun (you вҶ’ I/he/she) вң“, (3) Tense backshift (believe вҶ’ believed) вң“ вҖ” check all 3 вҶ’ A is the only option with all 3 correct!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Options-аөҪ quickly scan: 'that' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ (C) = wrong for question. Tense not shifted (D) = wrong. Wrong pronoun (B) = wrong. Remaining = answer!

questionText:
Dora is a scientist who is interested in __the fossil history of life__.
Choose the suitable one-word expression for the parts given in italics.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Palaeontologist

вҖў 'The fossil history of life' = fossils, prehistoric organisms, ancient life-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ study.
вҖў 'Palaeontologist' = a scientist who studies fossils and prehistoric life (аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙҫаҙҡаөҖаҙЁ аҙңаөҖаҙөаҙңаҙҫаҙІаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙіаөҶ fossils аҙөаҙҙаҙҝ аҙӘаҙ аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙ¶аҙҫаҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙңаөҚаҙһаө»).
вҖў 'Palaeontology' = paleo (ancient) + ontos (being) + logos (study) = ancient life study.
вҖў Dinosaur bones, fossils, prehistoric plants вҖ” аҙҮаҙө study аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ = Palaeontologist.
вҖў Dora = fossil history-аөҪ interested = Palaeontologist.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Fossils + Prehistoric life = Palaeontologist
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite '-ologist' scientists:
  - Palaeontologist = fossil/prehistoric life (paleo = ancient)
  - Anthropologist = human societies and cultures (anthropo = human)
  - Archaeologist = human past through artifacts and excavation (archaio = ancient + human)
  - Gynaecologist = women's reproductive health (gyne = woman)
  - Ornithologist = birds (ornitho = bird)
  - Entomologist = insects (entomo = insect)
  - Geologist = earth and rocks (geo = earth)
  - Meteorologist = weather (meteoro = atmosphere)
  - Cardiologist = heart (cardio = heart)
  - Oncologist = cancer (onco = tumor)
вҖў Etymology: PALEO = ancient (Greek) вҶ’ Palaeontology, Palaeolithic (Stone Age)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: One word substitutes (scientist types) вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The palaeontologist discovered a dinosaur fossil. (fossils)
  2. The anthropologist studied tribal customs. (human society)
  3. The archaeologist excavated ancient ruins. (artifacts/ruins)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Anthropologist вҖ” 'Anthropologist' = studies human societies, cultures, and evolution (аҙ®аҙЁаөҒаҙ·аөҚаҙҜ аҙёаҙ®аөӮаҙ№аҙӮ). Fossil history аҙҮаҙӨаҙҝаө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ primary focus аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'ancient' вҶ’ 'anthropologist' аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ students.

вқҢ (B) Archaeologist вҖ” 'Archaeologist' = studies human past through physical artifacts, excavations (utensils, buildings, tools). 'Fossil history of life' вү  archaeology. Archaeology = human-made objects. Paleontology = natural fossils/prehistoric organisms.

вқҢ (C) Gynaecologist вҖ” 'Gynaecologist' = doctor specializing in women's reproductive health. Fossils/prehistoric life-аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. This option is clearly wrong but tests if students confuse '-ologist' words.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'PALEO = OLD/ANCIENT = FOSSILS!' вҖ” 'Paleo' diet = ancient diet. Paleolithic = ancient stone age. PALEOntologist = ancient + life = FOSSILS! 'Paleo' = old вҶ’ аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ fossil = palaeontologist guaranteed!
вҖў Key distinction: Archaeologist vs Palaeontologist вҖ” Archaeologist = human artifacts (pots, tools). Palaeontologist = natural fossils (dinosaurs, ancient plants). Both study old things but different types!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'fossil history of life' = paleo (ancient life). PALEO root аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ вҶ’ palaeontologist = guaranteed answer!

questionText: What is the meaning of the idiom, 'kick the bucket'?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) To die

вҖў 'Kick the bucket' = to die (informal/humorous expression for death).
вҖў 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ bucket kick аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ' = informal way of saying 'he died'.
вҖў Origin: аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ theory аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӮ вҖ” execution/suicide-аөҪ bucket kick аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ bucket remove аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ вҶ’ death. Another theory: 'bucket' = beam in French ('buquet') used for slaughtering вҖ” animals' legs kick when hung.
вҖў PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ idiom.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Kick the bucket = Die
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite idioms about life and death:
  - Kick the bucket = die
  - Bite the dust = die/fail
  - Meet one's maker = die
  - Pop one's clogs = die (British)
  - Breathe one's last = die
  - Burn the midnight oil = work very late
  - Bite the bullet = endure a difficult situation bravely
  - Beat around the bush = avoid the main point
  - Break the ice = start a conversation in awkward situation
  - Spill the beans = reveal a secret
  - Let the cat out of the bag = accidentally reveal a secret
  - Hit the nail on the head = say exactly the right thing
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Idioms вҖ” PSC-аөҪ guaranteed topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The old professor finally kicked the bucket last year. (died)
  2. He burned the midnight oil for his exams. (worked late)
  3. She spilled the beans about the surprise party. (revealed secret)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) To lose a game вҖ” 'Kick' = game, 'bucket' = score аҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ feeling вҖ” аҙҲ literal interpretation wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Idioms literal аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ interpret аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'kick' + 'game' = lose a game feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) To hit the target вҖ” 'Kick' + 'bucket' = aim at something feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ. 'Hit the nail on the head' = hit the target (correct idiom). 'Kick the bucket' вү  hit target.

вқҢ (D) To curse вҖ” 'Kick' = anger feel аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, students 'curse' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ. 'Kick the bucket' аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ meaning anger/curse-аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” informal death expression аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'BUCKET LIST = things to do BEFORE dying! KICK the bucket = DONE with bucket list = DIED!' вҖ” 'Bucket list' (before-death list) аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. Kick = finish/done. Kick the bucket = done with life = die!
вҖў PSC idiom golden list: kick the bucket (die), bite the dust (fail/die), burn midnight oil (work late), spill the beans (reveal secret), break the ice (start conversation) вҖ” аҙҲ 5 аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ guaranteed marks!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Kick the bucket' = classic PSC idiom, direct meaning: TO DIE. No second thought needed вҖ” B is the answer!

questionText:
There was a rumour about an __advancing__ army.
Choose the most appropriate word opposite in meaning of the word given in italics.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Retreating

вҖў 'Advancing army' = forward movement-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі (enemy-аҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚ approach аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ) army.
вҖў 'Retreating army' = backward movement-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі (enemy-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ withdraw аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ) army.
вҖў Military context-аөҪ 'advance' (аҙ®аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҚ) вҶ” 'retreat' (аҙӘаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҚ) = exact opposites.
вҖў 'Retreating' = exact military antonym of 'advancing' вҖ” army context-аөҪ perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Advancing (forward) вҶ” Retreating (backward/withdrawing) вҖ” military context
вҖў Word family of 'advance':
  - Advance (verb/noun) = move forward
  - Advanced (adjective) = ahead, progressive
  - Advancement (noun) = progress
  - Advancing (adjective/participle) = moving forward
вҖў Word family of 'retreat':
  - Retreat (verb/noun) = move back, withdraw
  - Retreating (participle) = withdrawing
вҖў Receding vs Retreating:
  - Receding = gradually moving back (receding tide, receding hairline) вҖ” natural/gradual
  - Retreating = military withdrawal or backing away from danger
  - In army context вҶ’ Retreating is the specific antonym
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Antonyms вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The advancing army crossed the border. (moving forward)
  2. The retreating army left the battlefield. (withdrawing)
  3. The tide is receding. (gradual natural backward movement)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) disadvancing вҖ” 'Disadvancing' = not a real English word! 'dis-' prefix 'advance'-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҶ use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'dis-' = opposite prefix вҶ’ students 'disadvancing' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ. Real word: retreat/recede.

вқҢ (B) Receding вҖ” 'Receding' = gradually moving away (natural process). 'Receding hairline', 'receding tide' вҖ” gradual, natural movement. Army context-аөҪ specific military withdrawal term 'retreating' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ more appropriate antonym.

вқҢ (D) Proceeding вҖ” 'Proceeding' = continuing forward, moving ahead. аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ 'advancing'-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ SYNONYM аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ! 'Proceeding' = going forward = advancing. Wrong direction.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ARMY ADVANCE = ATTACK! ARMY RETREAT = RUN BACK!' вҖ” Advancing army = аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙ®аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ army. Retreating army = аҙ“аҙҹаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ army. ADVANCE вҶ” RETREAT = military opposites!
вҖў Receding vs Retreating: Receding = tide/hairline (nature). Retreating = army/people (intentional withdrawal). Army context вҶ’ Retreating!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Disadvancing' = fake word вҶ’ eliminate. 'Proceeding' = synonym not antonym вҶ’ eliminate. 'Receding' vs 'Retreating' вҖ” army context вҶ’ Retreating wins!

questionText:
"Any way, we have to ________ this problem."
Choose the appropriate phrasal verb from the choices given below.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Sort out

вҖў 'Sort out' = to deal with/resolve/solve a problem (аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ problem resolve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•).
вҖў 'We have to sort out this problem' = 'аҙҲ problem-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ resolution аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ'.
вҖў 'Sort out' = organize confusion into order, resolve a difficulty.
вҖў Context: 'we have to _____ this problem' = problem solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙЈаҙӮ = sort out.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Sort out = Resolve/Solve (a problem, situation, confusion)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite 'out' phrasal verbs:
  - Sort out = resolve, organize (problem/situation solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•)
  - Find out = discover information (information аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•)
  - Seek out = search for deliberately (specifically search аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ find аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•)
  - Put out = extinguish (fire/light/cigarette), also: inconvenience someone
  - Work out = solve (a problem/calculation), also: exercise
  - Figure out = understand/solve
  - Rule out = eliminate as a possibility
  - Carry out = perform/execute a task
  - Point out = indicate/draw attention to
  - Stand out = be noticeable
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Phrasal verbs вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Let's sort out this misunderstanding. (resolve)
  2. Can you find out when the train arrives? (discover)
  3. They sought out the best doctor. (deliberately searched)
  4. Firefighters put out the fire. (extinguish)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Find out вҖ” 'Find out' = discover information ('find out the answer', 'find out who did it'). Problem-аҙЁаөҶ solve/resolve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ• аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” information discover аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•аҙҜаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. 'Find out this problem' = awkward/wrong meaning. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'find' + 'problem' = solution find feel.

вқҢ (C) Seek out вҖ” 'Seek out' = deliberately search for something/someone specific. 'Seek out this problem' = strange meaning. Problem аҙҮаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙ•аҙӮ identified аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙҮаҙЁаҙҝ search аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙЈаҙӮ = sort out.

вқҢ (D) Put out вҖ” 'Put out' primarily = extinguish (fire, candle, cigarette). Problem extinguish аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ• idiomatic аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'sort out' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ standard. 'Put out a problem' = unusual. Standard usage: 'put out a fire/light'.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'SORT = ORGANIZE = SOLVE! Sort out = ORGANIZE the problem OUT of your life!' вҖ” Sorting = putting things in order. Sort out a problem = organize/remove the problem = SOLVE IT!
вҖў Problem-solving phrasal verbs: Sort out, Work out, Figure out, Resolve вҖ” аҙҮаҙө аҙ’аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ problem solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ. Find out = discover. Seek out = search. Put out = extinguish.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'problem solve' context = 'sort out'. Problem + solve feeling = sort out, work out, figure out. Options-аөҪ sort out аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ = first choice!

questionText: What is the expansion of "e.g." meaning "for example"?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Exempli gratia

вҖў 'e.g.' = Latin 'exempli gratia' = 'for the sake of example' = 'for example'.
вҖў 'Exempli' = of an example (genitive form of 'exemplum' = example).
вҖў 'Gratia' = for the sake of, out of (Latin).
вҖў 'e.g.' = 'exempli gratia' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ exactly 'for example' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў Usage: 'I like fruits, e.g., apples and mangoes.' = for example, apples and mangoes.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: e.g. = exempli gratia = for example
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite Latin abbreviations:
  - e.g. = exempli gratia = for example (examples аҙ•аөҠаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ)
  - i.e. = id est = that is (clarification аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ)
  - etc. = et cetera = and so on (list continue аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ)
  - vs. = versus = against
  - cf. = confer = compare
  - NB = nota bene = note well (important point)
  - P.S. = post scriptum = after writing (letter-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ add аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ note)
  - A.M. = ante meridiem = before noon
  - P.M. = post meridiem = after noon
  - viz. = videlicet = namely (specific list)
вҖў e.g. vs i.e.: e.g. = examples (list-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ part). i.e. = exact meaning (complete explanation).
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Abbreviations/Latin phrases вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. I enjoy water sports, e.g., swimming and surfing. (for example)
  2. The CEO, i.e., the chief executive, will attend. (that is)
  3. We need food, water, shelter, etc. (and so on)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Exempli gratuise вҖ” 'Gratuise' = not a Latin word. Correct: 'gratia'. 'Gratuise' = fake/misspelled option. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Exempli' correct part аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ students confirm аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ, 'gratuise' wrong spelling miss аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (B) Example gratui вҖ” 'Example' = English word, Latin abbreviation-аөҪ English word аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Exempli' (Latin) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ correct, 'Example' (English) аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Gratui' = also incomplete/wrong Latin.

вқҢ (C) Exaea gratia вҖ” 'Exaea' = not a Latin word at all! 'Exempli' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ correct first word. 'Exaea' = completely wrong. 'Gratia' part correct аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ first word totally wrong.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'e.g. = EXAMPLE GIVEN! вҖ” e for example, g for given!' вҖ” Simple English: e.g. = Example Given. Real Latin: Exempli Gratia. Remember either way!
вҖў e.g. vs i.e. trick: 'e.g. = EGG (example, guess) вҖ” I give examples'. 'i.e. = I EXPLAIN' вҖ” complete clarification. 'I like citrus fruits, e.g., lemons.' (examples follow). 'He is vegetarian, i.e., he doesn't eat meat.' (exact meaning follows).
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Latin abbreviations аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ list аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ: e.g.=exempli gratia, i.e.=id est, etc.=et cetera, P.S.=post scriptum вҖ” аҙҲ 4 = guaranteed PSC marks!

questionText: A group of eagles is called:

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Convocation

вҖў Eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҶ group-аҙЁаөҶ 'Convocation' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Convocation' = a formal gathering/assembly вҖ” eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ ceremonial gathering-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ image.
вҖў Eagles soaring together in the sky = convocation of eagles.
вҖў Collective nouns for animals PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙӮ аҙ•аҙҙаөҒаҙ•аө» = A convocation of eagles' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Collective noun = group-аҙЁаөҶ аҙ’аҙұаөҚаҙұ word-аөҪ describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ noun
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite collective nouns for animals:
  - Eagles = Convocation
  - Owls = Parliament
  - Crows = Murder
  - Lions = Pride
  - Wolves = Pack
  - Fish = School / Shoal
  - Bees = Swarm
  - Ants = Colony
  - Geese = Gaggle (on ground) / Skein (in flight)
  - Elephants = Herd
  - Monkeys = Troop
  - Cats = Clowder
  - Dogs = Pack
  - Whales = Pod
  - Butterflies = Flutter
  - Kangaroos = Mob
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Collective nouns for animals вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. A convocation of eagles circled the mountain. (eagles)
  2. A pride of lions rested under the tree. (lions)
  3. A swarm of bees attacked the hive. (bees)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Parliament вҖ” 'Parliament' = a group of OWLS-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ collective noun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! Eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: 'Parliament' = formal gathering feel, eagles formal birds вҶ’ students confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ. Parliament = Owls, Convocation = Eagles!

вқҢ (C) Council вҖ” 'Council' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ committee/advisory group (human). Animal collective noun аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ standard аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ group = convocation, council аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Headquarters вҖ” 'Headquarters' = main office/base of an organization. Collective noun аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. Eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ group-аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'EAGLE = CONVOCATION (graduation ceremony)!' вҖ” Eagles = majestic, proud birds. Convocation = graduation = proud achievement! Eagles-аө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ group = Convocation. Parliament = Owls (parliament = wise decisions, owls = wise birds)!
вҖў Eagles vs Owls confusion: Eagles = CONVOCATION (majestic flight together). Owls = PARLIAMENT (wise, sitting in judgment). аҙҲ pair аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Animal collective nouns list вҖ” Eagles/Convocation, Owls/Parliament, Lions/Pride, Bees/Swarm, Fish/School вҖ” аҙҲ 5 pairs guaranteed PSC material!

questionText: Find out the compound noun:

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Blockhead

вҖў 'Blockhead' = block + head = a stupid person (аҙ®аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаө») вҖ” compound NOUN аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў 'Blockhead' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ person-аҙЁаөҶ describe аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ NOUN аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” 'He is a blockhead.'
вҖў Compound noun = two words combine аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ new NOUN аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў Test: 'Blockhead' sentence-аөҪ noun position-аөҪ (subject/object) use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙӮ вҶ’ compound noun вң“
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Compound NOUN = two words вҶ’ new NOUN (person/place/thing/idea)
вҖў Types of compound words:
  - Compound Noun: two words = noun (blockhead, football, notebook, sunlight)
  - Compound Verb: two words = verb (brainwash, proofread, babysit, daydream)
  - Compound Adjective: hyphenated usually, = adjective (ice-cold, grass-fed, well-known, kind-hearted)
вҖў Identifying compound nouns:
  - Can it be subject/object in a sentence? вҶ’ noun
  - 'He is a ___' вҶ’ if fits = noun
  - 'Blockhead' = 'He is a blockhead.' вң“ вҶ’ noun
  - 'Brainwash' = 'They brainwash people.' = verb, not noun in primary use
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Compound words вҖ” Parts of Speech identification topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ compound nouns:
  1. Blockhead = stupid person (noun)
  2. Football = sport (noun)
  3. Notebook = writing book (noun)
  4. Sunflower = plant (noun)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Brainwash вҖ” 'Brainwash' = primarily a compound VERB (to brainwash someone = to manipulate their thinking). 'They brainwash prisoners.' = verb. Noun form: 'brainwashing'. Compound noun question-аөҪ answer wrong. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'brain' + 'wash' = noun feel, students confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) Ice-cold вҖ” 'Ice-cold' = compound ADJECTIVE (hyphenated). 'An ice-cold drink' = adjective modifying noun. Noun аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” person/place/thing indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Grass-fed вҖ” 'Grass-fed' = compound ADJECTIVE (hyphenated past participle). 'Grass-fed cattle' = adjective. Noun position-аөҪ use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'COMPOUND NOUN = it IS something (a person/thing)!' вҖ” 'Blockhead IS a person (stupid one).' = NOUN! 'Brainwash = an ACTION' = VERB! 'Ice-cold = a QUALITY' = ADJECTIVE!
вҖў Quick test for compound noun: Put it after 'He is a ___' or 'I have a ___.' If it fits naturally = compound noun! 'He is a blockhead вң“' 'He is a brainwash вң—' 'He is an ice-cold вң—'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Hyphenated words (-) = almost always adjective. Verb-feel word = verb. Clear person/thing = noun. 'Blockhead' = clearly a person вҶ’ compound noun!

questionText: Antonym of the word 'lasting' is:

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Momentary

вҖў 'Lasting' = continuing for a long time, permanent, enduring (аҙҰаөҖаөјаҙҳаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙӮ аҙЁаҙҝаҙІаҙЁаҙҝаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ).
вҖў 'Momentary' = lasting for only a moment, very brief, transient (аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙЁаҙҝаҙ®аҙҝаҙ·аҙӮ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі).
вҖў Lasting (permanent/long) вҶ” Momentary (brief/instant) = exact opposites.
вҖў 'Momentary pain' = just for a second. 'Lasting peace' = long-lasting. Perfect antonyms!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: Lasting (long duration) вҶ” Momentary (very brief)
вҖў Synonyms of 'lasting': perpetual, eternal, infinite, permanent, enduring, everlasting
вҖў Antonyms of 'lasting': momentary, fleeting, transient, temporary, brief, short-lived
вҖў вӯҗ Options A, C, D are all SYNONYMS of 'lasting'!
  - Perpetual = continuing forever (synonym)
  - Infinite = without end (synonym)
  - Eternal = lasting forever (synonym)
вҖў Word family of 'moment':
  - Moment (noun) = a very short time
  - Momentary (adjective) = very brief
  - Momentarily (adverb) = for a moment
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Antonyms вҖ” PSC-аөҪ guaranteed topic! 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The pain was only momentary. (just for a second)
  2. They built a lasting friendship. (long-term)
  3. The peace was momentary вҖ” soon war resumed. (brief)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Perpetual вҖ” 'Perpetual' = never ending, continuous = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Perpetual motion', 'perpetual peace' = forever continuing. рҹӘӨ PSC Classic Trap: 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern. Students аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ synonym choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ.

вқҢ (C) Infinite вҖ” 'Infinite' = without limit or end = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. 'Infinite patience', 'infinite universe' = everlasting. Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Eternal вҖ” 'Eternal' = lasting forever = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. 'Eternal life', 'eternal love' = everlasting. Antonym аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'MOMENT = BLINK! Momentary = BLINK and it's gone!' вҖ” A moment = blink of an eye. Momentary = blink-fast duration = opposite of lasting!
вҖў Antonym pattern trick: Options A, C, D (perpetual, infinite, eternal) вҖ” аҙҮаҙө аҙ’аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶ 'forever' meaning. Same meaning = synonyms = eliminate all 3! Remaining B (momentary) = antonym!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-аөҪ options-аөҪ 3 same-direction words аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ вҶ’ аҙҶ 3 = synonyms вҶ’ 4th option = antonym answer!

questionText: Which is the correct spelling?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Lieutenant

вҖў 'Lieutenant' = correct spelling: L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T (10 letters).
вҖў 'Lieutenant' = a military/police officer rank (аҙІаҙ«аөҚаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙЁаө»аөҚаҙұаөҚ).
вҖў Breakdown: Lieu + ten + ant = Lieutenant.
вҖў 'Lieu' = place (French) вҖ” 'in lieu of' = in place of. Lieutenant = one who holds the place (of a superior).
вҖў British pronunciation: 'Leftenant' (аҙҮаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙЁаөҶ pronounce аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ spelling 'Lieutenant' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ!).
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ Quick Formula: L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T (Lieu + ten + ant)
вҖў Etymology: French 'lieu' (place) + 'tenant' (holding) = one holding the place of a superior
вҖў 'Lieu' word family:
  - Lieutenant (military rank)
  - In lieu of = instead of (аҙҮаҙӨаҙҝаө»аөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙӘаҙ•аҙ°аҙӮ)
вҖў British vs American pronunciation:
  - British: 'Leftenant' (pronounced like 'left')
  - American: 'Lootenent'
  - But SPELLING is always: Lieutenant (not Leftenant!)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC favourite tricky spellings:
  - Lieutenant = L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T
  - Necessary = N-E-C-E-S-S-A-R-Y (1C + 2S)
  - Accommodation = A-C-C-O-M-M-O-D-A-T-I-O-N (2C + 2M)
  - Occurrence = O-C-C-U-R-R-E-N-C-E (2C + 2R)
  - Separate = S-E-P-A-R-A-T-E (not 'seperate')
  - Privilege = P-R-I-V-I-L-E-G-E
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Spelling вҖ” PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ topic.
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The lieutenant led the platoon bravely. (military rank)
  2. I accepted cash in lieu of a cheque. (in place of)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Leiutenant вҖ” 'ei' аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ, аҙӘаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҶ 'u' вҖ” wrong order. Correct: 'lieu' (L-I-E-U). 'Lei-u' = wrong vowel order. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'ei' vs 'ie' confusion вҖ” students common mistake.

вқҢ (B) Leuitenant вҖ” 'Leu' вү  'Lieu'. Vowel order wrong: L-E-U vs correct L-I-E-U. 'i' missing аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Similar sound, wrong spelling.

вқҢ (C) Leftenant вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ British PRONUNCIATION аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! British people 'Lieutenant'-аҙЁаөҶ 'Leftenant' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ pronounce аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ correct SPELLING аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. Pronunciation = leftenant, Spelling = Lieutenant. рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: British accent-аөҪ 'leftenant' аҙ•аөҮаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ аҙҶ spelling correct feel аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip
