вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He was accused of stealing. (аҙ®аөӢаҙ·аөҚаҙҹаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙ°аөӢаҙӘаҙЈаҙӮ)
  2. She is suspected of lying. (аҙЁаөҒаҙЈ аҙӘаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ аҙёаҙӮаҙ¶аҙҜаҙӮ)
  3. They convicted him of murder. (аҙ•аөҠаҙІаҙӘаҙҫаҙӨаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ аҙ¶аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ·)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) for
вҖў 'Accuse for' = wrong collocation. 'Blame FOR' = correct (blame for = аҙ•аөҒаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙӮ аҙҡаөҒаҙ®аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•), but 'accuse' = 'OF' only.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'For' = reason indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҶ’ 'accused for talking' = reason feel. But 'accuse' = 'of' fixed pair. 'Blame for' вү  'accuse of' вҖ” different verbs, different prepositions!

вқҢ (C) about
вҖў 'Accuse about' = not a standard English collocation. 'About' = topic indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ, not accusation.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'About talking' = talking-аҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ feel вҶ’ 'about' natural sound. But 'accuse' = 'of' fixed. 'Complain about' вң“ / 'accuse of' вң“ вҖ” confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ.

вқҢ (D) after
вҖў 'Accuse after' = grammatically impossible meaning. 'After' = time/sequence indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҖ” accusation context-аөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'After talking' = sequence feel вҶ’ she accused me after I talked. But 'accuse + after' = not a collocation.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ACCUSED OF a CRIME вҖ” OF always follows accusation! Like in court: accused OF theft, OF murder, OF lying!' вҖ” Legal language: 'accused OF' = always OF. аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ 'accused FOR/ABOUT/AFTER' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Quick list: accuse OF, suspect OF, convict OF, guilty OF вҖ” all crimes/wrongdoings use 'OF'.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Accuse + blank' question вҶ’ 'of' option аҙүаҙҹаө» choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•. 'for/about/after' = all eliminate. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: We have been living here __________ five years.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) for

вҖў 'Five years' = a period/duration of time (аҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙҰаөҲаөјаҙҳаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ) вҶ’ 'for' вң“
вҖў 'For' = duration indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ preposition вҖ” 'for + number + years/months/days'
вҖў 'We have been living here for five years' = 'аҙһаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙ…аҙһаөҚаҙҡаөҚ аҙөаөјаҙ·аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙӨаҙҫаҙ®аҙёаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
вҖў Present Perfect Continuous (have been living) + for = duration вң“
вҖў Key distinction: 'for' = duration (5 years, 3 months, 2 hours). 'since' = starting point (2019, Monday, morning).
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: FOR + DURATION (period of time). SINCE + POINT IN TIME.
вҖў FOR vs SINCE (PSC most-tested distinction):
  - FOR + duration: for five years, for two hours, for a week, for ages
  - SINCE + point: since 2019, since Monday, since morning, since childhood
вҖў Test: 'five years' = duration (аҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙҰаөҲаөјаҙҳаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ) вҶ’ FOR вң“
вҖў 'Five years' = not a specific starting point вҶ’ SINCE вң—
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: FOR vs SINCE = PSC-аөҪ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ frequently tested preposition pair!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. I have been waiting FOR two hours. (two hours = duration)
  2. She has lived here SINCE 2015. (2015 = starting point)
  3. He has been working FOR three years. (three years = duration)
  4. They have known each other SINCE childhood. (childhood = starting point)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) since
вҖў 'Since' = point in time (specific moment) indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. 'Five years' = duration, not a point.
вҖў 'Since five years' = wrong. 'Since 2019' вң“ / 'for five years' вң“
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ common mistake! Both 'since' and 'for' = Perfect tense-аөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ. But 'five years' = duration вҶ’ 'for'. 'Since 2019' вң“ but 'since five years' вң—.

вқҢ (B) before
вҖў 'Before' = earlier time indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. 'We have been living before five years' = meaningless/wrong.
вҖў 'Before' = sequence/earlier time preposition, not duration marker.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Before' sounds like time context вҶ’ time feel. But 'before' = earlier than, not duration.

вқҢ (D) about
вҖў 'About' = approximately (аҙҸаҙ•аҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ). 'For about five years' = acceptable informally, but 'for' alone is the correct answer here since 'about' is approximate.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'About five years' = 'approximately five years' casual feel. But 'for' = grammatically precise answer for duration.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'FOR = a span of time you can MEASURE! SINCE = a specific moment you can POINT TO on a calendar!' вҖ” FOR five years (measure/count), SINCE 2019 (point on calendar). 'Five years' = measurable span вҶ’ FOR!
вҖў Quick test: Ask 'how long?' вҶ’ FOR. Ask 'from when?' вҶ’ SINCE.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Number + years/months/days/hours' вҶ’ always FOR. Specific year/day/season вҶ’ always SINCE. 'Five years' = number + years вҶ’ FOR. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Seena __________ tomorrow to attend the wedding.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) will come

вҖў 'Tomorrow' = аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝ (future) signal word вҶ’ future tense вң“
вҖў 'Will come' = Simple Future tense вҖ” future planned action вң“
вҖў 'Seena will come tomorrow to attend the wedding' = 'Seena аҙЁаҙҫаҙіаөҶ аҙөаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ№аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙӘаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӮ.'
вҖў 'Tomorrow' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ вҶ’ Simple Future (will + V1) вң“
вҖў Pattern: Subject + will + base verb (V1) = future action
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: TOMORROW / NEXT WEEK / SOON / LATER вҶ’ WILL + V1 (Simple Future)
вҖў Future tense signal words:
  - tomorrow, next week, next year, next month
  - soon, later, in the future, shortly
  - tonight (future context), the day after tomorrow
вҖў Future tense structure: Subject + WILL + V1 (base form)
  - Seena will come (not 'will comes' вҖ” base form after 'will'!)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Signal words + tense matching = PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаөҶ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He will arrive tomorrow. (tomorrow = future)
  2. They will meet next week. (next week = future)
  3. She will call soon. (soon = future)
  4. I will finish it later. (later = future)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) come
вҖў 'Come' = Simple Present base form. Present tense-аөҪ 'Seena come' = wrong (subject-verb agreement error too вҖ” 'Seena comes').
вҖў 'Tomorrow' = future вҶ’ present tense аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ here.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Come' = simple/base form вҶ’ familiar. But 'tomorrow' = future вҶ’ 'will come' needed.

вқҢ (B) has come
вҖў 'Has come' = Present Perfect tense вҖ” past action with present relevance. 'Tomorrow' = future вҶ’ past tense impossible.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Has come' = correct-sounding formal phrase. But Present Perfect = past-to-present; 'tomorrow' = future. Tense mismatch!

вқҢ (C) coming
вҖў 'Coming' = present participle (gerund/continuous form). Without auxiliary 'is', 'coming' alone as main verb = incomplete sentence.
вҖў 'Seena coming tomorrow' = incomplete (no auxiliary). 'Seena IS coming tomorrow' вң“ вҖ” but 'coming' alone = wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Coming tomorrow' = conversational feel. But without 'is', 'coming' alone = grammatically incomplete.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'TOMORROW = WILL! See tomorrow, write will вҖ” it's a deal!' вҖ” 'Tomorrow' = future вҶ’ 'will' = future helper. No exceptions! 'Tomorrow' + 'will' = always partners.
вҖў Signal word вҶ’ tense:
  - yesterday вҶ’ Simple Past (V2)
  - now/currently вҶ’ Present Continuous (is/are + V-ing)
  - already/just вҶ’ Present Perfect (has/have + V3)
  - tomorrow/next вҶ’ Simple Future (will + V1)
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Tomorrow' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі sentence вҶ’ 'will + V1' option choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: It was __________ unanimous decision.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) a

вҖў 'Unanimous' = аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҫаҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ 'you-NAN-i-mous' вҖ” аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ sound = /j/ (consonant sound).
вҖў Article rule: a/an = SOUND-based, not spelling-based! 'U' letter аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ sound = /j/ (consonant) вҶ’ 'a' вң“
вҖў 'A unanimous decision' = 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҸаҙ•аөӢаҙӘаҙҝаҙӨ аҙӨаөҖаҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙӮ' вң“
вҖў 'An' = vowel SOUND аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ. 'Unanimous' vowel letter (U) аҙҶаҙЈаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ consonant sound вҶ’ 'a'.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ…аҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҸаҙ•аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙ аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ аҙӨаөҖаҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙӮ аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: A/AN = SOUND, not SPELLING! Vowel SOUND вҶ’ AN. Consonant SOUND вҶ’ A.
вҖў Tricky 'U' words вҖ” consonant sound /j/ вҶ’ 'A' (not 'an'):
  - a unanimous decision (you-NAN-i-mous)
  - a university (you-ni-VER-si-ty)
  - a unique opportunity (you-NEEK)
  - a useful tool (you-SE-ful)
  - a union (YOU-nion)
вҖў Tricky words вҖ” vowel sound вҶ’ 'AN' (even if consonant letter):
  - an hour (h silent вҶ’ 'our')
  - an honest man (h silent вҶ’ 'on-est')
  - an MBA (em-bee-ay вҖ” vowel sound 'e')
  - an X-ray (ex-ray вҖ” vowel sound 'e')
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Unanimous/university/unique/useful' article trap = PSC-аөҪ classic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. It was a unique experience. (you-NEEK = consonant /j/)
  2. She is a university student. (you-ni = consonant /j/)
  3. It was an unanimous... вң— / a unanimous вң“
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) an
вҖў аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ common mistake! 'Unanimous' 'U' vowel letter-аөҪ аҙӨаөҒаҙҹаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ вҶ’ 'an' feel.
вҖў But 'U' in unanimous = /j/ consonant sound 'you' вҶ’ 'a' not 'an'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: U = vowel letter вҶ’ 'an unanimous' = natural sounding. But SOUND rule: unanimous = 'you-nanimous' вҶ’ consonant вҶ’ 'a'! Classic PSC trap.

вқҢ (C) none
вҖў 'Unanimous decision' = countable singular noun вҶ’ article needed. 'None' (no article) = wrong for singular countable nouns in this context.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'None' = skip article feel вҶ’ clean sentence feel. But singular countable noun 'decision' вҶ’ article required.

вқҢ (D) the
вҖў 'The' = definite article (specific/known item). 'A unanimous decision' = first mention, one of many possible decisions вҶ’ indefinite 'a' needed.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'The decision' = sounds specific/important вҶ’ 'the' feel. But no specific decision established вҶ’ 'a' (indefinite).
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'U words starting with YOU sound = A, not AN! A University, A Unanimous, A Unique, A Useful, A Union вҖ” all start with YOU sound!' вҖ” Say it aloud: 'you-nanimous' вҶ’ starts with Y sound вҶ’ 'A'.
вҖў Opposite trap: 'An Hour, An Honest, An MBA, An X-ray' вҖ” letter H/M/X but vowel sound вҶ’ 'an'.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Unanimous/university/unique/useful/union' question вҶ’ always 'a' (not 'an'). Say the word aloud and listen to the first sound. Consonant sound = 'a'. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: One who treats skin diseases is known as __________.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Dermatologist

вҖў 'Dermatologist' = аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ® аҙ°аөӢаҙ— аҙөаҙҝаҙҰаҙ—аөҚаҙ§аө» вҖ” skin diseases treat аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ doctor.
вҖў Root: Greek 'derma' = skin (аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙӮ) + 'logos' = study + 'ist' = specialist.
вҖў Dermatology = аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ® аҙ¶аҙҫаҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ; Dermatologist = аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ® аҙ°аөӢаҙ— аҙЎаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙҹаөј.
вҖў Treats: acne, eczema, psoriasis, skin allergies, rashes вҖ” all skin conditions.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ® аҙ°аөӢаҙ—аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙҡаҙҝаҙ•аҙҝаҙӨаөҚаҙёаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаөҶ Dermatologist аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: DERMA (Greek) = SKIN вҶ’ DERMATOLOGIST = skin specialist!
вҖў Medical specialists вҖ” root word memory:
  - Dermatologist вҶ’ 'derma' = skin (аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙӮ)
  - Psychologist вҶ’ 'psycho' = mind (аҙ®аҙЁаҙёаөҚаҙёаөҚ)
  - Ophthalmologist вҶ’ 'ophthalmos' = eye (аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаөҚ)
  - Gynaecologist вҶ’ 'gyne' = woman (аҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҖ) вҖ” female diseases
  - Cardiologist вҶ’ 'cardio' = heart (аҙ№аөғаҙҰаҙҜаҙӮ)
  - Neurologist вҶ’ 'neuro' = nerve (аҙЁаҙҫаҙЎаҙҝ)
  - Orthopaedic вҶ’ 'ortho' = bone/straight (аҙ…аҙёаөҚаҙҘаҙҝ)
  - Paediatrician вҶ’ 'paed' = child (аҙ•аөҒаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝ)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Medical specialists one-word substitutes = PSC-аөҪ highly frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She visited a dermatologist for her skin allergy. (аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ® аҙ…аҙІаөјаҙңаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ)
  2. The ophthalmologist checked her vision. (аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаөҚ specialist)
  3. A cardiologist treats heart problems. (аҙ№аөғаҙҰаҙҜ specialist)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Psychologist
вҖў 'Psychologist' = аҙ®аҙЁаҙғаҙ¶аҙҫаҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙңаөҚаҙһаө» вҖ” mind/behaviour treat аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙөаө» (not skin).
вҖў 'Psycho' = mind вҶ’ Psychologist = mental health specialist.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: '-ologist' suffix common вҶ’ all medical specialist feel. 'Psycho' = mind, not skin. аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•: аҙҡаөјаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙӮ = 'derma'.

вқҢ (B) Ophthalmologist
вҖў 'Ophthalmologist' = аҙЁаөҮаҙӨаөҚаҙ° аҙөаөҲаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаө» вҖ” eye specialist. 'Ophthalmos' = eye.
вҖў Treats: vision problems, glaucoma, cataracts вҖ” eye diseases, not skin.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Long complicated word вҶ’ important/specialist feel. 'Ophthalm' = eye root вҖ” skin аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (C) Gynaecologist
вҖў 'Gynaecologist' = аҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҖ аҙ°аөӢаҙ— аҙөаҙҝаҙҰаҙ—аөҚаҙ§ вҖ” female reproductive system specialist. 'Gyne' = woman.
вҖў Treats: women's health issues вҖ” pregnancy, menstrual disorders, not skin diseases.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Medical doctor feel вҶ’ could be any specialist. 'Gyne' = woman, not skin.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'DERMA sounds like DERM = SKIN in Malayalam too! DERMatologist = SKIN doctor! Think of dermal cream for skin!' вҖ” Dermal cream = skin cream вҶ’ Dermatologist = skin doctor!
вҖў Must-know doctor list for PSC:
  - Skin вҶ’ Dermatologist рҹ§ҙ
  - Eye вҶ’ Ophthalmologist рҹ‘ҒпёҸ
  - Mind вҶ’ Psychologist рҹ§ 
  - Heart вҶ’ Cardiologist вқӨпёҸ
  - Child вҶ’ Paediatrician рҹ‘¶
  - Woman вҶ’ Gynaecologist рҹ‘©
  - Bone вҶ’ Orthopaedician рҹҰҙ
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Skin diseases' вҶ’ Dermatologist. Root 'derma' = skin = always. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Do you mind __________ the answers?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) writing

вҖў 'Mind' = gerund (V-ing) take аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ verb вҖ” fixed rule!
вҖў 'Do you mind writing the answers?' = 'аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙҺаҙҙаөҒаҙӨаҙҫаө» аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҮаҙ·аөҚаҙҹаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ / mind аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ?'
вҖў Pattern: mind + V-ing (gerund) вҖ” 'to write' аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ, 'writing' вң“
вҖў 'Mind' = dislike/object to вҖ” always followed by gerund, never infinitive.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙҺаҙҙаөҒаҙӨаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙӨаҙҹаҙёаөҚаҙёаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: MIND + V-ING (gerund) вҖ” always! Never 'mind to + V1'.
вҖў Verbs always followed by GERUND (V-ing) вҖ” PSC must-know list:
  - mind + V-ing вҶ’ 'mind writing'
  - enjoy + V-ing вҶ’ 'enjoy reading'
  - avoid + V-ing вҶ’ 'avoid going'
  - finish + V-ing вҶ’ 'finish eating'
  - consider + V-ing вҶ’ 'consider moving'
  - suggest + V-ing вҶ’ 'suggest trying'
  - keep + V-ing вҶ’ 'keep working'
  - risk + V-ing вҶ’ 'risk losing'
  - deny + V-ing вҶ’ 'deny stealing'
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Gerund vs Infinitive after specific verbs = PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Do you mind waiting? (wait вҶ’ waiting вң“)
  2. He enjoys playing cricket. (play вҶ’ playing вң“)
  3. She avoided going there. (go вҶ’ going вң“)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) to write
вҖў 'To write' = infinitive. 'Mind + to write' = wrong вҖ” 'mind' never takes infinitive.
вҖў 'Mind to write' = incorrect English. Mind takes ONLY gerund.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'To write' = formal/correct-sounding. Many verbs DO take infinitive (want to, plan to, hope to). But 'mind' is NOT one of them. 'Mind + V-ing' only.

вқҢ (C) write
вҖў 'Write' = bare infinitive (base form). After 'mind', base form without 'to' = also wrong.
вҖў Only gerund ('writing') works after 'mind'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Write' = simplest/shortest option вҶ’ first instinct. But 'mind' requires '-ing' form, not base form.

вқҢ (D) wrote
вҖў 'Wrote' = Simple Past tense. Tense аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ relevant аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” verb form (gerund) question аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў 'Do you mind wrote?' = tense + structure = both wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Wrote' = past writing context feel. But this is a present question about willingness, not past action.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'MIND the ING! Do you MIND? Always add -ING after MIND!' вҖ” 'Mind' = -ing partner. mind + writing, mind + waiting, mind + coming вҖ” always -ing.
вҖў Gerund verb list song: 'Enjoy, avoid, mind, finish, keep, suggest вҖ” all take -ING, never forget!'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Mind + blank' вҶ’ choose the '-ing' option immediately. 'To write/write/wrote' = all eliminate. 'Writing' = only correct form. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: What is the meaning of the word 'dormant'?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) inactive

вҖў 'Dormant' = 'аҙЁаҙҝаҙ·аөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙӮ, аҙүаҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі, temporarily inactive' вҖ” sleeping but not dead.
вҖў 'Inactive' = 'аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙ°аҙ№аҙҝаҙӨаҙӮ' вҖ” not active/working = synonym of dormant вң“
вҖў Word root: Latin 'dormire' = to sleep (аҙүаҙұаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙ•) вҶ’ dormant = sleeping state.
вҖў Real-world examples: 'dormant volcano' (аҙүаҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі аҙ…аҙ—аөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙӘаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙӨаҙӮ), 'dormant account' (аҙЁаҙҝаҙ·аөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙҝаҙҜ account).
вҖў Meaning: 'Dormant = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ temporarily аҙЁаҙҝаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙөаҙҡаөҚаҙҡ/аҙүаҙұаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙ…аҙөаҙёаөҚаҙҘ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: DORMANT = DORMIR (sleep in Latin/French) вҶ’ sleeping/inactive state!
вҖў Word family of 'dormant':
  - dormant (adjective) = inactive, sleeping
  - dormancy (noun) = the state of being dormant
  - dormitory (noun) = sleeping room (dorm = dormir = sleep)
вҖў Synonyms of dormant: inactive, sleeping, latent, quiescent, resting, suspended
вҖў Antonyms of dormant: active, awake, working, functioning, operational
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Vocabulary synonyms вҖ” '-ant' adjectives from Latin roots = PSC frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The volcano has been dormant for centuries. (аҙүаҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ, not erupting)
  2. His talent lay dormant for years. (hidden/inactive talent)
  3. A dormant bank account will be frozen. (inactive account)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) active
вҖў 'Active' = 'аҙёаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙӮ, аҙҠаөјаҙңаөҚаҙңаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙІаҙӮ' вҖ” dormant-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ ANTONYM (opposite), synonym аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ!
вҖў 'Dormant volcano' = NOT active. 'Active volcano' = erupting/alive.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Active' = familiar positive word. But question = meaning of 'dormant' вҶ’ 'active' = exact opposite!

вқҢ (B) working
вҖў 'Working' = 'аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ' вҖ” also opposite of dormant. Dormant = NOT working.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Working' = common word вҶ’ easy answer feel. But dormant = not working, so 'working' = antonym.

вқҢ (C) smart
вҖў 'Smart' = 'аҙ¬аөҒаҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҝаҙ¶аҙҫаҙІаҙҝ, clever' вҖ” dormant-аҙүаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ relation-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Smart' = positive/impressive word вҶ’ could feel like a compliment meaning. But dormant has nothing to do with intelligence.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'DORMANT = DORMITORY = sleeping room! Dormant = sleeping/inactive! Things in a DORM are sleeping!' вҖ” Dormitory (hostel sleeping room) вҶ’ DORM вҶ’ sleep вҶ’ dormant = sleeping/inactive state.
вҖў Dormant = things that COULD be active but are currently sleeping:
  - Dormant volcano рҹҢӢ (sleeping, could erupt)
  - Dormant seed рҹҢұ (not yet sprouted)
  - Dormant account рҹҸҰ (not being used)
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Dormant' = inactive. See 'active/working' вҶ’ eliminate (opposites). See 'smart' вҶ’ eliminate (unrelated). 'Inactive' = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: The evening was __________ than the morning.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) hotter

вҖў 'Than' = comparative degree signal word вҶ’ comparative form аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ.
вҖў 'Hot' = one-syllable adjective вҶ’ comparative = '-er' suffix add аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•: hot вҶ’ hotter вң“
вҖў 'The evening was hotter than the morning' = 'morning-аҙҮаҙЁаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаөҫ evening аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ аҙҡаөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
вҖў Spelling rule: hot = consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) pattern вҶ’ final consonant double: hot вҶ’ hott + er = hotter вң“
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ°аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙҝ, morning-аҙҮаҙЁаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаөҫ аҙҡаөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: ONE-SYLLABLE adjective + 'than' вҶ’ add -ER (double final consonant if CVC pattern).
вҖў Degrees of comparison вҖ” 'hot':
  - Positive: hot (simple)
  - Comparative: hotter + than (comparing two)
  - Superlative: the hottest (comparing with all)
вҖў CVC doubling rule (Consonant-Vowel-Consonant):
  - hot вҶ’ hotter (t doubles)
  - big вҶ’ bigger (g doubles)
  - thin вҶ’ thinner (n doubles)
  - fat вҶ’ fatter (t doubles)
  - sad вҶ’ sadder (d doubles)
вҖў 'More hot' = WRONG вҖ” 'more' used only with 2+ syllable adjectives.
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: One-syllable adjective comparative forms = PSC-аөҪ regular!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰа®ҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Today is hotter than yesterday. (hot вҶ’ hotter)
  2. He is bigger than his brother. (big вҶ’ bigger)
  3. This road is longer than that one. (long вҶ’ longer)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) hot
вҖў 'Hot' = positive degree. 'Than' signal word аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ comparative needed. 'Hot than' = wrong вҖ” positive degree + 'than' = impossible combination.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Hot' = base word, familiar. 'Hot than' natural sound feel. But 'than' = comparative marker вҶ’ 'hotter' required.

вқҢ (C) more hot
вҖў 'More hot' = wrong вҖ” 'more' used only with adjectives of 2+ syllables (more beautiful, more interesting). 'Hot' = 1 syllable вҶ’ '-er' suffix, not 'more'.
вҖў 'More hot' = double comparative error вҖ” never correct in English.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'More hot' = formal/emphatic feel. But 1-syllable adjectives NEVER use 'more' вҖ” always '-er'. 'More hotter' = also wrong (double comparative).

вқҢ (D) hottest
вҖў 'Hottest' = superlative degree (comparing with all). 'Than' signal = comparative, not superlative.
вҖў Superlative uses 'the' + '-est': 'the hottest day'. 'Than' + superlative = impossible.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Hottest' = extreme heat feel вҶ’ fits evening context. But 'than' = two items compared вҶ’ comparative (-er), not superlative (-est).
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'THAN = COMPARATIVE (-ER). THE + EST = SUPERLATIVE. Than аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ -er. The + est аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ superlative!' вҖ” 'Than' = two things compared = comparative. No 'than' + 'the hottest' = superlative.
вҖў CVC doubling rule: hotвҶ’hotter, bigвҶ’bigger, thinвҶ’thinner, fatвҶ’fatter. Say it: C-V-C = Double the C!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Than' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі sentence вҶ’ '-er' comparative option choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•. 'More hot' = always wrong for 1-syllable. 'Hottest' = superlative, needs 'the', not 'than'. 'Hotter' = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Nothing will happen, __________ ?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) will it?

вҖў 'Nothing' = negative word (аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ) вҶ’ question tag = POSITIVE.
вҖў Negative statement вҶ’ Positive tag rule: 'Nothing will happen' (negative) вҶ’ 'will it?' (positive tag) вң“
вҖў Pronoun: 'Nothing' = thing/idea вҶ’ pronoun = 'it' вң“
вҖў Auxiliary: main sentence = 'will' вҶ’ tag auxiliary = 'will' вң“
вҖў 'Nothing will happen, will it?' = 'аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ аҙёаҙӮаҙӯаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ, аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ?'
вҖў Meaning: Negative statement-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ positive tag = confirmation/reassurance seek аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: NEGATIVE statement вҶ’ POSITIVE tag. POSITIVE statement вҶ’ NEGATIVE tag.
вҖў 'Nothing' = negative pronoun (like no one, nobody, nowhere, never, hardly, scarcely) вҶ’ sentence is NEGATIVE вҶ’ tag = POSITIVE.
вҖў Negative words that make a sentence negative:
  - nothing, nobody, no one, nowhere, never
  - hardly, scarcely, barely, seldom, rarely
  - All these вҶ’ POSITIVE question tag!
вҖў Tag construction:
  1. Identify auxiliary in main sentence: 'will'
  2. Identify pronoun for subject: 'nothing' вҶ’ 'it'
  3. Sentence negative? вҶ’ Positive tag: 'will it?'
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Nothing/nobody/nowhere' question tags = PSC-аөҪ classic trap!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Nothing is perfect, is it? (nothing=negative вҶ’ positive tag 'is it')
  2. Nobody came, did they? (nobody=negative вҶ’ positive 'did they')
  3. She never lies, does she? (never=negative вҶ’ positive 'does she')
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) won't it?
вҖў 'Won't it?' = negative tag. 'Nothing will happen' = already negative sentence вҶ’ POSITIVE tag needed, not negative.
вҖў 'Nothing will happen, won't it?' = negative + negative = double negative = wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Will happen' positive verb feel вҶ’ negative tag 'won't it' feel. But 'nothing' makes sentence negative вҶ’ positive tag!

вқҢ (C) wouldn't it?
вҖў 'Wouldn't' = negative of 'would' вҖ” wrong auxiliary. Main sentence = 'will', not 'would'. Tag must match main auxiliary.
вҖў 'Would' вү  'will' вҖ” different tenses. Tag must use SAME auxiliary as main sentence.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Wouldn't it?' = formal/polite question feel. But wrong auxiliary (would вү  will) AND wrong positive/negative.

вқҢ (D) would it?
вҖў 'Would' = wrong auxiliary вҖ” main sentence uses 'will', not 'would'. Tag auxiliary must match.
вҖў 'Would it?' = correct positive form BUT wrong auxiliary.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Would it?' = positive tag (correct type) but wrong auxiliary. 'Will' вҶ’ 'will it?' (not 'would it?'). Auxiliary must be exact match.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'NOTHING = NEGATIVE = TAG GOES POSITIVE! Nothing negative вҶ’ tag positive, like a seesaw вҖ” one side down, other side UP!' вҖ” Negative statement вҶ’ positive tag. 'Nothing' = down (negative) вҶ’ tag = up (positive 'will it?').
вҖў Negative word checklist: nothing, nobody, no one, never, hardly, barely, scarcely вҶ’ ALL need POSITIVE tags!
вҖў Two-step tag formula:
  1. 'Nothing will happen' вҶ’ negative sentence вҶ’ positive tag
  2. Auxiliary = 'will', pronoun = 'it' вҶ’ 'will it?' вң“
вҖў PSC exam strategy: See 'nothing/nobody/never' вҶ’ choose POSITIVE tag option. 'Will it?' = positive auxiliary 'will' + positive form. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Rewrite the following sentence using 'so вҖҰ that':
The germ is too small to be seen with a naked eye.
optionA: The germ is so small that it can be seen with a naked eye.
optionB: The germ is so small that it cannot be seen with a naked eye.
optionC: The germ is so small that it could not be seen with a naked eye.
optionD: The germ is so small to be seen with a naked eye.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) The germ is so small that it cannot be seen with a naked eye.

вҖў 'Too + adjective + to + verb' вҶ’ 'So + adjective + that + subject + cannot + verb' = standard transformation rule вң“
вҖў 'Too small to be seen' = 'so small that it CANNOT be seen' вҖ” negative meaning preserved вң“
вҖў 'Too' = negative implication (аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙ…аҙөаҙёаөҚаҙҘ) вҶ’ 'cannot' in the 'so...that' form.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙҶ аҙ°аөӢаҙ—аҙҫаҙЈаөҒ аҙЁаҙ—аөҚаҙЁаҙЁаөҮаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ аҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ аҙ•аҙҫаҙЈаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙөаҙҝаҙ§аҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙұаөҒаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ.'
вҖў Key: 'Too...to' = inability вҶ’ 'so...that...cannot' вҖ” negative preserved always!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: TOO + adj + to + V1 вҶ’ SO + adj + THAT + subject + CANNOT + V1
вҖў Transformation table:
  - too small to see вҶ’ so small that we cannot see
  - too heavy to lift вҶ’ so heavy that we cannot lift it
  - too tired to walk вҶ’ so tired that she could not walk
вҖў вҡ пёҸ Critical rule: 'Too' = negative meaning вҶ’ 'that' clause MUST have 'cannot/could not' (NEVER 'can')
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Too...to' вҶ’ 'so...that' transformation = PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаөҶ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He is too weak to run. вҶ’ He is so weak that he cannot run.
  2. The box is too heavy to carry. вҶ’ The box is so heavy that we cannot carry it.
  3. She was too busy to attend. вҶ’ She was so busy that she could not attend.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) ...so small that it CAN be seen...
вҖў 'Can be seen' = positive meaning. 'Too small to be seen' = cannot be seen (negative). 'Can' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ meaning аҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ вҖ” germ naked eye-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ•аҙҫаҙЈаҙҫаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ, which is opposite!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'So small that it can' = аҙ’аҙҙаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аө» sentence feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'too...to' = negative вҶ’ 'cannot' mandatory.

вқҢ (C) ...so small that it COULD NOT be seen...
вҖў 'Could not' = past modal. Original sentence present tense ('is') вҶ’ 'cannot' (present) is correct, not 'could not' (past/hypothetical).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Could not' = formal/polite negative feel вҶ’ correct sound. But tense mismatch вҖ” 'is too small' (present) вҶ’ 'cannot' (present).

вқҢ (D) ...so small TO be seen...
вҖў This keeps 'to' вҖ” not a proper 'so...that' transformation. 'So small to be seen' = incomplete/ungrammatical in this form.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Looks like a transformation but 'that' clause is missing. 'So...that' needs a full clause with subject + verb.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'TOO = CANNOT! Too tired to walk = so tired that I CANNOT walk. Too always means CANNOT in disguise!' вҖ” 'Too' hidden meaning = inability = cannot.
вҖў Formula in one line: TOO + adj + to + V вҶ’ SO + adj + THAT + cannot + V
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Too...to' вҶ’ 'so...that' question вҶ’ Look for 'cannot/could not' option first. Option with 'can' (positive) = always eliminate. Option with 'to' alone (no 'that') = eliminate. 'Cannot' = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Find the word equivalent to 'a feeling of shyness or embarrassment that stops you from doing or saying what you really want'.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Inhibition

вҖў 'Inhibition' = 'аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝ, аҙІаҙңаөҚаҙң, аҙүаөҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҹаҙёаөҚаҙёаҙӮ' вҖ” a psychological feeling of shyness/embarrassment that holds you back.
вҖў 'Inhibition stops you from doing/saying what you want' = exact definition вң“
вҖў Word root: Latin 'inhibere' = to hold back (аҙӨаҙҹаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒаҙЁаҙҝаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•) вҶ’ inhibition = the feeling that holds you back.
вҖў Plural 'inhibitions': 'She has no inhibitions' = аҙ…аҙөаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаөҫ аҙҶаҙ—аөҚаҙ°аҙ№аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӨаҙҹаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙІаҙңаөҚаҙң/аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝ/аҙүаөҫаҙӯаҙҜаҙӮ = Inhibition.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: IN- (in/against) +HIBIT (hold) = hold back feeling вҶ’ INHIBITION!
вҖў Word family of 'inhibit/inhibition':
  - inhibit (verb) = аҙӨаҙҹаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒаҙөаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, hold back
  - inhibition (noun) = the feeling of being held back
  - inhibited (adjective) = shy, self-conscious, restrained
  - uninhibited (adjective) = free, without restraint (opposite)
вҖў Related psychological terms (PSC vocabulary):
  - Inhibition = shyness/restraint feeling
  - Pessimism = negative outlook (аҙҰаөҒаҙ°аҙҫаҙ¶)
  - Optimism = positive outlook (аҙ¶аөҒаҙӯаҙҫаҙӘаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙөаҙҝаҙ¶аөҚаҙөаҙҫаҙёаҙӮ)
  - Aggressiveness = hostile/forceful behaviour
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: One-word substitute for psychological feelings = PSC frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Stage fright is a common inhibition. (stage-аөҪ nervous = inhibition)
  2. Her inhibitions prevented her from speaking. (inhibition blocked speech)
  3. He has no inhibitions about expressing himself. (no shyness)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Miserable
вҖў 'Miserable' = 'аҙҰаҙҜаҙЁаөҖаҙҜаҙӮ, аҙҰаөҒаҙғаҙ–аҙ•аҙ°аҙӮ' вҖ” a feeling of being very unhappy/uncomfortable. Not specifically shyness/restraint.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Miserable' = negative feeling word вҶ’ shyness = also negative feeling вҶ’ seem related. But miserable = sadness/discomfort, not the specific restraint/shyness inhibition describes.

вқҢ (C) Pessimism
вҖў 'Pessimism' = 'аҙЁаҙҝаҙ°аҙҫаҙ¶аҙҫаҙөаҙҫаҙҰаҙӮ' вҖ” the tendency to see the worst in everything. Nothing to do with shyness.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Pessimism = negative attitude вҶ’ negative feeling like shyness вҶ’ related feel. But pessimism = outlook about future, not a restraining social feeling.

вқҢ (D) Aggressiveness
вҖў 'Aggressiveness' = 'аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙ®аҙЈаөӢаҙӨаөҚаҙёаөҒаҙ•аҙӨ' вҖ” forceful, hostile behaviour. Complete OPPOSITE of shyness/inhibition.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Aggressiveness' = strong/intense feeling вҶ’ feeling question вҶ’ could fit. But inhibition = holds you BACK. Aggressiveness = pushes you FORWARD. Opposite meanings.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'INHIBITION = IN +HIBIT = held IN! Feeling held inside that stops you from coming out!' вҖ” Inhibition = trapped feeling inside, can't speak/act freely.
вҖў 'Inhibitions' in everyday life: stage fright (stage-аөҪ аҙӯаҙҜаҙӮ), social anxiety (social аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ nervous), shyness in public = all forms of inhibition.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Shyness/embarrassment that STOPS you' вҶ’ look for 'restraint/holding back' word = Inhibition. 'Miserable' = sadness, 'Pessimism' = future outlook, 'Aggressiveness' = opposite. Only Inhibition = correct. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank in the given below sentence:
Neither of them __________ me.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) loves

вҖў 'Neither of them' = singular subject вҖ” 'neither' = distributive pronoun, always singular вң“
вҖў Singular subject вҶ’ singular verb: 'loves' (not 'love') вң“
вҖў 'Neither of them loves me' = 'аҙ…аҙөаҙ°аҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ°аөҒаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҶ аҙёаөҚаҙЁаөҮаҙ№аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.'
вҖў 'Them' plural аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ subject = 'neither' (singular) вҶ’ verb = singular.
вҖў Pattern: Neither of + plural noun/pronoun вҶ’ singular verb (is/has/loves/goes etc.)
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: NEITHER / EITHER + of + plural вҶ’ SINGULAR verb!
вҖў Distributive pronouns вҖ” always singular:
  - Neither of them LOVES (not love)
  - Either of the boys IS (not are)
  - Each of the students HAS (not have)
  - Every one of them KNOWS (not know)
вҖў 'Love' (base form) vs 'loves' (3rd person singular):
  - He/She/It/Neither/Each/Every вҶ’ loves (with 's')
  - I/We/You/They вҶ’ love (no 's')
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Neither/Either + of = singular verb = PSC-аөҪ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ tested SVA rule!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Neither of the boys loves cricket. (neither вҶ’ loves)
  2. Either of them is welcome. (either вҶ’ is)
  3. None of the students knows the answer. (none вҶ’ knows)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) love
вҖў 'Love' = plural/base form. 'Neither' = singular вҶ’ 'loves' (singular) аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ.
вҖў 'Neither of them love me' = grammatically incorrect (plural verb with singular subject).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Them' plural вҶ’ 'love' (plural verb) feel. But subject = 'neither' (singular) вҶ’ 'loves'.

вқҢ (B) are loving
вҖў 'Are loving' = present continuous plural. Double error: (1) 'are' = plural auxiliary; (2) 'love' = state verb, not used in continuous form.
вҖў 'Love' = stative verb (love, like, hate, know) вҖ” never used in '-ing' continuous form!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Are loving' = active/continuous feel вҶ’ seems dynamic. But 'love' = state (not action) вҶ’ no continuous form.

вқҢ (C) has love
вҖў 'Has love' = wrong verb phrase. 'Has' = auxiliary for Present Perfect (has + V3). 'Has love' is not a verb form вҖ” 'love' вү  past participle.
вҖў 'Loves' (simple present) = correct; 'has loved' (present perfect) = possible; but 'has love' = neither.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Has' = singular feel вҶ’ correct for 'neither'. But 'has + love' (noun) = not a valid verb structure.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'NEITHER = ONE of two things вҖ” ONE is singular! Neither of them LOVES (like He loves)!' вҖ” Neither = picking one from two = singular. He loves, She loves, Neither loves!
вҖў State verbs вҖ” NEVER in continuous (important for PSC):
  - love, like, hate, know, believe, understand, want, need вҶ’ never 'is loving, are loving'
  - Always: loves, knows, believes (simple present)
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Neither of them + blank' вҶ’ choose singular verb with 's' = 'loves'. 'love' (plural) = eliminate. 'are loving' (continuous state verb) = eliminate. 'has love' (wrong structure) = eliminate. 'loves' = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change into indirect speech:
"Alas! My uncle met with an accident", he said.
optionA: My uncle Alas had met with an accident
optionB: He cried out that his uncle had met with an accident
optionC: He told his uncle that he had met with an accident
optionD: He cried that his uncle Alas met with an accident

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) He cried out that his uncle had met with an accident

вҖў 'Alas!' = exclamation of grief/sorrow вҶ’ indirect speech reporting verb = 'cried out' вң“
вҖў 'My' вҶ’ 'his' (pronoun change: first person вҶ’ third person) вң“
вҖў 'Met' (Simple Past) вҶ’ 'had met' (Past Perfect вҖ” backshift) вң“
вҖў 'Alas' = interjection вҖ” NOT included in indirect speech (only meaning/emotion preserved through 'cried out') вң“
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ…аҙөаө» аҙөаҙҝаҙІаҙӘаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ, аҙӨаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ…аҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙҫаҙөаҙЁаөҚ аҙ…аҙӘаҙ•аҙҹаҙӮ аҙӘаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҝаҙҜаөҶаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: Exclamatory sentences вҶ’ indirect speech: said вҶ’ cried out/exclaimed. Interjection removed. Tense backshift.
вҖў Exclamatory indirect speech rules:
  1. Reporting verb: 'said' вҶ’ 'cried out' / 'exclaimed' / 'shouted' (based on emotion)
  2. Interjections (Alas!, Hurrah!, Oh!, Wow!) вҶ’ removed from indirect speech
  3. Pronoun change: 'My' вҶ’ 'his/her' (based on context)
  4. Tense backshift: Simple Past ('met') вҶ’ Past Perfect ('had met')
вҖў Exclamation types and reporting verbs:
  - Alas! (grief) вҶ’ cried out, exclaimed sorrowfully
  - Hurrah! (joy) вҶ’ cried out, exclaimed joyfully
  - Oh! / Wow! вҶ’ exclaimed
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Exclamatory sentences вҶ’ indirect speech = PSC-аөҪ regular!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. 'Alas! I have lost my purse' вҶ’ She cried out that she had lost her purse.
  2. 'Hurrah! We won!' вҶ’ They exclaimed joyfully that they had won.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) My uncle Alas had met with an accident
вҖў 'Alas' = interjection вҖ” indirect speech-аөҪ 'Alas' word include аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ. Plus no reporting verb/subject ('he said that' missing entirely).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Alas' word keep аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҚ tense backshift ('had met') = partial correct feel. But interjection must be removed, reporting verb must be added.

вқҢ (C) He told his uncle that he had met with an accident
вҖў 'Told his uncle' = wrong вҖ” the sentence says 'My uncle met an accident' (uncle is the victim), not that he told his uncle something.
вҖў Meaning distorted: (C) implies he spoke TO his uncle. But original = he spoke ABOUT his uncle.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Told his uncle' = smooth sentence. But 'told his uncle' = uncle аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ listener. Original = uncle = accident victim. Meaning completely changed!

вқҢ (D) He cried that his uncle Alas met with an accident
вҖў Two errors: (1) 'Alas' still included (interjection must be removed); (2) 'met' = no tense backshift (should be 'had met').
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Cried' as reporting verb = correct direction. But 'Alas' kept + no backshift = incomplete transformation.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ALAS = SADNESS вҶ’ CRIED OUT! Indirect speech = throw away Alas, use cried out instead!' вҖ” Interjection (Alas/Hurrah) = trash can (throwaway) in indirect speech. Replace with emotional reporting verb.
вҖў 3-step exclamatory transformation:
  1. Remove interjection (Alas вҶ’ gone)
  2. Change reporting verb (said вҶ’ cried out)
  3. Backshift tense (met вҶ’ had met) + pronoun (my вҶ’ his)
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Alas!' indirect speech вҶ’ option with 'cried out' + no 'Alas' + tense backshift = correct. Eliminate options keeping 'Alas' word or wrong meaning. (B) = only correct. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change the following sentence into superlative degree:
Light travels faster than anything else.
optionA: Anything else travels faster than light in the world.
optionB: Anything else travels fastest than light in the world.
optionC: Of all things in the world, light travels the fastest.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Of all things in the world, light travels the fastest.

вҖў 'Faster than anything else' (comparative) вҶ’ 'the fastest of all things' (superlative) вң“
вҖў Superlative form: fast вҶ’ faster вҶ’ the fastest вң“
вҖў 'Of all things in the world' = superlative context (comparing with EVERYTHING) вң“
вҖў Structure: 'Of all + noun, subject + verb + the + superlative' вң“
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙІаөӢаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҶ аҙҺаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙіаҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ, аҙөаөҶаҙіаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙӮ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаөҮаҙ—аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙёаҙһаөҚаҙҡаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: Comparative 'faster than anything else' вҶ’ Superlative 'the fastest of all'
вҖў Degree transformation table for 'fast':
  - Positive: Light travels fast.
  - Comparative: Light travels faster than anything else.
  - Superlative: Of all things, light travels the fastest. вң“
вҖў Superlative structure patterns:
  - 'Of all + noun, subject + the + superlative'
  - 'Subject + the + superlative + of all'
  - 'Subject + the + superlative + noun'
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Comparative вҶ’ Superlative transformation = PSC-аөҪ class question!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She is taller than anyone else вҶ’ She is the tallest of all.
  2. He runs faster than all others вҶ’ He runs the fastest of all.
  3. Gold is more expensive than any other metal вҶ’ Gold is the most expensive metal.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Anything else travels faster than light in the world.
вҖў This reverses the meaning вҖ” 'anything else' travels faster than light = wrong! Light is the fastest, not 'anything else'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Sentence restructured with 'anything else' as subject = sounds like a transformation. But meaning flipped вҖ” light is fastest, not other things.

вқҢ (B) Anything else travels fastest than light in the world.
вҖў Double error: (1) meaning reversed (same as A); (2) 'fastest than' = wrong вҖ” superlative never uses 'than' ('the fastest OF all' / 'faster THAN' вҖ” never mix).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Fastest' = superlative word = correct degree. But 'fastest than' = impossible combination + meaning reversed.

вқҢ (D) Nothing is faster than light.
вҖў This is a different form (positive degree using negative: 'nothing is faster than') вҖ” not a superlative transformation.
вҖў 'Nothing is faster' = valid expression of the idea but NOT the superlative degree form requested.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Nothing is faster than light' = logically correct and familiar. But question asks for SUPERLATIVE form specifically вҶ’ 'the fastest' structure needed.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'Faster than anything else вҶ’ THE FASTEST of all! Remove anything else, add THE FASTEST and OF ALL!' вҖ” 'Anything else' comparison = comparative. 'Of all things' = superlative. Swap them!
вҖў Transformation shortcut: 'faster than anything/anyone else' вҶ’ 'the fastest of all'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Comparative вҶ’ superlative question вҶ’ find option with 'the + adjective-est + of all' structure. Subject must remain light (fastest traveller). (C) = 'light travels the fastest' = correct subject + superlative. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct preposition to fill in the blank given below:
The burglar jumped __________ the compound wall.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) over

вҖў 'Jump over' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ obstacle аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ/аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ…аҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҒаҙұаҙӮ аҙӘаөӢаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҖ” wall-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ®аҙұаөҒаҙӯаҙҫаҙ—аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙҺаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ• вң“
вҖў 'The burglar jumped over the compound wall' = 'аҙ•аҙіаөҚаҙіаө» compound wall аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.' вң“
вҖў 'Over' = from one side to the other side, clearing the obstacle.
вҖў Physical action: burglar was on one side вҶ’ jumped вҶ’ landed on the other side = 'over' perfectly describes this motion.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ•аҙіаөҚаҙіаө» compound wall-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ®аөҒаҙ•аҙіаҙҝаҙІаөӮаҙҹаөҶ аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ аҙ…аҙӨаҙҝаөј аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: JUMP OVER = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ obstacle аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӘаөӢаҙ•аөҒаҙ• (clear from one side to other)
вҖў Movement prepositions with 'jump':
  - jump OVER = аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• (wall/fence/hurdle вҖ” clear an obstacle)
  - jump INTO = аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ аҙ…аҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ•аҙҜаҙұаөҒаҙ• (jump into a pool)
  - jump OUT OF = аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ аҙӘаөҒаҙұаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙ• (jump out of a window)
  - jump AT = eagerly accept (jump at a chance = аҙ…аҙөаҙёаҙ°аҙӮ аҙҶаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаөӢаҙҹаөҶ аҙёаөҚаҙөаөҖаҙ•аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҖ” idiomatic)
  - jump ONTO = аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ аҙ•аҙҜаҙұаөҒаҙ• (jump onto the bus)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Movement prepositions (over/into/onto/out of) = PSC-аөҪ regular!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The horse jumped over the fence. (fence аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ)
  2. The child jumped into the water. (аҙөаөҶаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ)
  3. She jumped over the puddle. (water puddle аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҡаҙҫаҙҹаҙҝ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) at
вҖў 'Jump at' = idiomatic meaning вҖ” eagerly accept an opportunity ('jump at the chance'). Physical wall context-аөҪ 'jump at a wall' = wrong meaning.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'At' = location preposition feel вҶ’ wall location вҶ’ 'at the wall' feel. But 'jump at' = idiom (eagerness), not physical action.

вқҢ (B) in
вҖў 'Jump in' = enter something ('jump in the pool'). Wall = vertical barrier, not a container to enter. 'Jumped in the wall' = physically impossible meaning.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'In' = very common preposition вҶ’ familiar choice. But 'in' = inside/enclosed space, not crossing a barrier.

вқҢ (C) by
вҖў 'Jump by' = not a standard preposition phrase with 'jump'. 'By' = beside/method ('standing by the wall' вң“, but 'jump by' = no standard meaning).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'By the wall' = standing next to it feel. But 'jumped by the wall' = unclear/wrong meaning for crossing action.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'OVER = going ABOVE and BEYOND! Jump OVER = fly above the wall and land on the other side!' вҖ” Imagine the burglar jumping UP and OVER the wall like an arch вҖ” OVER!
вҖў Preposition picture: at = pointing to, in = inside, by = beside, over = above and crossing.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Jumped + wall/fence/hurdle' вҶ’ always 'over' (crossing obstacle). 'At' = eagerness idiom. 'In' = entering. 'By' = beside. Physical crossing = OVER. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Find out the meaning of the idiom 'tie yourself up in knots'.
optionB: make no progress in an argument
optionC: become very confused when you are trying to explain something

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) become very confused when you are trying to explain something

вҖў 'Tie yourself up in knots' = 'аҙ•аөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ аҙ•аөҒаҙҹаөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” confuse yourself so much that you can't explain clearly.
вҖў Visual image: rope-аөҪ knots (аҙ•аөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•аөҫ) аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаөҪ rope unravel аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” similarly, thoughts knot аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ explanation unravel аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Tie up in knots' = thoughts/words аҙ•аөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝ, explanation difficult аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙёаөҚаҙҘаҙҝаҙӨаҙҝ вң“
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ explain аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙ®аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөӢаҙұаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ confused аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙ…аҙөаҙёаөҚаҙҘ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: KNOTS = CONFUSION! Tie in knots = tangled thoughts = confused explanation!
вҖў Idioms with 'knot/tie' theme:
  - tie yourself up in knots = become confused explaining
  - tie the knot = marry (аҙөаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ№аҙӮ аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•)
  - at a rate of knots = very fast (аҙөаҙіаҙ°аөҶ аҙөаөҮаҙ—аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ)
  - a knotty problem = a very difficult problem (аҙ•аөҒаҙҙаҙһаөҚаҙһ problem)
вҖў Related 'confusion' idioms for PSC:
  - in a muddle = confused
  - at sea = confused/lost
  - go around in circles = make no progress
  - lose the thread = forget what you were saying
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Idiom meanings = PSC-аөҪ аҙҮаҙҹаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаөҶ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He tied himself up in knots trying to explain the rule. (explain аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөӢ confused)
  2. She gets tied up in knots whenever she speaks in public. (public speech = confused)
  3. Stop tying yourself up in knots вҖ” just say it simply! (advice to be clear)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) believes something good about someone
вҖў 'Believes something good' = positive belief about a person. 'Tie in knots' = confusion вҖ” nothing to do with belief or positive opinion.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Knots' = tight/strong вҶ’ strongly believe feel. But 'tie in knots' = tangled confusion, not firm belief.

вқҢ (B) make no progress in an argument
вҖў 'Make no progress' = stuck/stagnant. 'Tie in knots' specifically = confusion during EXPLANATION, not just lack of progress.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Being confused вҶ’ could mean making no progress. But the idiom specifically = confusion during explaining, not general stagnation.

вқҢ (D) trying to modify an opinion
вҖў 'Modify an opinion' = change one's view. No connection to 'tying knots' imagery.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Knots' = changing/adjusting feel? No logical connection. The image is tangled confusion, not opinion modification.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'Tie in KNOTS = your THOUGHTS get KNOTTED like tangled earphones! Can't straighten them out to explain clearly!' вҖ” Earphones/headphones аҙ•аөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙҜ image вҶ’ thoughts confused вҶ’ explanation confused = tied up in knots!
вҖў PSC idiom tip: Idiom meaning questions вҶ’ think about the visual image. Knots = tangled = confused. Which option matches 'confused while explaining'? = (C).
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Tie in knots' вҶ’ look for 'confused/confusing' option. Eliminate 'believe' (no connection), 'no progress' (partial), 'modify opinion' (no connection). (C) = confused explanation = exact match. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the following sentence using suitable articles:
On __________ way we saw __________ __one-eyed__ beggar.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) the, a

вҖў Blank 1: 'On THE way' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ specific journey/route refer аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ вҖ” definite article 'the' вң“
вҖў Blank 2: 'A one-eyed beggar' вҖ” 'one' word-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҫаҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ = /wКҢn/ = 'w' consonant sound вҶ’ 'a' (not 'an') вң“
вҖў 'On the way' = fixed phrase вҖ” the journey (specific) вҶ’ 'the'.
вҖў 'A one-eyed beggar' = first mention, indefinite + 'one' = /w/ sound вҶ’ 'a'.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙҜаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҹаҙҜаҙҝаөҪ аҙһаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ’аҙұаөҚаҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаө» аҙҜаҙҫаҙҡаҙ•аҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: THE = specific/known. A/AN = first mention/indefinite. A/AN = SOUND-based (not spelling)!
вҖў Blank 1 вҖ” 'the way':
  - 'On the way' = fixed expression for a specific journey вң“
  - 'On a way' = wrong (no such fixed expression)
вҖў Blank 2 вҖ” 'one-eyed':
  - 'One' = pronounced /wКҢn/ вҖ” starts with /w/ = consonant sound вҶ’ 'A'
  - NOT 'an one' вҖ” even though 'o' is a vowel letter, 'one' = /w/ consonant sound!
вҖў Tricky words starting with vowel letter but consonant sound вҶ’ 'A':
  - a one-eyed (wКҢn = /w/)
  - a university (ju: = /j/)
  - a unique (ju: = /j/)
  - a European (jКҠЙҷr = /j/)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'One-eyed / one + noun' article = PSC-аөҪ classic trick!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. On the way, we stopped for tea. (the way = specific journey)
  2. She met a one-armed soldier. (one = /w/ sound вҶ’ a)
  3. It was a one-time offer. (one = /w/ вҶ’ a)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) a, an
вҖў 'A way' = wrong ('on the way' = fixed expression needing 'the'). 'An one-eyed' = wrong ('one' = /w/ sound вҶ’ 'a'). Double error!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'An one-eyed' = 'one' starts with 'O' vowel letter вҶ’ 'an' feel. But SOUND = /w/ consonant вҶ’ 'a'.

вқҢ (C) the, an
вҖў 'The way' вң“ (correct!), but 'an one-eyed' вң— ('one' = /w/ consonant sound вҶ’ 'a', not 'an').
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Most common mistake вҖ” 'the' correct for blank 1, BUT 'an one' = wrong. 'One' = /wКҢn/ вҶ’ 'a'. This is the most tempting wrong answer!

вқҢ (D) a, a
вҖў 'A way' = wrong (should be 'the way' вҖ” specific journey). 'A one-eyed' = correct (a one = /w/ sound). One correct, one wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'A one-eyed' = correct! But 'a way' = wrong. 'On the way' is fixed phrase requiring 'the'.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'ONE sounds like WON (won a race) вҖ” W sound! So it's A one, not AN one! A one-eyed, a one-time, a one-way!' вҖ” 'One' = say it aloud: /wКҢn/ = starts with W вҶ’ A!
вҖў Two-blank article strategy:
  1. 'On ___ way' вҶ’ always 'the way' (fixed phrase) вҶ’ eliminate A and D
  2. '___ one-eyed' вҶ’ 'one' = /w/ sound вҶ’ 'a' вҶ’ eliminate C (an)
  3. Only (B) the, a = both correct!
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Two blanks вҶ’ eliminate wrong options step by step. 'The way' = fixed phrase вҶ’ eliminates A(a,an) and D(a,a). 'One' = /w/ sound вҶ’ 'a' вҶ’ eliminates C(the,an). (B) the, a = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
What is the expansion and meaning of the abbreviation given below?
AM

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Ante-Meridiem, Before Midday

вҖў 'AM' = Ante Meridiem (Latin) = 'Meridiem-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ' = Before Midday (аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ) вң“
вҖў Latin 'ante' = before (аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ); 'meridiem' = midday/noon (аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙЁаөҮаҙ°аҙӮ).
вҖў AM = 12:00 midnight to 12:00 noon (midnight аҙ®аөҒаҙӨаөҪ аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙөаҙ°аөҶ).
вҖў PM = Post Meridiem = After Midday (аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ = 12:00 noon to midnight).
вҖў Meaning: '9 AM = 9 o'clock in the morning = аҙ°аҙҫаҙөаҙҝаҙІаөҶ 9 аҙ®аҙЈаҙҝ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: AM = ANTE (before) + MERIDIEM (midday) = BEFORE MIDDAY. PM = POST (after) + MERIDIEM = AFTER MIDDAY!
вҖў Latin roots (PSC must-know):
  - Ante = before (аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ) вҶ’ AM = before noon
  - Post = after (аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ) вҶ’ PM = after noon
  - Meridiem = midday/noon (аҙүаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙЁаөҮаҙ°аҙӮ)
вҖў Time abbreviations for PSC:
  - AM = Ante Meridiem = Before Midday (12 midnight вҖ“ 12 noon)
  - PM = Post Meridiem = After Midday (12 noon вҖ“ 12 midnight)
  - GMT = Greenwich Mean Time
  - IST = Indian Standard Time
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Common abbreviations (AM/PM/GMT etc.) = PSC-аөҪ regular!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The exam starts at 10 AM. (10 in the morning)
  2. The meeting is at 3 PM. (3 in the afternoon)
  3. 12 AM = midnight; 12 PM = noon (аҙҮаҙө confuse аҙҶаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙёаҙҫаҙ§аөҚаҙҜаҙӨ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) After Midday, After Noon
вҖў 'After Midday' = PM-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning! AM = BEFORE midday, PM = AFTER midday. Complete confusion!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: AM/PM confuse аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ common. 'After Midday' = PM meaning. AM = Ante (before) = morning. Never 'After' with AM.

вқҢ (C) Ante-Meridiem, After Noon
вҖў Expansion correct: 'Ante-Meridiem' вң“. But meaning wrong: 'After Noon' = PM-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning. Ante = before, not after!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Ante-Meridiem' = looks impressive/correct вҶ’ choose this. But meaning 'After Noon' = contradicts 'ante' (before). Half right = fully wrong!

вқҢ (D) After Midday, Morning
вҖў Both wrong: expansion wrong (After Midday вү  Ante Meridiem) AND meaning partially confused.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Morning' = AM = morning time = correct feeling. But AM вү  'After Midday' expansion. 'Morning' alone doesn't fully define AM (AM = midnight to noon, not just morning).
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'A in AM = ANTE = AADHYAM (аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ)! A comes FIRST in alphabet = AM comes FIRST in the day (morning)! P in PM = POST = PINNALE (аҙӘаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаҙІаөҶ)! P comes after A = PM comes after AM!' вҖ” A before P in alphabet = AM before PM in the day!
вҖў Simple memory: AM = Morning time (after midnight, before noon). PM = Evening/Night (after noon, before midnight).
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'AM' expansion = Ante-Meridiem (not After). Meaning = Before Midday (not After). Only (B) has both correct: Ante-Meridiem + Before Midday. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Find out the Synonym for the word 'Feud'.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Quarrel

вҖў 'Feud' = 'аҙҰаөҖаөјаҙҳаҙ•аҙҫаҙІ аҙөаөҲаҙ°аҙӮ, аҙ•аөҒаҙҹаөҒаҙӮаҙ¬ аҙ¶аҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҒаҙӨ, bitter conflict' вҖ” a long-lasting angry dispute, especially between families/groups.
вҖў 'Quarrel' = 'аҙөаҙҙаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ, аҙӨаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӮ, conflict' = synonym of feud вң“ (both = angry dispute)
вҖў 'Feud' = usually longer/more bitter than a simple quarrel вҖ” family feuds can last generations.
вҖў Word root: Old French 'faide' = hostility (аҙ¶аҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҒаҙӨ).
вҖў Meaning: 'Feud = аҙҰаөҖаөјаҙҳаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ аҙ•аҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙөаөҲаҙ°аҙӮ/аҙөаҙҙаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ, especially between families.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: FEUD = long-lasting QUARREL/CONFLICT вҖ” family/group enmity over time!
вҖў Word family of 'feud':
  - feud (noun) = long bitter dispute (аҙҰаөҖаөјаҙҳаҙ•аҙҫаҙІ аҙ¶аҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҒаҙӨ)
  - feudal (adjective) = relating to feudalism (feudal system)
  - feuding (verb/adjective) = engaged in a feud (feuding families)
вҖў Synonyms of feud: quarrel, conflict, dispute, enmity, rivalry, vendetta, hostility
вҖў Antonyms of feud: friendship, harmony, peace, accord, goodwill
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Vocabulary synonyms with misleading options = PSC classic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The two families had a feud for decades. (decades = long quarrel)
  2. Their feud over land finally ended. (property dispute = feud)
  3. The celebrity feud made news. (public conflict = feud)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Friendship
вҖў 'Friendship' = 'аҙёаө—аҙ№аөғаҙҰаҙӮ' вҖ” exact ANTONYM of feud. Feud = enmity; friendship = opposite.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Feud' = sounds like a relationship word (like 'friend' pattern?) вҶ’ 'friendship' feel. But 'feud' = hostility, 'friendship' = harmony. Completely opposite!

вқҢ (B) Father-in-law
вҖў 'Father-in-law' = 'аҙ…аҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаө»' вҖ” a family relationship, not an English vocabulary synonym. Completely unrelated.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Feud' sometimes associated with families ('family feud') вҶ’ family relation 'father-in-law' = feel related. But feud = quarrel/conflict, not a family member title.

вқҢ (D) Ignorant
вҖў 'Ignorant' = 'аҙ…аҙңаөҚаҙһаө», аҙ…аҙұаҙҝаҙөаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ' вҖ” completely unrelated to feud. Feud = conflict, ignorant = lack of knowledge.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Ignorant' = negative word вҶ’ feud also negative вҶ’ both negative = seem synonymous. But negative вү  same meaning. Conflict вү  ignorance.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'FEUD = FOOD FIGHT! Family FEUD = long family food/quarrel fight! FEUD rhymes with RUDE вҖ” rude people quarrel!' вҖ” Family Feud (TV show!) = famous family quarrel show вҶ’ FEUD = quarrel/conflict!
вҖў Quick synonym pairs to remember:
  - Feud = Quarrel/Conflict/Vendetta
  - Amity = Friendship (amity = feud-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ antonym)
  - Enmity = Hostility/Hatred
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Feud' synonym вҶ’ eliminate opposites first. 'Friendship' (A) = antonym вҶ’ eliminate. 'Father-in-law' (B) = unrelated person вҶ’ eliminate. 'Ignorant' (D) = different category вҶ’ eliminate. 'Quarrel' (C) = conflict = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag.
He has left his home, __________

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) hasn't he?

вҖў 'He has left his home' = Positive statement вҶ’ Negative question tag вң“
вҖў Auxiliary verb: 'has' вҶ’ tag auxiliary = 'hasn't' (negative of 'has') вң“
вҖў Pronoun: 'He' вҶ’ tag pronoun = 'he' вң“
вҖў Positive statement вҶ’ Negative tag rule: 'has' вҶ’ 'hasn't he?' вң“
вҖў 'He has left his home, hasn't he?' = 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙөаөҖаҙҹаөҚ аҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӘаөӢаҙҜаҙҝ, аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ?'
вҖў Meaning: Positive sentence-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ confirmation аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ negative tag.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: POSITIVE statement вҶ’ NEGATIVE tag. NEGATIVE statement вҶ’ POSITIVE tag.
вҖў Question tag construction steps:
  1. Identify the auxiliary: 'has' (present perfect)
  2. Make it negative: 'hasn't'
  3. Identify the subject pronoun: 'he'
  4. Tag = 'hasn't he?'
вҖў Present Perfect question tags:
  - He has gone вҶ’ hasn't he?
  - She has finished вҶ’ hasn't she?
  - They have arrived вҶ’ haven't they?
  - We have tried вҶ’ haven't we?
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Question tags with has/have = PSC-аөҪ classic!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She has eaten, hasn't she? (has вҶ’ hasn't)
  2. They have left, haven't they? (have вҶ’ haven't)
  3. He has won, hasn't he? (has вҶ’ hasn't)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) have he?
вҖў 'Have he' = wrong вҖ” two errors: (1) 'have' = plural form; subject 'he' = singular вҶ’ 'has/hasn't'; (2) positive tag for positive statement = wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Have' = familiar word, 'he' = correct pronoun вҶ’ looks plausible. But 'he' = singular вҶ’ 'has/hasn't', not 'have'. Plus positive sentence вҶ’ negative tag.

вқҢ (B) haven't he?
вҖў 'Haven't he' = wrong auxiliary. 'He' = singular вҶ’ needs 'hasn't' (not 'haven't'). 'Haven't' = used with 'they/we/you/I'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Negative tag вң“ (correct direction), 'he' pronoun вң“. But 'haven't he' = wrong вҖ” singular 'he' needs 'hasn't'. 'Haven't they' вң“ but 'haven't he' вң—.

вқҢ (C) is he?
вҖў 'Is he?' = wrong auxiliary entirely. Main verb = 'has left' (Present Perfect with 'has'). Tag must use SAME auxiliary вҶ’ 'hasn't he?', not 'is he?'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Is he?' = familiar, simple question tag feel. But auxiliary mismatch вҖ” 'has' вү  'is'. Tag must mirror the main auxiliary.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'HE HAS вҶ’ HE HASN'T! Positive to Negative, same verb, same pronoun вҖ” just add NOT!' вҖ” He has (positive) вҶ’ hasn't he (negative tag). Mirror the verb, flip positive/negative!
вҖў Singular vs Plural with have:
  - He/She/It + HAS вҶ’ HASN'T he/she/it?
  - I/We/You/They + HAVE вҶ’ HAVEN'T I/we/you/they?
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'He has left' вҶ’ Step 1: positive вҶ’ negative tag. Step 2: auxiliary = 'has' вҶ’ 'hasn't'. Step 3: pronoun = 'he'. Answer = 'hasn't he?' = (D). Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct one word substitute for the underlined words in the given sentence.
She teaches the art of producing __beautiful handwriting__.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Calligraphy

вҖў 'Calligraphy' = 'аҙёаөҒаҙІаөҮаҙ–аҙЁаҙӮ, beautiful handwriting-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ•аҙІ' вҖ” the art of producing beautiful, decorative handwriting вң“
вҖў Word root: Greek 'kallos' = beauty (аҙёаө—аҙЁаөҚаҙҰаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ) + 'graphia' = writing (аҙҺаҙҙаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ) вҶ’ beautiful writing = Calligraphy вң“
вҖў 'She teaches calligraphy' = 'аҙ…аҙөаөҫ аҙёаөҒаҙІаөҮаҙ–аҙЁ аҙ•аҙІ аҙӘаҙ аҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
вҖў Calligraphers use special pens/brushes to create artistic, decorative text.
вҖў Meaning: 'Calligraphy = аҙ®аҙЁаөӢаҙ№аҙ°аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ аҙ•аөҲаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙ° аҙ•аҙІ вҖ” artistic handwriting.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: CALLI (beautiful) + GRAPHY (writing) = CALLIGRAPHY = beautiful writing!
вҖў '-graphy' suffix = writing/recording (PSC must-know):
  - Calligraphy = beautiful handwriting (calli = beauty)
  - Choreography = art of dance composition (choreo = dance)
  - Demography = study of human populations (demo = people)
  - Photography = writing with light (photo = light)
  - Biography = life writing (bio = life)
  - Geography = earth writing (geo = earth)
  - Stenography = shorthand writing (steno = narrow)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: '-graphy' word meanings = PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Islamic calligraphy is considered an art form. (Arabic beautiful writing)
  2. The calligrapher wrote the wedding invitations. (handwriting artist)
  3. She learned calligraphy to improve her handwriting. (beautiful writing art)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Choreography
вҖў 'Choreography' = 'аҙЁаөғаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙёаҙӮаҙөаҙҝаҙ§аҙҫаҙЁаҙӮ' вҖ” the art of composing/arranging dance sequences. Nothing to do with handwriting.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Both 'calligraphy' and 'choreography' end in '-graphy' вҶ’ seem similar. But 'choreo' = dance, 'calli' = beauty/writing. Dance вү  handwriting.

вқҢ (C) Demography
вҖў 'Demography' = 'аҙңаҙЁаҙёаҙӮаҙ–аөҚаҙҜаҙҫ аҙ¶аҙҫаҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ' вҖ” the study of human populations (birth rate, death rate, etc.). Unrelated to handwriting.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Demography' also ends in '-graphy' вҶ’ scientific study feel. But 'demo' = people/population, not writing art.

вқҢ (D) None of the above
вҖў (A) Calligraphy = exact correct answer. 'None of the above' is wrong here.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: When unsure, 'None of the above' feels safe. But Calligraphy = precisely defined as 'the art of beautiful handwriting'. Never choose 'none' when a perfect match exists.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'CALLI sounds like KALLI (аҙ•аҙіаөҚаҙіаҙҝ вҖ” sharp/beautiful in feel) + GRAPHY = WRITING. BEAUTIFUL WRITING = CALLIGRAPHY!' вҖ” 'Calli' = beautiful. Graphy = writing. Calli + graphy = beautiful writing!
вҖў '-graphy' family cheat sheet:
  - CALLI + graphy = Beautiful writing рҹ–ҠпёҸ
  - CHOREO + graphy = Dance composition рҹ’ғ
  - DEMO + graphy = Population study рҹ‘Ҙ
  - PHOTO + graphy = Light/pictures рҹ“·
  - BIO + graphy = Life story рҹ“–
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Art of beautiful handwriting' вҶ’ 'Calli' (beautiful) + 'graphy' (writing) = Calligraphy. Eliminate dance (choreo) and population (demo). Guaranteed mark!

questionText: What is the plural of 'Daughter-in-law'?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Daughters in-law

вҖў Compound noun 'Daughter-in-law' вҶ’ plural = main noun 'daughter' gets the plural вҶ’ 'Daughters-in-law' вң“
вҖў Rule: Compound nouns-аөҪ MAIN NOUN pluralize аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•, not the last word.
вҖў 'Daughter' = main noun in 'daughter-in-law' вҶ’ 'Daughters-in-law' вң“
вҖў 'In-law' = describes the relationship (not the main noun) вҶ’ unchanged.
вҖў Meaning: 'Daughter-in-law-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ plural = Daughters-in-law (аҙ®аҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҫ вҖ” аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаҙ§аҙҝаҙ•аҙӮ).'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: Compound nouns вҶ’ MAIN NOUN gets plural (usually first or most important noun)
вҖў '-in-law' compound noun plurals:
  - Daughter-in-law вҶ’ Daughters-in-law вң“
  - Son-in-law вҶ’ Sons-in-law вң“
  - Mother-in-law вҶ’ Mothers-in-law вң“
  - Father-in-law вҶ’ Fathers-in-law вң“
  - Brother-in-law вҶ’ Brothers-in-law вң“
  - Sister-in-law вҶ’ Sisters-in-law вң“
вҖў Other compound noun plurals:
  - Passer-by вҶ’ Passers-by
  - Commander-in-chief вҶ’ Commanders-in-chief
  - Man-of-war вҶ’ Men-of-war
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Compound noun plurals = PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Both my daughters-in-law are doctors. (two marumakkal)
  2. His brothers-in-law visited yesterday. (plural brothers-in-law)
  3. All the mothers-in-law attended the function.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Daughter in-laws
вҖў 'In-laws' = pluralizing 'law' вҖ” wrong. 'Law' is not the main noun. Main noun = 'daughter' вҶ’ 'daughters'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Most natural English-speaker mistake! 'In-laws' (my in-laws) = common informal expression. But formal plural = 'daughters-in-law', not 'daughter in-laws'.

вқҢ (C) Daughter in-law
вҖў This is the singular form, not plural. No change made.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Daughter in-law' = question itself. No plural formed = clearly wrong.

вқҢ (D) None of the above
вҖў (B) Daughters in-law = correct plural. 'None of the above' is wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Compound plurals seem tricky вҶ’ 'none' feels safe. But 'Daughters-in-law' is the standard accepted plural in English grammar.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'In compound words, the MAIN CHARACTER gets the S! Daughter is the MAIN person вҖ” so DAUGHTERS-in-law!' вҖ” Daughter = main person (protagonist). 'In-law' = supporting role. Main character gets the plural 'S'!
вҖў All -in-law plurals follow same pattern: SonвҶ’Sons, MotherвҶ’Mothers, FatherвҶ’Fathers, BrotherвҶ’Brothers, SisterвҶ’Sisters + '-in-law'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: '-in-law' compound вҶ’ pluralize the FIRST WORD (the family member). 'Daughter' вҶ’ 'Daughters'. 'In-laws' alone = informal, not the compound plural. (B) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which of the following is correctly spelt?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Transient

вҖў 'Transient' = 'аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙЈаҙҝаҙ•, аҙӨаҙҫаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙҝаҙ•, not permanent' вҖ” lasting only for a short time вң“
вҖў Correct spelling: T-R-A-N-S-I-E-N-T вң“
вҖў Word breakdown: 'trans' (across/through) + 'ient' (going) = passing through = temporary.
вҖў Common usage: 'transient worker' (temporary worker), 'transient pain' (temporary pain).
вҖў Meaning: 'Transient = аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙЈаҙЁаөҮаҙ°аҙӮ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ аҙЁаҙҝаҙІаҙЁаҙҝаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ, аҙӨаҙҫаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: TRANS + IENT = TRANSIENT. 'Trans' prefix = across (no 'c' after trans here!)
вҖў Spelling analysis of 'transient':
  - Prefix: 'trans-' = across/through (transport, transfer, transform)
  - Root: '-ient' = going/passing (like 'ient' in patient, ancient)
  - Full: trans + ient = transient вң“
  - NO 'c' between trans and ient!
вҖў Common spelling errors:
  - вң— Transcient вҖ” extra 'c' inserted
  - вң— Trensient вҖ” 'a' changed to 'e' in 'trans'
  - вң— Trenscient вҖ” both errors combined
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Spelling of 'trans-' prefix words = PSC frequent!
вҖў Word family:
  - transient (adjective) = temporary, short-lived
  - transiently (adverb) = temporarily
  - transience (noun) = the state of being transient
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Transcient
вҖў Extra 'c' added: 'Trans-C-ient' = wrong. 'Trans' prefix directly joins 'ient' вҖ” no 'c' in between.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Transient' sounds like there could be a 'c' вҖ” 'tran-shent' pronunciation вҶ’ 'c' feel. But spelling = T-R-A-N-S-I-E-N-T, no 'c'.

вқҢ (C) Trensient
вҖў 'Tren-' instead of 'Trans-' вҖ” vowel error: 'a' changed to 'e'. Prefix = 'trans' (not 'trens').
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Fast reading вҶ’ 'trans' and 'trens' look similar. But 'trans' = standard prefix (transport, transfer). Always 'A' in 'trans'.

вқҢ (D) Trenscient
вҖў Combines both errors: 'tren-' (aвҶ’e) + 'sc' (extra c). Double error.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Longest/most complex-looking вҶ’ might seem 'complete'. But most errors = most wrong.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'TRANS = TRANSPORT, TRANSFER, TRANSFORM вҖ” always T-R-A-N-S! TRANSIENT = TRANS + IENT, no C, no E!' вҖ” If you know 'transport', you know 'TRANS'. Just add IENT: TRANS + IENT = TRANSIENT.
вҖў Prefix 'trans-' words (always spelled T-R-A-N-S):
  - Transport, Transfer, Transform, Translate, Transparent, Transient
  - All start with T-R-A-N-S вҖ” never 'trens' or 'transc'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Trans-' prefix вҶ’ 'A' after 'TR' always (not 'E'). No extra 'C' after 'trans'. Only (A) Transient = correct. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blanks.
Those spectacles __________ of no use to me.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) are

вҖў 'Spectacles' = always plural noun (аҙ’аҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ 'spectacle' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ lens вҖ” 'spectacles' = a pair = plural) вҶ’ plural verb 'are' вң“
вҖў 'Those spectacles are of no use to me' = 'аҙҶ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаҙҹ аҙҺаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙүаҙӘаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.'
вҖў 'Spectacles' like 'trousers/scissors/glasses' = always plural in English вҶ’ always plural verb.
вҖў 'Those' = plural determiner вҶ’ confirms plural verb.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙҶ spectacles (аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаҙҹ) аҙҺаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ—аөҒаҙЈаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: ALWAYS PLURAL nouns вҶ’ always plural verb (are/were/have)!
вҖў 'Always Plural' nouns (PSC must-know list):
  - Spectacles/Glasses вҶ’ are (аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙЈаҙҹ)
  - Trousers/Pants вҶ’ are (аҙӘаҙҫаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҚ)
  - Scissors вҶ’ are (аҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•)
  - Tongs вҶ’ are (аҙ…аҙҹаөҒаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҚ аҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҝ)
  - Pliers вҶ’ are (pliers)
  - Binoculars вҶ’ are (аҙҰаөӮаҙ°аҙҰаөјаҙ¶аҙҝаҙЁаҙҝ)
  - Pyjamas вҶ’ are (pyjamas)
  - Shorts вҶ’ are (shorts)
вҖў These nouns: (1) always plural, (2) no singular form in common use, (3) always take plural verb.
вҖў 'A pair of spectacles IS' = singular (pair = one unit). But 'spectacles ARE' = plural.
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Always-plural nouns + verb agreement = PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. My glasses are broken. (glasses = plural вҶ’ are)
  2. The scissors are on the table. (scissors = plural вҶ’ are)
  3. Those trousers are too long. (trousers = plural вҶ’ are)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) is
вҖў 'Is' = singular verb. 'Spectacles' = always plural вҶ’ 'are', never 'is'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Spectacles' = one object (pair of glasses = one thing) вҶ’ 'is' singular feel. But grammar treats 'spectacles' as plural always вҶ’ 'are'.

вқҢ (C) am
вҖў 'Am' = only for first person singular 'I'. Subject = 'spectacles' (third person plural) вҶ’ 'am' impossible.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Am' is a form of 'be' вҶ’ verb feel вҶ’ could fit. But 'am' = only 'I am'. Never for 'spectacles'.

вқҢ (D) none of the above
вҖў (B) 'are' is correct. 'None of the above' is wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Is' feels right (one pair = one thing) вҶ’ if unsure вҶ’ 'none'. But 'are' is the standard grammar rule.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'SPECTACLES = TWO LENSES = TWO = PLURAL! Two lenses = ARE, not IS! Glasses, Scissors, Trousers вҖ” all come in PAIRS = always plural!' вҖ” Two lenses in spectacles = pair = plural = ARE!
вҖў Always-plural chant: 'Spectacles ARE, Scissors ARE, Trousers ARE, Glasses ARE вҖ” pairs ARE always plural!'
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Spectacles/Glasses/Scissors/Trousers' + blank вҶ’ always choose 'are'. Never 'is' or 'am'. (B) are = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: The meaning of 'carpe diem' is

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) enjoy the present day
