вҖў 'Carpe diem' = Latin phrase. 'Carpe' = 'аҙ•аөҠаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' (seize/pluck). 'Diem' = 'аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ' (day). аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ = 'аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙ•аөҠаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' = Seize the day.
вҖў аҙҲ phrase-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ core message: 'аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙӘаөӮаөјаҙЈаөҚаҙЈаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙңаөҖаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҖ” аҙЁаҙҫаҙіаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ• аҙөаөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹ, аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөӢаҙ·аҙӮ аҙ…аҙЁаөҒаҙӯаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•.'
вҖў Roman poet Horace-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙёаҙҝаҙҰаөҚаҙ§ аҙөаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аҙіаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӯаҙөаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡ аҙҲ phrase аҙІаөӢаҙ•аҙ®аөҶаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаҙҫаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў Meaning: 'Carpe diem = аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙӮ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҖ” аҙӯаөӮаҙӨаҙ•аҙҫаҙІаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ аҙ–аөҮаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹ, аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝаҙҜаөҶ аҙӯаҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹ.'
вҖў аҙҡаөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӮ: 'аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙңаөҖаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ carpe diem-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: CARPE = SEIZE (аҙ•аөҠаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ). DIEM = DAY (аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ). CARPE DIEM = SEIZE THE DAY = аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•!
вҖў PSC-аөҪ аҙ…аҙұаҙҝаҙһаөҚаҙһаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹ Latin/Foreign phrases:
  - Carpe diem = аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• (enjoy the present day)
  - Bona fide = аҙёаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ, genuine (аҙҜаҙҘаҙҫаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙҘ)
  - Status quo = аҙЁаҙҝаҙІаҙөаҙҝаҙІаөҶ аҙ…аҙөаҙёаөҚаҙҘ (existing condition)
  - Per capita = аҙҶаҙіаөҠаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ (per person)
  - Alma mater = аҙӨаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙӘаҙ аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ…аҙөаҙёаҙ°аҙӮ аҙӨаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ school/college
  - Vice versa = аҙЁаөҮаҙ°аөҶ аҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒаҙӮ (the other way around)
  - Ad hoc = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙӨаөҚаҙҜаөҮаҙ• аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ (for this specific purpose)
  - Et cetera (etc.) = аҙ®аҙұаөҚаҙұаөҒаҙӮ, аҙҮаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙҰаҙҝ (and so on)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Latin/Foreign phrases = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ question guaranteed аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Live by the motto 'carpe diem' вҖ” аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ.
  2. Her carpe diem attitude made her enjoy every moment. (аҙ“аҙ°аөӢ аҙЁаҙҝаҙ®аҙҝаҙ·аҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ)
  3. Carpe diem вҖ” don't waste today worrying about tomorrow. (аҙЁаҙҫаҙіаөҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙөаөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҝ аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙЁаҙ·аөҚаҙҹаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) schedule
вҖў 'Schedule' = 'аҙёаҙ®аҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙ°аҙ®аҙӮ, аҙ·аөҶаҙЎаөҚаҙҜаөӮаөҫ' вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ plan/timetable. Carpe diem-аҙүаҙӮ schedule-аҙүаҙӮ аҙӨаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙҝаөҪ аҙҜаҙҫаҙӨаөҠаҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Schedule = future plan. Carpe diem = аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝаҙҜаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ аҙҶаҙІаөӢаҙҡаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӨаөҶ аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙңаөҖаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Diem' = day вҶ’ daily schedule feel вҶ’ 'schedule' аҙӨаөӢаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ carpe diem = schedule аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙІаөҚаҙІ, аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ.

вқҢ (B) carefully
вҖў 'Carefully' = 'аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҫаҙӘаөӮаөјаҙөаөҚаҙөаҙӮ' вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ adverb. Carpe diem-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning = careful-аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ• аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІ, аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҶ аҙҰаҙҝаҙЁаҙӮ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Latin words = serious/careful sounding вҶ’ 'carefully' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ carpe = seize (аҙҶаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаөӢаҙҹаөҶ аҙ•аөҠаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҚ), not carefully. Careful вү  seize.

вқҢ (C) first sight
вҖў 'First sight' = 'аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ аҙ•аҙҫаҙҙаөҚаҙҡаҙҜаҙҝаөҪ' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ 'love at first sight' phrase-аҙІаөҮ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаөӮ. Carpe diem-аҙүаҙӮ first sight-аҙүаҙӮ аҙӨаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙҝаөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Carpe' = unfamiliar sound вҶ’ random association. 'Diem' = day вҶ’ 'first day' вҶ’ 'first sight' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ carpe diem = enjoy present, not 'first sight'.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'CARPE DIEM = CARPET + DAY! Roll out the CARPET for TODAY вҖ” celebrate TODAY! Carpe = grab/seize, Diem = day. Grab today and enjoy it!' вҖ” Carpet аҙҡаөҒаҙ°аөҒаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙөаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• = Carpe diem!
вҖў Latin phrase PSC cheat sheet:
  - Carpe diem = аҙҮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• рҹҺү
  - Bona fide = genuine, authentic вң…
  - Status quo = current state рҹ“Ҡ
  - Per capita = per person рҹ‘Ө
  - Vice versa = opposite way рҹ”„
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Carpe diem' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ аҙүаҙҹаө» 'enjoy the present day' option choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•. 'Schedule/carefully/first sight' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҶ’ eliminate. (D) = guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the most appropriate passive voice for the given statement.
The postman has delivered the letter.
optionA: The letter is being delivered by the postman
optionB: The letter was being delivered by the postman
optionC: The letter has been delivered by the postman
optionD: The letter have been delivered by the postman

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) The letter has been delivered by the postman

вҖў Active voice: 'The postman HAS DELIVERED the letter' = Present Perfect tense.
вҖў Present Perfect Active вҶ’ Present Perfect Passive аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаөҚ:
  - Active: Subject + has/have + V3 (delivered)
  - Passive: Object + has/have + been + V3 (delivered) + by + Agent
вҖў 'The postman has delivered' вҶ’ 'The letter has been delivered by the postman' вң“
вҖў 'Has been delivered' = Present Perfect Passive formula вң“ ('letter' = singular вҶ’ 'has', not 'have')
вҖў Meaning: 'Postman аҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ deliver аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ (Active) вҶ’ аҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ postman-аҙҮаөҪ deliver аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ (Passive).'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: Present Perfect Passive = Object + HAS/HAVE + BEEN + V3 + by + Agent
вҖў Tense-wise Passive Voice formulae (PSC must-know):
  - Simple Present: is/am/are + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter IS delivered'
  - Present Continuous: is/am/are + being + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter IS BEING delivered'
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter WAS delivered'
  - Past Continuous: was/were + being + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter WAS BEING delivered'
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter HAS BEEN delivered' вң“
  - Past Perfect: had + been + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter HAD BEEN delivered'
  - Simple Future: will + be + V3 вҶ’ 'The letter WILL BE delivered'
вҖў Singular 'letter' вҶ’ 'has been' (not 'have been') вң“
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Active вҶ’ Passive tense matching = PSC-аөҪ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She has written the report вҶ’ The report has been written by her.
  2. He has cooked the meal вҶ’ The meal has been cooked by him.
  3. They have fixed the problem вҶ’ The problem has been fixed by them.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) The letter is being delivered by the postman
вҖў 'Is being delivered' = Present Continuous Passive. Active sentence = Present Perfect ('has delivered'), not Present Continuous. Tense match аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Present Continuous Passive = 'is/are + being + V3'. аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ use аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ tense = Present Perfect вҶ’ 'has been delivered' вң“
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Is being delivered' = active delivery happening feel вҶ’ logical context. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ tense wrong вҖ” Present Continuous вү  Present Perfect.

вқҢ (B) The letter was being delivered by the postman
вҖў 'Was being delivered' = Past Continuous Passive. Active = Present Perfect ('has delivered') вҶ’ Past Continuous аҙҶаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Tense аҙ’аҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ match аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Was being' = formal/past feel вҶ’ impressive sound. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'has delivered' = present perfect, past continuous-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ convert аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) The letter have been delivered by the postman
вҖў 'Have been delivered' = wrong вҖ” 'letter' = singular noun вҶ’ 'has been delivered' (not 'have'). 'Have' = plural (they/we/you). 'The letter' = singular вҶ’ 'has'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Have been delivered' = present perfect passive sound вң“ direction. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ singular 'letter' вҶ’ 'has been', not 'have been'. Subject-verb agreement error!
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'HAS DELIVERED вҶ’ HAS BEEN DELIVERED! Just add BEEN in between! Present Perfect-аөҪ BEEN аҙ•аөҠаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙ•, object subject-аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•!' вҖ” Active-аөҪ 'has delivered' вҶ’ Passive-аөҪ 'has BEEN delivered'. BEEN = passive-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ identity card!
вҖў Quick passive check: Active-аҙІаөҶ tense identify вҶ’ same tense passive formula apply:
  - has/have + V3 (active) вҶ’ has/have + been + V3 (passive)
  - 'letter' singular вҶ’ 'has been' (not 'have been')
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Present Perfect passive question вҶ’ 'has/have been + V3' option choose. Singular subject вҶ’ 'has'. 'Is being' = present continuous (wrong tense). 'Was being' = past continuous (wrong). 'Have been' = plural (letter is singular). (C) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: What is the antonym for 'kindle'?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (D) Extinguish

вҖў 'Kindle' = 'аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•, аҙңаөҚаҙөаҙІаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙүаҙЈаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to set fire to / to arouse feelings.
вҖў 'Extinguish' = 'аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•, аҙ…аҙЈаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to put out fire/light completely.
вҖў Kindle (аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•) вҶ” Extinguish (аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•) = exact antonyms вң“
вҖў Kindle = start/ignite вҶ’ Extinguish = stop/put out = opposite actions.
вҖў Meaning: 'Kindle = аҙҶаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, Extinguish = аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙіаҙҜаөҒаҙ• вҖ” аҙҮаҙө opposite meanings.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: KINDLE = ignite/start fire. EXTINGUISH = put out/stop fire. аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҪ вҶ” аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҪ = opposites!
вҖў Word family of 'kindle':
  - kindle (verb) = аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•, аҙҶаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•
  - rekindle (verb) = аҙөаөҖаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙңаөҚаҙөаҙІаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• (rekindle a friendship = аҙёаө—аҙ№аөғаҙҰаҙӮ аҙӘаөҒаҙӨаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•)
  - kindling (noun) = аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҫаө» аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙҡаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ
вҖў Word family of 'extinguish':
  - extinguish (verb) = аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•, аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•
  - extinguisher (noun) = fire extinguisher (аҙ…аҙ—аөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙ¶аҙ®аҙЁ аҙүаҙӘаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ)
  - extinction (noun) = аҙөаҙӮаҙ¶аҙЁаҙҫаҙ¶аҙӮ (species extinction)
вҖў Figurative uses:
  - Kindle hope = аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙӨаөҖаҙ•аөҚаҙ· аҙңаөҚаҙөаҙІаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ” Extinguish hope = аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙӨаөҖаҙ•аөҚаҙ· аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•
  - Kindle interest = аҙӨаҙҫаөҪаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙүаҙЈаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ• вҶ” Extinguish interest = аҙӨаҙҫаөҪаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Fire-related antonyms = PSC vocabulary section-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. He kindled the fire in the fireplace. (аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝ)
  2. Firefighters extinguished the blaze quickly. (аҙӨаөҖ аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝ)
  3. Her words kindled a fire in his heart. (аҙүаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙІаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙңаөҚаҙөаҙІаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Book
вҖў 'Book' = 'аҙӘаөҒаҙёаөҚаҙӨаҙ•аҙӮ' вҖ” kindle-аҙүаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Kindle' = Amazon-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ e-reader device-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙӘаөҮаҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ vocabulary-аөҪ 'kindle' = to ignite.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Amazon Kindle = e-book reader вҶ’ 'Book' = kindle device feel! аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'kindle' = verb (to ignite), 'book' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ antonym-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (B) Distinguish
вҖў 'Distinguish' = 'аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙёаҙӮ аҙ•аҙҫаҙЈаөҒаҙ•, аҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙұаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to recognize/identify differences. Kindle-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ antonym аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, fire-аҙүаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Distinguish' = 'distinguish' вҶ’ 'extinguish'-аҙүаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ sound similar feel! 'Dis-tinguish' vs 'Ex-tinguish' вҖ” аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙёаөҚаҙӨ words. 'Extinguish' = put out fire. 'Distinguish' = tell apart.

вқҢ (C) Desolate
вҖў 'Desolate' = 'аҙүаҙӘаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹ, аҙҸаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ, barren' вҖ” a place that is empty/abandoned. Kindle-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ antonym аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Desolate' = negative/dark word вҶ’ kindle-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ bright/fire meaning-аҙЁаөҚ opposite feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ desolate = emptiness/abandonment, not 'putting out fire'.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'KINDLE = CANDLE lit! EXTINGUISH = EXIT the light! Candle KINDLE вҶ’ candle EXIT = Extinguish!' вҖ” Kindle = candle аҙҶаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ. Extinguish = candle EXIT аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ (аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ). Candle ON вҶ” Candle OFF!
вҖў Fire antonym pair: Kindle (аҙ•аөҠаҙіаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҪ) вҶ” Extinguish (аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҪ). аҙҲ pair аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•!
вҖў Watch out: 'Distinguish' вү  'Extinguish'. 'Dis-' = different. 'Ex-' = out/away. Fire аҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҪ = Extinguish.
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Kindle' = to light fire. Antonym = to put out fire = 'Extinguish'. 'Book' (unrelated), 'Distinguish' (identify), 'Desolate' (abandoned) = all eliminate. (D) Extinguish = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the most appropriate Indirect speech for the given sentence.
"The singer is leaving for England tomorrow", said the PA.
optionA: The PA told that the singer was leaving for England the next day
optionB: The PA told that the singer was leaving for England tomorrow
optionC: The PA told that the singer is leaving for England tomorrow
optionD: The PA told that the singer has leaving for England the next day

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) The PA told that the singer was leaving for England the next day

вҖў Indirect speech-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ®аҙҫаҙұаөҚаҙұаөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ changes аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ:
  1. Tense backshift: 'is leaving' (Present Continuous) вҶ’ 'was leaving' (Past Continuous) вң“
  2. Time expression: 'tomorrow' вҶ’ 'the next day' вң“ (Indirect speech-аөҪ time words аҙ®аҙҫаҙұаөҒаҙӮ)
вҖў 'Said' = past tense reporting verb вҶ’ аҙүаөҫаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ tense backshift аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў 'The singer is leaving' вҶ’ 'the singer was leaving' (аҙҶ аҙёаҙ®аҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙӘаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, past reference).
вҖў Meaning: 'PA аҙӘаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒ вҖ” singer аҙ…аҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ England-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙӘаөӢаҙ•аөҒаҙ•аҙҜаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: Direct вҶ’ Indirect:
  (1) Tense: IS LEAVING вҶ’ WAS LEAVING (present вҶ’ past backshift)
  (2) Time: TOMORROW вҶ’ THE NEXT DAY
вҖў Tense backshift table (PSC must-know):
  - is/am/are вҶ’ was/were
  - is doing/am doing вҶ’ was doing вң“ (аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'is leaving' вҶ’ 'was leaving')
  - has/have done вҶ’ had done
  - will вҶ’ would
  - can вҶ’ could
  - may вҶ’ might
вҖў Time expression changes in Indirect speech:
  - tomorrow вҶ’ the next day / the following day вң“
  - today вҶ’ that day
  - yesterday вҶ’ the day before
  - now вҶ’ then
  - here вҶ’ there
  - this вҶ’ that
  - these вҶ’ those
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Indirect speech tense backshift + time change = PSC-аөҪ class question!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. 'I am going tomorrow' вҶ’ He said he was going the next day.
  2. 'She is singing now' вҶ’ He said she was singing then.
  3. 'We will come tomorrow' вҶ’ They said they would come the next day.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) ...was leaving for England tomorrow
вҖў Tense backshift вң“ ('was leaving' = correct). аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'tomorrow' = unchanged вҖ” wrong! Indirect speech-аөҪ 'tomorrow' вҶ’ 'the next day' аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Was leaving' correct аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙҜ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ 'tomorrow' аҙ…аҙӨаөҮ аҙӘаөӢаҙІаөҶ leave аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ вҖ” half right, half wrong. Time word change = mandatory in indirect speech!

вқҢ (C) ...the singer is leaving for England tomorrow
вҖў 'Is leaving' = present tense unchanged. 'Tomorrow' = unchanged. аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ wrong! Reporting verb 'said' = past вҶ’ аҙүаөҫаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ backshift аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Direct speech-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаөҚ аҙ…аҙӨаөҮ аҙӘаҙҹаҙҝ copy аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨ feel вҖ” аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ change аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Indirect speech-аөҪ tense-аҙүаҙӮ time-аҙүаҙӮ change mandatory.

вқҢ (D) ...the singer has leaving for England the next day
вҖў 'Has leaving' = grammatically impossible! 'Has' = auxiliary for Present Perfect, 'leaving' = present participle. 'Has leaving' = no such verb form exists in English.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Has' = backshift attempt feel (has вҶ’ Present Perfect feel). аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'has leaving' = completely wrong structure. 'Was leaving' (Past Continuous) = correct backshift.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'TOMORROW in INDIRECT = THE NEXT DAY! And IS вҶ’ WAS! Two changes, two marks safe!' вҖ” Indirect speech = two changes always: (1) tense goes back (isвҶ’was), (2) time word changes (tomorrowвҶ’next day). Both changes = full marks!
вҖў Time word cheat sheet:
  - tomorrow вҶ’ the next day рҹ“…
  - today вҶ’ that day
  - yesterday вҶ’ the day before
  - now вҶ’ then
  - here вҶ’ there
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Indirect speech question вҶ’ check TWO things: (1) tense backshift done? (is вҶ’ was вң“), (2) time word changed? (tomorrow вҶ’ next day вң“). Only (A) has BOTH correct. (B) time wrong, (C) nothing changed, (D) impossible verb. (A) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Choose the appropriate feminine form of 'Beau'.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) Belle

вҖў 'Beau' = (French origin) 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙҜ/stylish аҙҶаҙҜ аҙҜаөҒаҙөаҙҫаҙөаөҚ, аҙ•аҙҫаҙ®аөҒаҙ•аө», admirer' вҖ” an elegant, well-dressed man or a woman's boyfriend/admirer (masculine).
вҖў 'Belle' = 'аҙёаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙҰаҙ°аҙҝ, аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙҜ аҙҜаөҒаҙөаҙӨаҙҝ, the most admired woman' вҖ” the feminine equivalent of beau вң“
вҖў Both beau and belle = French words. аҙ’аҙ°аөҮ root: French 'beau' = beautiful (masculine), 'belle' = beautiful (feminine).
вҖў Famous usage: 'Belle of the ball' = аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҶ аҙҸаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙөаөҒаҙӮ attractive аҙҶаҙҜ аҙёаөҚаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҖ.
вҖў Meaning: 'Beau (elegant man/admirer) вҶ” Belle (elegant woman/beauty) = masculine-feminine pair.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: BEAU (masculine, French) вҶ” BELLE (feminine, French). Same root, gender pair!
вҖў Masculine вҶ” Feminine word pairs (PSC must-know):
  - Beau вҶ’ Belle (аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝаҙҜ man вҶ” woman)
  - Buck вҶ’ Doe (male deer вҶ” female deer)
  - Hart вҶ’ Hind (male adult deer вҶ” female adult deer)
  - Drake вҶ’ Duck (male duck вҶ” female duck)
  - Gander вҶ’ Goose (male вҶ” female)
  - Ram вҶ’ Ewe (male sheep вҶ” female sheep)
  - Boar вҶ’ Sow (male pig вҶ” female pig)
  - Stallion вҶ’ Mare (male horse вҶ” female horse)
  - Lad вҶ’ Lass (young boy вҶ” young girl)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Gender/Feminine forms = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ question guaranteed!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The beau brought flowers to the belle. (elegant man вҶ’ elegant woman)
  2. She was the belle of the party. (аҙҶаҙҳаөӢаҙ·аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙІаөҶ аҙёаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙҰаҙ°аҙҝ)
  3. The belle smiled at her beau. (аҙ•аҙҫаҙ®аөҒаҙ•аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ®аөҒаҙ•аҙЁаөҒаҙӮ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Buck
вҖў 'Buck' = 'аҙҶаөә аҙ®аҙҫаө», аҙҶаөә аҙ®аөҒаҙҜаөҪ' (male deer/rabbit) вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ masculine form аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! Beau-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ feminine form аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Buck' вҶ” 'Doe' = deer pair. 'Beau' вҶ” 'Belle' = elegant person pair. аҙҮаҙө аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙёаөҚаҙӨ pairs.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Buck' = short, simple, masculine sound вҶ’ opposite of 'beau' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ buck = male animal, beau-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ feminine аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (B) Hart
вҖў 'Hart' = 'аҙ®аөҒаҙӨаҙҝаөјаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ аҙҶаөә аҙ®аҙҫаө» (5 аҙөаөјаҙ·аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ)' (adult male deer) вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҒаҙӮ masculine аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ! 'Hart' вҶ” 'Hind' = adult deer pair.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Hart' = 'heart' sound вҶ’ romantic feel вҶ’ beau (romantic admirer) pair feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ hart = male deer, romance-аҙүаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (D) Lass
вҖў 'Lass' = 'аҙҮаҙіаҙӮ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙҫаҙҜаҙ®аөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі аҙӘаөҶаөәаҙ•аөҒаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝ, young girl' вҖ” feminine аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'lad'-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ feminine pair аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, 'beau'-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Lad' вҶ” 'Lass' = young boy вҶ” young girl. 'Beau' вҶ” 'Belle' = elegant person pair. Pairs confuse аҙҶаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Lass' = feminine word, 'beau' = masculine word вҶ’ opposite gender = match feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ pair match аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ: beau-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ pair = belle, lad-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ pair = lass. Pair confuse аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҚ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ аҙҲ trap.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'BEAU and BELLE are from BEAUTY! Beau = beautiful man. Belle = beautiful woman. Beauty and the Beast = Belle! Beauty вҶ’ Beau/Belle!' вҖ” Disney film 'Beauty and the Beast'-аҙІаөҶ Belle = beau-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ feminine! Both = beautiful in French.
вҖў Gender pairs cheat sheet:
  - Beau вҶ” Belle (elegant/beautiful person) рҹ’ғ
  - Lad вҶ” Lass (young boy/girl) рҹ‘Ұрҹ‘§
  - Buck вҶ” Doe (deer) рҹҰҢ
  - Hart вҶ” Hind (adult deer) рҹҰҢ
  - Ram вҶ” Ewe (sheep) рҹҗ‘
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Beau (elegant man)' feminine вҶ’ 'Belle (elegant woman)'. Eliminate 'Buck' (male deer), 'Hart' (male deer), 'Lass' (lad's pair, not beau's). Only 'Belle' = beau-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ feminine. (C) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using correct tense form of the verb given in bracket.
I ________ (stay) here till Sunday.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) will stay

вҖў 'I will stay here till Sunday' = аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙһаҙҫаҙҜаҙұаҙҫаҙҙаөҚаҙҡ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙЁаҙҝаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҠаҙ°аөҒ аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝаҙ•аҙҫаҙІ plan/decision аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў 'Till Sunday' = 'аҙһаҙҫаҙҜаҙұаҙҫаҙҙаөҚаҙҡ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ future time limit indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. Till/until + specific day = Simple Future 'will + V1' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаөҚ.
вҖў Simple Future (will + V1) = speaker-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ definite decision/plan express аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. 'I will stay' = аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙЁаҙҝаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ (аҙүаҙұаҙҡаөҚаҙҡ аҙӨаөҖаҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙӮ).
вҖў 'Stay' = V1 (base form). Future-аөҪ 'will' + V1 = 'will stay' вң“. Till Sunday = аҙһаҙҫаҙҜаөј аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙҶ action аҙӨаөҒаҙҹаҙ°аөҒаҙӮ вҖ” simple future-аөҪ аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙ•аҙҹаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙһаҙҫаҙҜаҙұаҙҫаҙҙаөҚаҙҡ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙӨаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙӮ' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҠаҙ°аөҒ personal decision аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: TILL/UNTIL + specific day/time = SIMPLE FUTURE (will + V1)!
вҖў Simple Future = WILL + V1 (base form of verb)
  - Positive: I will stay, She will come
  - Negative: I will not (won't) stay
  - Question: Will you stay?
вҖў Future tense signal words (аҙҮаҙө аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ future tense!):
  - tomorrow = аҙЁаҙҫаҙіаөҶ
  - next week/month/year = аҙ…аҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ аҙҶаҙҙаөҚаҙҡ/аҙ®аҙҫаҙёаҙӮ/аҙөаөјаҙ·аҙӮ
  - soon = аҙүаҙҹаө»
  - till Sunday/till tomorrow = аҙһаҙҫаҙҜаөј/аҙЁаҙҫаҙіаөҶ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ
  - in the future = аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаөҪ
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Till + day' + stay/wait/remain = will + V1 pattern PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. I will wait here till Monday. (аҙһаө» аҙӨаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аөҫ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ аҙ•аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ)
  2. She will stay with us till Friday. (аҙ…аҙөаөҫ аҙөаөҶаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙҝ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙһаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҠаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙӮ аҙЁаҙҝаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ)
  3. They will remain silent till the meeting ends. (аҙ®аөҖаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҝаҙӮаҙ—аөҚ аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙ…аҙөаөј аҙ®аө—аҙЁаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) will have stayed
вҖў 'Will have stayed' = Future Perfect tense. 'By Sunday, I will have stayed here for a week' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҒаҙӘаөӢаҙІаөҶ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ specific time-аҙЁаөҚ BEFORE complete аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action-аҙЁаөҮ Future Perfect аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ.
вҖў аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ sentence = 'till Sunday' = Sunday аҙөаҙ°аөҶ аҙӨаөҒаҙҹаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action. Future Perfect вү  till-аҙүаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҝ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі simple plan. Simple Future 'will stay' вң“.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Will have' = future-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӨаҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ, correct аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ Future Perfect = 'by Sunday' (before Sunday complete) аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөӢаҙҙаөҮ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. 'Till Sunday' = by-аҙүаҙҹаө» аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.

вқҢ (C) will be staying
вҖў 'Will be staying' = Future Continuous. 'At this time on Sunday, I will be staying here' вҖ” specific future moment-аөҪ in progress аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action-аҙЁаөҮ Future Continuous аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ.
вҖў 'Will stay' (simple future) = general plan. 'Will be staying' = ongoing at a specific future time. 'Till Sunday' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ simple future = more natural вң“.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Will be staying' = future-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі staying feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ context = simple plan, not 'in progress at a moment'. Simple 'will stay' вң“.

вқҢ (D) stay
вҖў 'Stay' = Present tense (Simple Present or base form). 'I stay here till Sunday' = grammatically incomplete future meaning аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'stay' = verb given in bracket вҶ’ directly use? No! 'Fill in blank using correct tense form' = future tense form use аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙЈаҙӮ. Base form 'stay' alone = future express аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'TILL = WILL! Till Sunday аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ вҶ’ will stay! Till-аөҪ WILL аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ!' вҖ” 'Till' аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ simple future 'will + V1' choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ.
вҖў Future tense quick selector:
  - Simple plan/decision + till/tomorrow вҶ’ will + V1 вң“
  - Action in progress at a future time вҶ’ will be + V-ing
  - Action complete before a future time + by вҶ’ will have + V3
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Till Sunday' + stay/wait/remain аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі question вҶ’ Simple Future 'will + V1'. Future Perfect 'will have stayed' = 'by Sunday' context. Future Continuous = 'at noon tomorrow' context. аҙҲ three-way distinguish аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҚ choose аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙ• вҖ” (A) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the right option that suggests change in voice of the sentence.
Please draw the figure.
optionA: You are instructed to draw the figure.
optionB: You are requested to draw the figure.
optionC: You are ordered to draw the figure.
optionD: You are implored to draw the figure.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) You are requested to draw the figure.

вҖў 'Please draw the figure' = polite imperative/request. 'Please' = politeness marker вҖ” аҙҰаҙҜаҙөаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ tone.
вҖў Imperative sentence-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ Passive formula: You are + (appropriate verb) + to + V1.
вҖў 'Please' = request word вҶ’ Passive-аөҪ 'requested' вң“. 'Please draw' = аҙҰаҙҜаҙөаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙөаҙ°аҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ вҶ’ 'You are requested to draw' = аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙіаөӢаҙҹаөҚ аҙөаҙ°аҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» request аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Instructed' = formal direction. 'Ordered' = strong command. 'Implored' = begging. аҙҮаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ 'please' (polite request)-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning match аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Meaning: 'Figure/аҙҡаҙҝаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ аҙөаҙ°аҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ (please)' вҶ’ Passive = 'Figure аҙөаҙ°аҙҜаөҚаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙіаөӢаҙҹаөҚ request аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.'
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: PLEASE (polite) вҶ’ REQUESTED. DO NOT (prohibition) вҶ’ FORBIDDEN. LET (permission) вҶ’ LET BE. Imperative word = passive verb!
вҖў Imperative вҶ’ Passive conversion table:
  - 'Please + V' вҶ’ 'You are requested to + V' вң“
  - 'Do + V' (command) вҶ’ 'You are ordered/commanded to + V'
  - 'Don't + V' вҶ’ 'You are forbidden/advised not to + V'
  - 'Let us + V' вҶ’ 'Let + V + be + V3' (e.g., Let us go вҶ’ Let it be done)
вҖў Key words and their passive equivalents:
  - Please = polite вҶ’ requested
  - Kindly = formal polite вҶ’ requested/advised
  - Must/Do (strong) = ordered, commanded
  - Please, could you = requested, asked
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Imperative passive = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ guaranteed question! 'Please' вҶ’ 'requested' always!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Please open the door. вҶ’ You are requested to open the door.
  2. Please sit down. вҶ’ You are requested to sit down.
  3. Please submit your report. вҶ’ You are requested to submit your report.
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) You are instructed to draw the figure.
вҖў 'Instructed' = formal direction/training аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ imply аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ. 'A teacher instructs students' вҖ” official/professional context. 'Please draw' = casual polite request, instruction вү  request.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Instructed' = telling someone to do something feel вҶ’ аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'instructed' = formal authority аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Please' = friendly request вҶ’ 'requested' вң“.

вқҢ (C) You are ordered to draw the figure.
вҖў 'Ordered' = very strong command вҖ” military/official order. 'Please draw' = gentle polite request. 'Ordered' = аҙҶаҙңаөҚаҙһ = opposite of 'please' tone.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Ordered' = active-аөҪ аҙҶаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ order аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨ feel вҶ’ passive feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'please' word = gentle, 'ordered' = harsh. Tone match аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!

вқҢ (D) You are implored to draw the figure.
вҖў 'Implored' = desperately begged = аҙҶаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаөӢаҙҹаөҶ аҙ…аҙӘаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•. 'Please draw' = simple polite request, desperate begging аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Implored' = asking/begging вҶ’ request feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ implored = over-the-top emotional begging. 'Please' = simple politeness. 'Requested' = perfect match вң“.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'PLEASE = R-E-Q-U-E-S-T-E-D! Both are POLITE! Please draw вҶ’ You are REQUESTED to draw!' вҖ” 'Please' аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі active sentence вҶ’ always 'requested' in passive. аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ аҙ®аҙұаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ!
вҖў Imperative passive cheat sheet:
  - Please вҶ’ requested рҹҷҸ
  - Order/Command вҶ’ ordered/commanded вҡЎ
  - Don't/Never вҶ’ forbidden/advised not to рҹҡ«
  - Let us вҶ’ Let + be done рҹӨқ
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Active sentence-аөҪ 'please' аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ? вҶ’ Passive-аөҪ 'requested' choose. 'Instructed' (formal), 'ordered' (harsh), 'implored' (desperate) = all eliminate. (B) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using suitable preposition.
I ________walk ten miles without getting tired.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) can

вҖў 'I can walk ten miles without getting tired' = аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҖаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ аҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ®аөҲаөҪ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ ABILITY (аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ) express аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Can' = ability modal verb = аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ¶аҙ•аөҚаҙӨаҙҝ/аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ. 'Ten miles without getting tired' = physical ability вң“.
вҖў 'Without getting tired' = аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҖаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ remarkable ability indicate аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ context аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. Ability = 'can' вң“.
вҖў 'Can walk ten miles' = 'аҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ®аөҲаөҪ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ physical capability statement.
вҖў Note: Question-аөҪ 'preposition' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҺаҙҙаөҒаҙӨаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ options = modal verbs (can/must/should/will). аҙҮаҙө auxiliary/modal verbs аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: ABILITY (аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ) вҶ’ CAN. OBLIGATION (аҙ•аҙҹаҙ®) вҶ’ MUST. ADVICE (аҙүаҙӘаҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ) вҶ’ SHOULD. FUTURE PLAN вҶ’ WILL.
вҖў Modal verbs and their meanings:
  - Can = ability (аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ): I can swim. (аҙЁаөҖаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ)
  - Could = past ability: I could run fast when young. (аҙҡаөҶаҙұаөҒаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ“аҙҹаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙһаөҚаҙһаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ)
  - Must = strong obligation: You must study. (аҙӘаҙ аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙ®аөҮ)
  - Should = advice/mild obligation: You should exercise. (аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙҜаҙҫаҙ®аҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ)
  - Will = future intention: I will help you. (аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙёаҙ№аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ)
  - May/Might = possibility/permission: It may rain. (аҙ®аҙҙ аҙӘаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙӨаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Modal verb = ability/obligation/advice distinguish = PSC-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She can speak five languages. (5 аҙӯаҙҫаҙ· аҙёаҙӮаҙёаҙҫаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҖ” ability)
  2. He can lift 100 kg. (100 аҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөӢ аҙүаҙҜаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҖ” physical ability)
  3. I can solve this problem easily. (аҙҲ problem solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӮ вҖ” mental ability)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) must
вҖў 'Must' = strong obligation/compulsion. 'I must walk ten miles' = 'аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ®аөҲаөҪ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ (аҙЁаҙҝаөјаҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙӮ)' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ ability аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, obligation аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў Sentence-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ meaning = 'аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҖаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ walk аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ' = ability = can вң“. 'Must' = obligation вү  ability.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Must walk ten miles' = strong/impressive feel вҶ’ correct feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ must = obligation (duty), not ability (physical skill).

вқҢ (B) should
вҖў 'Should' = advice/recommendation. 'I should walk ten miles' = 'аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ®аөҲаөҪ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ (аҙүаҙӘаҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ)' вҖ” ability express аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Without getting tired' = ability context. 'Should' = suggestion/advice вү  physical capability.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Should' = health advice context вҶ’ walk ten miles = health advice feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'without getting tired' = impressive physical ability context вҶ’ 'can' вң“.

вқҢ (D) will
вҖў 'Will' = future intention/promise. 'I will walk ten miles' = 'аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙӘаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ®аөҲаөҪ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ (аҙӯаҙҫаҙөаҙҝ intention)' вҖ” present ability express аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Will walk' = future action feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'without getting tired' = right now, always = present ability = 'can' вң“. Future intention вү  present physical ability.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'CAN = CAPABLE! C in CAN = C in CAPABLE! If you're CAPABLE of doing something = I CAN do it!' вҖ” Physical ability (capable of doing) вҶ’ 'can'. аҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҖаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ 10 miles = capable = CAN!
вҖў Modal quick selector:
  - Ability (аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаөҚ, capability) вҶ’ can вң“
  - Obligation (duty, must do) вҶ’ must
  - Advice (should do, recommended) вҶ’ should
  - Future plan/intention вҶ’ will
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Without getting tired' = physical ability context вҶ’ 'can'. 'Must' (obligation), 'should' (advice), 'will' (future) = all wrong context. (C) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the following sentence using appropriate question tag.
It is not raining, _______

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) is it?

вҖў Main sentence: 'It is not raining' = NEGATIVE sentence. Auxiliary verb = 'is'. Subject = 'it'.
вҖў Question Tag rule: Negative main clause вҶ’ Positive tag. Positive main clause вҶ’ Negative tag.
вҖў 'It is NOT raining' = negative вҶ’ tag = POSITIVE. Positive tag = auxiliary without 'not'.
вҖў Auxiliary = 'is' вҶ’ Positive tag = 'is it?' вң“ (No 'not' in positive tag)
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ®аҙҙ аҙӘаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ?' вҖ” аҙҲ question tag аҙүаҙұаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҚ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ (seeking confirmation).
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: NEGATIVE main clause вҶ’ POSITIVE tag (no 'not'). POSITIVE main clause вҶ’ NEGATIVE tag (with 'not'/'n't').
вҖў Step-by-step process:
  Step 1: Identify auxiliary verb in main clause вҖ” 'is'
  Step 2: Check if main clause is positive or negative вҖ” 'is NOT' = NEGATIVE
  Step 3: Negative вҶ’ Positive tag = same auxiliary WITHOUT 'not' = 'is'
  Step 4: Subject = 'it' вҶ’ tag = 'is it?'
вҖў Common sentence вҶ’ tag patterns:
  - It is... вҶ’ isn't it? (positive вҶ’ negative)
  - It is not... вҶ’ is it? вң“ (negative вҶ’ positive)
  - She has... вҶ’ hasn't she? (positive вҶ’ negative)
  - They don't... вҶ’ do they? (negative вҶ’ positive)
  - He can't... вҶ’ can he? (negative вҶ’ positive)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Question tags = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ question guaranteed!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. It is raining, isn't it? (positive вҶ’ negative tag)
  2. It is not snowing, is it? (negative вҶ’ positive tag вң“)
  3. She is not coming, is she? (negative вҶ’ positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) isn't it?
вҖў 'Isn't it?' = NEGATIVE tag. Main clause 'It is not raining' = already negative вҶ’ tag should be POSITIVE ('is it?'). Negative вҶ’ Negative tag = WRONG rule violation!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'It is not raining... isn't it?' = two negatives together feel natural to some students. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ rule = Negative sentence вҶ’ POSITIVE tag. Negative + Negative tag = always wrong!

вқҢ (C) will it?
вҖў 'Will it?' = future auxiliary tag. Main sentence auxiliary = 'is' (present tense). Tag auxiliary must MATCH main clause auxiliary.
вҖў 'Is not raining' = present continuous вҶ’ tag = 'is it?' not 'will it?'. 'Will' = future tense вҶ’ completely wrong tense.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Raining' = weather вҶ’ weather forecast feeling вҶ’ 'will it?' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ tag = main clause auxiliary match аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ. 'Is' not raining вҶ’ 'is it?' вң“.

вқҢ (D) wont it?
вҖў 'Wont it?' = 'won't it?' (will not) вҖ” misspelled + wrong auxiliary. Future negative tag вү  present continuous sentence.
вҖў 'Is not' auxiliary = 'is' вҶ’ tag = 'is it?'. 'Won't' = will not = future tense = completely wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Wont' looks like 'won't' (will not) вҶ’ negative tag feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ (1) spelling wrong вҖ” 'won't' not 'wont', (2) auxiliary mismatch вҖ” 'is' not 'will'. Double error!
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'NOT in sentence = NOT in tag is OUT! Negative sentence вҶ’ Positive tag = NO NOT in tag!' вҖ” Main sentence-аөҪ NOT аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ? вҶ’ Tag-аөҪ NOT аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ = positive tag. Main-аөҪ NOT аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаөҮ? вҶ’ Tag-аөҪ NOT аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ = negative tag.
вҖў Two-step formula:
  1. Main negative? вҶ’ Tag positive (no 'not'): is it? can he? do they?
  2. Main positive? вҶ’ Tag negative (with 'not'): isn't it? can't he? don't they?
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'It is NOT raining' вҶ’ tag = POSITIVE. Look for option WITHOUT 'not'/'n't' that uses same auxiliary 'is' вҶ’ 'is it?' = (A). 'Isn't it?' = negative (wrong), 'will it?' = wrong tense, 'wont it?' = wrong. (A) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Combine the two sentences using suitable correlative conjunction.
It is not useful. It is not ornamental.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) neither...nor

вҖў аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ sentences-аҙүаҙӮ NEGATIVE аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ: 'not useful', 'not ornamental'. аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ 'not' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ negative ideas combine аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙЈаҙӮ вҶ’ 'neither...nor' вң“. Neither = аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Nor = аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Combined: 'It is neither useful nor ornamental' = 'аҙ…аҙӨаөҚ аҙүаҙӘаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙҰаҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙ…аҙІаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙ°аҙөаөҒаҙӮ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ' вң“.
вҖў 'Neither...nor' = BOTH negatives. Neither A nor B = A-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, B-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ…аҙӨаөҚ аҙүаҙӘаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙ®аөӢ аҙ…аҙІаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙ®аөӢ аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ' вҖ” negative correlative pair вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: NEITHER...NOR = BOTH NEGATIVE! EITHER...OR = CHOICE. BOTH...AND = BOTH POSITIVE. NOT ONLY...BUT ALSO = ADDITION.
вҖў Correlative conjunction pairs (PSC must-know):
  - Both...and = A-аҙүаҙӮ B-аҙүаҙӮ (both positive): Both useful AND ornamental
  - Either...or = A-аҙ“ B-аҙ“ (choice): Either useful OR ornamental (one of them)
  - Neither...nor = A-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, B-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ (both negative): Neither useful NOR ornamental вң“
  - Not only...but also = A аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙ®аҙІаөҚаҙІ, B-аҙүаҙӮ (addition): Not only useful BUT ALSO ornamental
вҖў Usage rules:
  - Two positives to combine вҶ’ both...and
  - Two choices (one or the other) вҶ’ either...or
  - Two negatives to combine вҶ’ neither...nor вң“
  - Extra emphasis (more than expected) вҶ’ not only...but also
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Correlative conjunctions = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ class question! Neither/nor = most tested!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. It is neither hot nor cold. (аҙҡаөӮаҙҹаөӢ аҙӨаҙЈаөҒаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
  2. She is neither a doctor nor a teacher. (аҙЎаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙҹаҙұаөӢ аҙҹаөҖаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙұаөӢ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
  3. He is neither rich nor famous. (аҙ§аҙЁаҙөаҙҫаҙЁаөӢ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙёаҙҝаҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙЁаөӢ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) not only...but also
вҖў 'Not only...but also' = addition/extra emphasis. 'It is not only useful but also ornamental' = useful аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ ornamental-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ (POSITIVE addition).
вҖў аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ both sentences NEGATIVE аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” 'not useful, not ornamental'. Positive addition context аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'not only' = negative word аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ negative feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'not only...but also' = POSITIVE addition (A + B, both present). Negative context-аөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!

вқҢ (B) both...and
вҖў 'Both...and' = two positive things. 'It is both useful and ornamental' = useful-аҙүаҙӮ ornamental-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ (POSITIVE).
вҖў аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'not useful, not ornamental' = NEGATIVE. Positive correlative 'both...and' = wrong context.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Both' = two things combine feel вҶ’ useful + ornamental combine аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'both...and' = positive combination. Negative context вҶ’ neither...nor вң“.

вқҢ (D) either...or
вҖў 'Either...or' = choice between two options. 'It is either useful or ornamental' = useful аҙ“ ornamental аҙ“ аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ (CHOICE).
вҖў аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ meaning = NEITHER is true. Choice аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ, both negative аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Either...or, Neither...nor' = confusing pair! 'Either' = one of two вң“ (choice). 'Neither' = none of two (both negative). аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ neither вң“.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'NEITHER = NE + ITHER = NOT EITHER = NONE! Neither useful NOR ornamental = NONE of them! N in Neither = N in None!' вҖ” Neither = аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Nor = аҙүаҙӮ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. Both negative = neither...nor!
вҖў Correlative pair cheat sheet:
  - вһ•вһ• Both positive вҶ’ both...and
  - вһ•/вһ• Choice вҶ’ either...or  
  - вһ–вһ– Both negative вҶ’ neither...nor вң“
  - вһ•вһ• Extra addition вҶ’ not only...but also
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Two negative sentences combine аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ• вҶ’ 'neither...nor'. 'Not useful + Not ornamental' = both negative вҶ’ (C) neither...nor. 'both...and' (positive), 'either...or' (choice), 'not only...but also' (positive addition) = eliminate. (C) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the synonym of the word italicised in the sentence from the given options.
He was __killed__ in the battlefield.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) Slain

вҖў 'Killed' = 'аҙ•аөҠаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ, аҙ®аҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ аҙёаҙӮаҙӯаҙөаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ' вҖ” to cause someone's death. Battlefield = аҙҜаөҒаҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙіаҙӮ.
вҖў 'Slain' = 'slay' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ verb-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ past participle = 'аҙ•аөҠаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ'. Slay вҶ’ Slew (past) вҶ’ Slain (past participle). Killed = Slain = exact synonyms вң“.
вҖў 'He was killed in the battlefield' = passive sentence. 'Killed' вҶ’ 'Slain' вҶ’ 'He was slain in the battlefield' = same meaning, more literary/formal.
вҖў Etymology: 'Slay' = Old English 'slД“an' = to strike/kill. Battlefield context-аөҪ 'slain' = warrior/soldier kill аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ вҖ” noble/heroic context-аөҪ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ formal word.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙҜаөҒаҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙіаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ•аөҠаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ' вҖ” killed = slain = same meaning вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: SLAY вҶ’ SLEW вҶ’ SLAIN (irregular verb). SLAIN = KILLED. Battlefield + Slain = PSC favourite combination!
вҖў Word family of 'slay':
  - Slay (V1/present) = to kill
  - Slew (V2/past) = killed
  - Slain (V3/past participle) = killed (used in passive)
  - Slayer (noun) = one who kills, a killer
вҖў Words related to 'kill' вҖ” PSC vocabulary list:
  - Kill = аҙёаҙҫаҙ§аҙҫаҙ°аҙЈ word (general killing)
  - Slay/Slain = literary/formal (warriors, battles)
  - Murder = premeditated killing (intentional, criminal)
  - Massacre = mass killing (many people)
  - Execute = government-ordered killing
  - Assassinate = to kill a political/public figure
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Synonym of kill/killed = PSC vocabulary section-аөҪ frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The dragon was slain by the knight. (dragon-аҙЁаөҶ knight аҙ•аөҠаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ)
  2. Many soldiers were slain in the war. (аҙӘаҙІ аҙёаөҲаҙЁаҙҝаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӮ аҙҜаөҒаҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ•аөҠаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ)
  3. The hero slew the monster. (hero monster-аҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҠаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) Confiscate
вҖў 'Confiscate' = 'аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•, officially аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаҙіаөҒаҙҹаөҶ property аҙҺаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to officially seize property. аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ kill аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөӢаҙҹаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'The police confiscated his phone' = police аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ phone аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝ. Property-related word. Killed вү  Confiscate.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Confiscate' = formal/official word вҶ’ battlefield context-аөҪ formal feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ confiscate = property seizing, killing вү .

вқҢ (C) Outnumbered
вҖў 'Outnumbered' = 'аҙҺаҙЈаөҚаҙЈаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҪ аҙҶаҙіаөҒаҙ•аөҫ аҙ¶аҙӨаөҚаҙ°аөҒаҙөаҙҝаҙЁаөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ' вҖ” to be surpassed in numbers. 'They were outnumbered 10 to 1' = 10 against 1.
вҖў Outnumbered вү  killed. Outnumbered = numerical disadvantage, not death.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Battlefield context вҶ’ outnumbered = war feel вҶ’ synonym of killed feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ outnumbered = more enemies (numerical), not death itself.

вқҢ (D) Defeated
вҖў 'Defeated' = 'аҙӨаөӢаөҪаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•, аҙӘаҙ°аҙҫаҙңаҙҝаҙӨаҙЁаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ' вҖ” to beat someone in a contest/battle. Defeat вү  Kill. One can be defeated without being killed.
вҖў 'He was defeated in the battle' вү  'He was killed in the battle'. Defeat = lose. Kill = death.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Battlefield + defeated = common combination вҶ’ synonym feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ defeated = losing, not dying. Slain = killed = death.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'SLAIN = SLAIN in battle! Like SLAIN dragon in video games = KILLED dragon! Slay the enemy = Kill the enemy!' вҖ” Fantasy games/movies-аөҪ 'slain' = 'killed'. Battlefield + killed = slain!
вҖў Slay irregular verb: Slay вҶ’ Slew вҶ’ Slain (like fly вҶ’ flew вҶ’ flown!)
вҖў Synonym pairs for PSC:
  - Killed = Slain, Murdered, Slaughtered
  - Defeated = Conquered, Overcome, Vanquished
  - Seized/Confiscated = Impounded, Appropriated
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Killed' synonym вҶ’ 'Slain' (slay past participle). 'Confiscate' (property), 'Outnumbered' (numbers), 'Defeated' (lost) = all different meanings. (B) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using appropriate gerund form of the word given in bracket.
do you mind ________ (share) some pictures of your trip on Facebook ?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) sharing

вҖў 'Do you mind ________ (share)' вҖ” 'mind' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ verb аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙҮаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҒ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ GERUND (V+ing) аҙөаөҮаҙЈаҙӮ, infinitive (to + V) аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Mind + V-ing' = fixed grammar rule. 'Do you mind sharing?' = 'аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?' вң“.
вҖў 'Share' + '-ing' = 'sharing' (gerund form). Gerund = verb-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ noun-like form.
вҖў 'Do you mind sharing?' = informal/polite request. 'аҙ«аөҮаҙёаөҚаҙ¬аөҒаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ аҙҡаҙҝаҙІ аҙ«аөӢаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөӢаҙ•аөҫ share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?'
вҖў Meaning: 'mind = аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' + 'sharing = share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ' вҶ’ 'share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?' вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: MIND + GERUND (V-ing). NEVER 'mind + to + V'. Mind аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ вҶ’ V-ing choice аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!
вҖў Verbs that MUST be followed by Gerund (V-ing) вҖ” PSC must-know list:
  - Mind = аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ mind sharing вң“
  - Enjoy = аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ enjoy reading
  - Avoid = аҙ’аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ avoid making mistakes
  - Finish = аҙӨаөҖаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ finish eating
  - Consider = аҙӘаҙ°аҙҝаҙ—аҙЈаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ consider joining
  - Suggest = аҙЁаҙҝаөјаҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ¶аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ suggest going
  - Admit = аҙёаҙ®аөҚаҙ®аҙӨаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ admit lying
  - Deny = аҙЁаҙҝаҙ·аөҮаҙ§аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ deny stealing
  - Postpone = аҙЁаөҖаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаҙҝаҙөаөҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ postpone leaving
  - Keep = аҙӨаөҒаҙҹаҙ°аөҒаҙ• вҶ’ keep trying
вҖў Gerund form: V + ing (share вҶ’ sharing, run вҶ’ running, come вҶ’ coming)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Gerund after 'mind' = PSC-аөҪ classic question!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. Do you mind waiting for me? (аҙ•аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?)
  2. I don't mind sharing my notes. (notes share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
  3. Would you mind closing the window? (аҙңаҙЁаөҪ аҙ…аҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) to share
вҖў 'To share' = infinitive. 'Mind' + infinitive = WRONG grammar rule. 'Do you mind to share?' = grammatically incorrect in standard English.
вҖў Rule: Mind = gerund-taking verb. 'Mind to + V' = wrong structure. вң“ = 'Mind + V-ing'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'to share' = sounds polite/formal вҶ’ 'Do you mind to share?' = polite feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'mind' + 'to' = grammar rule violation. 'Mind' always takes gerund (V-ing), never infinitive.

вқҢ (C) being shared
вҖў 'Being shared' = passive gerund. 'Do you mind being shared?' = 'аҙЁаҙҝаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ share аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮаҙӘаҙ®аөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ?' вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ wrong meaning. Subject 'you' = sharer, not the one being shared.
вҖў Passive gerund = object аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action. аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ 'you' = active sharer, not passive recipient. 'Sharing' (active gerund) вң“.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Being shared' = gerund form вң“ вҶ’ mind-аҙЁаөҚ аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ passive meaning = wrong context here.

вқҢ (D) to sharing
вҖў 'To sharing' = grammatically impossible combination. 'To' (preposition/infinitive marker) + 'sharing' (gerund) = аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'To + V-ing' = wrong. Either 'to + V1' (infinitive) or just 'V-ing' (gerund). Both аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ = error.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'to sharing' = 'to' + gerund вҶ’ hybrid attempt. Students confuse 'to + V1' (infinitive) with 'V-ing' (gerund). 'To sharing' = both at once = impossible grammar!
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'MIND the ING! Mind always takes -ING! Do you MIND SHARING? MINDing = ING! If you MIND something, you MIND the -ING form!' вҖ” 'Mind' аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ? вҶ’ -ING form (sharing/waiting/going) вң“.
вҖў Gerund-taking verbs memory trick вҖ” MEAD FAKE:
  M-Mind, E-Enjoy, A-Avoid, D-Deny / F-Finish, A-Admit, K-Keep, E-consider
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Mind + ?' вҶ’ V-ing. 'To share' (infinitive = wrong), 'being shared' (passive = wrong meaning), 'to sharing' (impossible) = all eliminate. Only 'sharing' = gerund = вң“. (B) = Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Substitute the words underlined with a suitable phrasal verb.
The director __summoned__ me and asked for an explanation.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) send for

вҖў 'Summoned' = 'аҙ”аҙҰаөҚаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒаҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝ, authority аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ аҙҶаҙ°аөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаҙ°аҙЈаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to officially call someone to come.
вҖў 'Send for' = 'аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаҙіаөҶ/аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•, official/authoritative аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•' вҖ” to send a message asking someone to come = summon вң“.
вҖў 'The director summoned me' = 'The director sent for me' = Director-аҙүаҙҹаөҶ аҙ…аҙҹаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҚ аҙ”аҙҰаөҚаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаө» аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ вң“.
вҖў 'Send for' = authority аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі аҙҶаөҫ (director, principal, officer) аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаҙіаөҶ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ вҖ” summon-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ exact phrasal verb equivalent.
вҖў Meaning: 'Director-аҙүаҙҹаөҶ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜ аҙӘаөҚаҙ°аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙӮ аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙ№аҙҫаҙңаҙ°аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ, аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ explanation аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒ' вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: SEND FOR = SUMMON = officially call someone to come. SEND FOR = аҙ”аҙҰаөҚаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒаҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаөҒаҙ•!
вҖў 'Send' phrasal verb family (PSC must-know):
  - Send for = summon, call to come (аҙ”аҙҰаөҚаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•) вң“
  - Send down = to expel from university / to punish (university-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӘаөҒаҙұаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•)
  - Send in = to submit, to send to an authority (submission аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•: send in your application)
  - Send on = to forward something to another person/place (forward аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•: send on a letter)
  - Send off = to post/mail something / to send away (mail аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•, yell off)
  - Send out = to distribute widely (widely distribute аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•: send out invitations)
  - Send back = to return something (return аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Send for' = summon = PSC phrasal verb favourite!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. The principal sent for the student. (principal student-аҙЁаөҶ summon аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ)
  2. The manager sent for a report. (manager report аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝ)
  3. Please send for a doctor immediately. (аҙүаҙҹаҙЁаөҶ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ doctor-аҙЁаөҶ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) send down
вҖў 'Send down' = British English-аөҪ 'University-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ expel/suspend аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•' / 'prison-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙ…аҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•'. Director-аҙүаҙҹаөҶ office-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙөаҙҝаҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ  аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ¬аҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙөаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'He was sent down from Oxford' = University-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ expel аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаөҒ. Summon вү  send down.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Send down' = director has power вҶ’ 'send someone down' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ send down = expulsion/punishment. Summon = officially call to come = send for вң“.

вқҢ (C) send in
вҖў 'Send in' = 'official аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ submit аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” 'Send in your application by Friday' = application submit аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ. Report/document-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі submission.
вҖў 'The director sent in me' = wrong structure. 'Send in' = documents/reports/troops send аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ. аҙҶаҙіаөҒаҙ•аҙіаөҶ summon аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ 'send for' вң“.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Send in' = director context вҶ’ send someone in feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'send in' = submissions (reports, applications, troops). Person summon аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҪ = 'send for' вң“.

вқҢ (D) send on
вҖў 'Send on' = 'forward аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•' вҖ” 'Please send on this letter to him' = letter forward аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ. Messages/letters/packages forward аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙІаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ 'send on'.
вҖў Summon = personally call to come вү  send on (forward something).
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Send on' = continuing action feel вҶ’ summoned me to continue feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ send on = forwarding things/messages, not summoning people.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'SEND FOR = SEND a message FOR someone to come! Director SENT a message FOR me to come = sent for me = summoned me!' вҖ” 'Send for' = somebody-аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ 'come!' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ message аҙ…аҙҜаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•.
вҖў 'Send' phrasal verb cheat sheet:
  - Send FOR = summon (call to come) рҹ“һ
  - Send DOWN = expel from university вқҢ
  - Send IN = submit (documents) рҹ“„
  - Send ON = forward (letters/messages) вңүпёҸ
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Summoned' = officially called to come = 'send for'. 'Send down' (expel), 'send in' (submit), 'send on' (forward) = different meanings. (B) send for = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using suitable preposition.
He felt no qualms ________ borrowing money from his friends.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) about

вҖў 'Qualms' = 'аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•, аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝ, аҙүаөҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙіаҙҝаөј' вҖ” feelings of doubt/unease about doing something. 'No qualms' = аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаөӢ аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝаҙҜаөӢ аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Qualms about' = fixed collocation. 'Qualms about doing something' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•.
вҖў 'He felt no qualms about borrowing' = 'аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӘаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аҙҹаҙӮ аҙөаҙҫаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаөҒаҙӮ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ' вң“.
вҖў 'About + V-ing' = 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ'. 'Qualms about borrowing' = borrowing-аҙЁаөҶ аҙ•аөҒаҙұаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•.
вҖў Meaning: 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙ•аөӮаҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӘаҙЈаҙӮ аҙ•аҙҹаҙӮ аҙөаҙҫаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙ’аҙҹаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝ аҙ•аҙҫаҙЈаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ' вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: QUALMS ABOUT = fixed collocation! 'About' = qualms-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ partner preposition. No qualms about doing = аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!
вҖў 'About' preposition вҖ” common collocations with abstract nouns:
  - Qualms ABOUT = аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ• (doubt/unease)
  - Worried ABOUT = аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•
  - Anxious ABOUT = nervous
  - Happy ABOUT = аҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөӢаҙ·аҙӮ
  - Excited ABOUT = аҙҶаҙөаөҮаҙ¶аҙӮ
  - Curious ABOUT = аҙңаҙҝаҙңаөҚаҙһаҙҫаҙё
  - Concerned ABOUT = аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙ 
  - Hesitant ABOUT = аҙ®аҙҹаҙҝ
вҖў Fixed preposition collocations вҖ” Noun + about:
  - Concern about = аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙ 
  - Doubt about = аҙёаҙӮаҙ¶аҙҜаҙӮ
  - Qualms about вң“ = аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•
  - Knowledge about = аҙ…аҙұаҙҝаҙөаөҚ
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Noun/Adj + preposition collocation = PSC-аөҪ very frequent!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She had no qualms about telling the truth. (аҙёаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙӘаҙұаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
  2. I have no qualms about working late. (late аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
  3. He expressed no qualms about the decision. (аҙӨаөҖаҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҫаҙЁаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) to
вҖў 'Qualms to' = wrong collocation. 'To' аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ direction/destination/infinitive-аҙЁаөҒ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'Qualms to borrowing' = аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ standard English expression-аҙүаҙӮ аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'He felt no qualms to borrowing' = grammatically odd/non-standard. 'About borrowing' = correct collocation вң“.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'To borrowing' = direction feel (going towards borrowing) вҶ’ logical feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'qualms' = 'about' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ partner. 'Qualms to' = non-existent expression.

вқҢ (C) for
вҖў 'Qualms for' = wrong collocation. 'For' = purpose/reason-аҙЁаөҒ (I did it for you). 'No qualms for borrowing' = standard expression аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Qualms = about вң“. Not 'qualms for', not 'qualms to', not 'qualms in'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'For borrowing money' = reason feel вҶ’ 'no qualms for borrowing' = reason feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'qualms' + 'for' = fixed collocation аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'About' = the fixed partner вң“.

вқҢ (D) no preposition needed
вҖў 'He felt no qualms borrowing' = preposition аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҶ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Qualms' + gerund directly connect аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ pattern аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Qualms about borrowing' = standard fixed expression. Preposition 'about' = mandatory here.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'No preposition needed' = simple/direct feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ 'qualms about' = set collocation вҖ” preposition 'about' = essential. аҙ’аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'QUALMS = ABOUT! QUA + LMS = QUAlms = About! Worry ABOUT qualms = QUAlms ABOUT! Whenever you have qualms, you worry ABOUT something!' вҖ” qualms = аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ•. аҙҶаҙ¶аҙҷаөҚаҙ• = about аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ•аҙҫаҙ°аөҚаҙҜаҙӮ. Qualms ABOUT = always!
вҖў Emotion/feeling + preposition cheat sheet:
  - Qualms ABOUT вң“
  - Worried/Anxious ABOUT
  - Happy/Excited ABOUT
  - Afraid/Scared OF
  - Interested IN
  - Good/Bad AT
  - Responsible FOR
вҖў PSC exam strategy: 'Qualms ____' вҶ’ fixed collocation = 'about'. 'To' (direction), 'for' (purpose), 'no preposition' (impossible) = eliminate. (B) about = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Give the correct spelling of the adjective that means careful or taking great care.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (A) Conscientious

вҖў 'Conscientious' = 'аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҫаҙІаөҒаҙөаҙҫаҙҜ, аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝаҙҜаҙҝаөҪ аҙөаҙіаҙ°аөҶ аҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаҙІаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі, аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙөаҙҫаҙҰаҙҝаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ¬аөӢаҙ§аҙ®аөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі' вҖ” careful, thorough, showing great care/attention to duty.
вҖў Correct spelling: C-O-N-S-C-I-E-N-T-I-O-U-S. Break it down: CON + SCI + EN + TI + OUS = Conscientious вң“.
вҖў Etymology: Latin 'conscientia' = 'conscience, awareness'. 'Conscious' + 'tious' = conscientious. 'Conscience' (moral sense) вҶ’ conscientious (guided by conscience).
вҖў Related word: 'Conscience' (аҙҶаҙӨаөҚаҙ®аҙёаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙҝ, moral sense) вҶ’ 'Conscientious' (аҙҶаҙӨаөҚаҙ®аҙёаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аҙҝ-guided, careful in duty).
вҖў Meaning: 'A conscientious worker' = 'аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҫаҙІаөҒаҙөаҙҫаҙҜ аҙӨаөҠаҙҙаҙҝаҙІаҙҫаҙіаҙҝ' вҖ” someone who takes great care in their work вң“.
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҫаҙЁаөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі formula: CON-SCI-EN-TIOUS! 4 syllables. SC together! -ENT not -INT! -IOUS not -IUS or -EOUS!
вҖў Spelling breakdown вҖ” segment by segment:
  - CON = first part (like 'conscious', 'conflict')
  - SCI = 'sc' together (science, conscious, conscience)
  - EN = middle part (-ent, -ence endings)
  - TI = 'ti' sound (like 'nation', 'station')
  - OUS = adjective suffix (like 'famous', 'serious', 'cautious')
  - Full: CON + SCI + EN + TI + OUS = CONSCIENTIOUS вң“
вҖў PSC Spelling вҖ” commonly tested difficult words:
  - Conscientious (аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҫаҙІаөҒаҙөаҙҫаҙҜ) вң“
  - Necessary (аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜ) вҖ” 1 C, 2 S
  - Occurrence (аҙёаҙӮаҙӯаҙөаҙӮ) вҖ” double C, double R
  - Embarrass (аҙІаҙңаөҚаҙңаҙҝаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•) вҖ” double R, double S
  - Accommodation вҖ” double C, double M
  - Millennium вҖ” double L, double N
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: 'Conscientious' spelling = PSC-аөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ classic question!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. She is a conscientious student. (аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҫаҙІаөҒаҙөаҙҫаҙҜ аҙөаҙҝаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаөјаҙҘаҙҝ)
  2. He does his work in a conscientious manner. (аҙ¶аөҚаҙ°аҙҰаөҚаҙ§аҙҜаөӢаҙҹаөҶ аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ)
  3. A conscientious officer never ignores duty. (аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙөаҙҫаҙҰаҙҝаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ®аөҒаҙіаөҚаҙі officer аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ duty ignore аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ)
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (B) Conscintious
вҖў 'Conscintious' = WRONG. Error: '-SCIN-' аҙӘаҙ•аҙ°аҙӮ '-SCIEN-' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝ. 'Consci-EN-tious' = '-ent-' sound, '-int-' аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Science' вҶ’ 'scienti-' вҶ’ 'conscientious'. '-SCIN-' = non-existent spelling segment.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Conscin-' = 'consin-' sound feel вҶ’ Indians-аөҪ 'cousin' вҶ’ 'conscin-' feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ correct = '-SCIEN-' (like science). 'Conscientious' contains 'science' root!

вқҢ (C) Conscientius
вҖў 'Conscientius' = WRONG. Error: ending '-IUS' аҙӘаҙ•аҙ°аҙӮ '-IOUS' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝ. '-IOUS' = adjective suffix (like serious, curious, obvious).
вҖў '-IUS' вү  English adjective suffix. Latin ending аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ '-ius'. English adjective = '-ious'.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: '-ius' = Latin origin вҶ’ Latin word (Latin 'conscientia') вҶ’ '-ius' ending feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ English adjective suffix = '-ious' (not '-ius'). Serious, obvious, curious = all '-ious' вң“.

вқҢ (D) Concientious
вҖў 'Concientious' = WRONG. Error: 'SC' missing вҖ” 'Consc-' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝ, 'Conc-' аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. 'Con-SC-ientious' вҖ” the 'S' drop аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ.
вҖў 'Conscience' вҶ’ 'conscious' вҶ’ 'conscientious' вҖ” all have 'SC' together. 'Conc-' alone = wrong.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'Concientious' = 'con-' prefix + '-cient-' вҶ’ sufficient/efficient sound вҶ’ logical feel. аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ root = 'conscience' (SC аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ). 'SC' drop аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙҫаөҪ spelling wrong!
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'CONSCIENTIOUS has SCIENCE in it! CON + SCIENCE + TIOUS = CONSCIENTIOUS! A conscientious scientist uses SCIENCE carefully!' вҖ” 'Science' word-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ spelling аҙҶаөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ аҙ…аҙұаҙҝаҙҜаҙҫаҙӮ вҶ’ CONSCIENTIOUS-аөҪ SCIENCE аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ!
вҖў Spelling breakdown to memorize:
  CON + SCIEN + TIOUS
  (like CON + SCIENCE + TIOUS вҶ’ remove the E from SCIENCE = CONSCIENTIOUS)
вҖў Check list for writing CONSCIENTIOUS:
  вң“ Has SC (not just C)
  вң“ Has -SCIEN- (not -SCIN-)
  вң“ Ends in -IOUS (not -IUS)
  вң“ Starts CON- (not COM- or CON without SC)
вҖў PSC exam strategy: Remember 'SCIENCE is in CONSCIENTIOUS!' вҶ’ SC вң“, -SCIEN- вң“, -IOUS вң“. Eliminate: (B) -SCIN- (wrong), (C) -IUS (wrong ending), (D) missing SC. (A) = вң“. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which of the following sentences contains a gerund?

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (C) He enjoys playing soccer

вҖў 'Playing' аҙҮаҙөаҙҝаҙҹаөҶ gerund аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” 'enjoys' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ verb-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ object аҙҶаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ noun-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝ аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙ•аҙҜаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ.
вҖў Gerund = verb + ing form, аҙӘаҙ•аөҚаҙ·аөҮ аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ noun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” 'playing soccer' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ 'football аҙ•аҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ act' аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, action аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'He enjoys playing soccer' вҖ” 'аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ football аҙ•аҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ' вҖ” 'playing soccer' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ object аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙҮаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў Sentence-аөҪ verb + ing form аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙ•: аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ thing/activity аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? аҙҶаҙЈаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ gerund вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ miss аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ: verb + ing form-аҙЁаөҚ sentence-аөҪ noun-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ gerund аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ; verb-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙңаөӢаҙІаҙҝ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ present participle аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ!
вҖў Gerund-аҙЁаөҚ sentence-аөҪ 4 roles аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ:
  1. Subject аҙҶаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ: 'Swimming is good.' вҖ” (аҙЁаөҖаҙЁаөҚаҙӨаөҪ аҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ) вҖ” 'swimming' subject аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ
  2. Object аҙҶаҙ•аҙҫаҙӮ: 'He enjoys playing.' вҖ” (аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙ•аҙіаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ аҙҶаҙёаөҚаҙөаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ) вҖ” 'playing' object
  3. Preposition-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ: 'She is good at singing.' вҖ” (аҙӘаҙҫаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙөаҙіаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ) вҖ” 'at' preposition-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: enjoy, avoid, keep, suggest, finish, deny, miss, mind, consider вҖ” аҙҲ verbs-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ gerund ALWAYS аҙөаҙ°аөҒаҙӮ, аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ to + verb аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. 'She avoided making mistakes.' вҖ” (аҙ…аҙөаөҫ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаөҒаҙ•аөҫ аҙ’аҙҙаҙҝаҙөаҙҫаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙҝ) вҖ” 'making' gerund аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ
  2. 'Reading books is a good habit.' вҖ” (аҙӘаөҒаҙёаөҚвҖҢаҙӨаҙ•аҙӮ аҙөаҙҫаҙҜаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ аҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІ аҙ¶аөҖаҙІаҙ®аҙҫаҙЈаөҚ) вҖ” 'Reading' gerund аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ
  3. 'I am good at solving puzzles.' вҖ” (puzzles solve аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаөҪ аҙЁаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙөаҙЁаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ) вҖ” 'solving' gerund аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) She walked to the store
вҖў 'Walked' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ simple past verb аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” (аҙ…аҙөаөҫ store-аҙІаөҮаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ аҙЁаҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ) вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҠаҙ°аөҒ completed action аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў аҙҲ sentence-аөҪ verb + ing form аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” gerund аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аҙҫаө» -ing form аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙ®аҙҫаҙЈаөҚ!
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: -ing form аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІаҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙӨ option-аөҪ gerund аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ -ing form аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ gerund-аҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҚ chance аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙіаөӮ.

вқҢ (B) They watched the movie
вҖў 'Watched' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ simple past verb аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” (аҙ…аҙөаөј movie аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒ) вҖ” -ing form аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў 'Movie' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ regular noun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, gerund аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” gerund аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ verb-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ, regular noun аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Object noun аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҚ confuse аҙҶаҙ•аҙ°аөҒаҙӨаөҚ вҖ” regular noun вү  gerund.

вқҢ (D) We ate pizza for dinner
вҖў 'Ate' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ irregular past tense verb аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҖ” (аҙһаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ pizza аҙ•аҙҙаҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ) вҖ” -ing form аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў аҙҲ sentence-аөҪ -ing form аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” gerund аҙүаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙ•аҙҫаө» аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: Simple action sentences-аөҪ gerund аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” -ing form аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ gerund аҙҶаҙ•аҙҫаө» chance!
explanation.tip:
рҹ’Ў HOW COME Tip

вҖў рҹ§  Memory trick: 'Gerund = Noun-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ аҙөаөҮаҙ·аҙӮ аҙ§аҙ°аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡ Verb!' вҖ” 'playing soccer' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ activity аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ thing аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, presently happening action аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ. аҙҲ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ idea аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ gerund identify аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҪ аҙөаҙіаҙ°аөҶ easy аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙӮ!
вҖў аҙҲ verbs-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ¶аөҮаҙ·аҙӮ gerund ALWAYS: enjoy, avoid, keep, suggest, finish, mind, miss, consider, risk вҖ” аҙҲ list аҙ’аҙ°аөҒаҙ®аҙҝаҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҚ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. Infinitive (to play) аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ, gerund (playing) аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ аҙҮаҙө аҙҶаҙөаҙ¶аөҚаҙҜаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөҶаҙҹаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаөҚ.
вҖў PSC strategy: verb + ing form аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙӮ аҙҡаөӢаҙҰаҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ thing/activity аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ, аҙ…аҙӨаөӢ аҙҮаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ аҙЁаҙҹаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁ action аҙҶаҙЈаөӢ? Subject аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ, object аҙҶаҙ•аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөӢ? аҙҶаҙЈаөҶаҙҷаөҚаҙ•аҙҝаөҪ gerund вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ miss аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!

questionText: That was __________ disappointment, but we managed to get __________ room across __________ hall from it.

explanation.correct:
вң… аҙ¶аҙ°аҙҝаҙҜаөҒаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙӮ: (B) a, the, the

вҖў аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаҙӮ blank ('disappointment'-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ): 'd' аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ consonant аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ mention аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ 'a' вң“
вҖў аҙ°аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙҫаҙӮ blank ('room'-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ): hall-аҙЁаөҚ  opposite side-аөҪ аҙүаҙіаөҚаҙі specific room аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, known аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ 'the' вң“
вҖў аҙ®аөӮаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаҙӮ blank ('hall'-аҙЁаөҚ  аҙ®аөҒаҙ®аөҚаҙӘаөҚ): context-аөҪ аҙҸаҙӨаөҚ hall аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ reader-аҙЁаөҚ  clear аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, specific аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ вҶ’ 'the' вң“
вҖў аҙ“аҙ°аөӢ blank-аҙүаҙӮ independently analyze аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөҮаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ, аҙ’аҙ°аөҮ article аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ rule аҙҮаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” аҙҮаҙӨаөҚ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ!
explanation.rule:
рҹ“Ң аҙөаөҚаҙҜаҙҫаҙ•аҙ°аҙЈ аҙЁаҙҝаҙҜаҙ®аҙӮ

вҖў рҹ”‘ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ: аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ mention вҶ’ a/an | known/specific вҶ’ the | vowel аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ вҶ’ an, consonant аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ вҶ’ a!
вҖў аҙ“аҙ°аөӢ blank-аҙүаҙӮ independently:
  1. 'a disappointment' вҖ” 'd' consonant аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ, sentence-аөҪ аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ mention вҶ’ 'a' (аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ disappointment аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ)
  2. 'the room' вҖ” context-аөҪ specific, known room вҶ’ 'the' (аҙҶ room аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, аҙҸаҙӨаөҶаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ reader-аҙЁаөҚ  clear)
  3. 'the hall' вҖ” specific, known hall вҶ’ 'the' (аҙҶ hall аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, reader-аҙЁаөҚ  clear аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ)
вҖў вӯҗ PSC аҙҶаҙөаөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙЁаҙӮ: Multiple blank article questions PSC-аөҪ frequent вҖ” аҙ“аҙ°аөӢ blank-аҙүаҙӮ separately analyze аҙҡаөҶаҙҜаөҚаҙҜаөӮ!
вҖў аҙүаҙҰаҙҫаҙ№аҙ°аҙЈ sentences:
  1. 'I saw a dog. The dog was barking.' вҖ” (аҙһаҙҫаө» аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙЁаҙҫаҙҜаҙҜаөҶ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒ. аҙҶ аҙЁаҙҫаҙҜ аҙ•аөҒаҙ°аҙҡаөҚаҙҡаөҒ.) вҖ” аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ mention 'a', second time known 'the'
  2. 'She bought a book from the library.' вҖ” (аҙ…аҙөаөҫ аҙҶ library-аөҪ аҙЁаҙҝаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ book аҙөаҙҫаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаҙҝ.) вҖ” book first mention 'a', specific library 'the'
  3. 'He is an honest man.' вҖ” (аҙ…аҙҰаөҚаҙҰаөҮаҙ№аҙӮ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙёаҙӨаөҚаҙҜаҙёаҙЁаөҚаҙ§аҙЁаҙҫаҙҜ аҙ®аҙЁаөҒаҙ·аөҚаҙҜаҙЁаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ.) вҖ” 'honest' vowel аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ вҶ’ 'an'
вҖў рҹҢҹ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙ“аөјаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ аҙ•аҙҘ: аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙ’аҙ°аҙҫаөҫ аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜаҙ®аҙҫаҙҜаҙҝ аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҶаҙЁаҙҜаөҶ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒ. 'аҙ’аҙ°аөҒ аҙҶаҙЁ аҙ•аҙҹаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒаҙӘаөӢаҙҜаҙҝ' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӘаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒ вҖ” аҙҶаҙҰаөҚаҙҜ mention, listener-аҙЁаөҚ  unknown вҶ’ a. аҙӘаҙҝаҙұаөҚаҙұаөҮ аҙҰаҙҝаҙөаҙёаҙӮ аҙ…аҙӨаөҮ аҙҶаҙЁаҙҜаөҶ аҙөаөҖаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙ•аҙЈаөҚаҙҹаҙӘаөҚаҙӘаөӢаөҫ 'аҙҶ аҙҶаҙЁ аҙөаөҖаҙЈаөҚаҙҹаөҒаҙӮ аҙөаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҒ' аҙҺаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙӘаҙұаҙһаөҚаҙһаөҒ вҖ” second time, listener-аҙЁаөҚ  known вҶ’ the. аҙҮаҙӨаҙҫаҙЈаөҚ a/an vs the-аҙЁаөҚаҙұаөҶ core logic вҖ” аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөҪ аҙҲ аҙ•аҙҘ аҙ“аөјаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙ°аөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ аҙ’аҙ°аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аҙІаөҒаҙӮ confuse аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ!
explanation.wrong:
вқҢ аҙӨаөҶаҙұаөҚаҙұаҙҫаҙҜ аҙүаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙ°аҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҫ

вқҢ (A) an, the, a вҖ” 'an disappointment': 'd' consonant аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, vowel аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӮ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” 'an' vowel аҙ¶аҙ¬аөҚаҙҰаҙӨаөҚаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаөҚ  аҙ®аҙҫаҙӨаөҚаҙ°аҙӮ аҙүаҙӘаҙҜаөӢаҙ—аҙҝаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ. 'a hall' вҖ” аҙҶ hall specific/known аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, 'the' аҙҶаҙ•аҙЈаҙӮ, 'a' wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
вҖў рҹӘӨ PSC Trap: 'disappointment' 'd'-аөҪ аҙӨаөҒаҙҹаҙҷаөҚаҙҷаөҒаҙЁаөҚаҙЁаҙӨаҙҝаҙЁаҙҫаөҪ 'an' аҙҶаҙ•аҙҝаҙІаөҚаҙІ вҖ” sound аҙЁаөӢаҙ•аөҚаҙ•аөӮ, letter аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ!

вқҢ (C) the, a, a вҖ” 'the disappointment': first mention аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ, reader-аҙЁаөҚ  already known specific аҙ’аҙЁаөҚаҙЁаөҚ аҙ…аҙІаөҚаҙІ вҶ’ 'the' wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ. 'a hall' вҖ” specific context-аөҪ known hall-аҙЁаөҚ  'a' wrong аҙҶаҙЈаөҚ.
